Title: Coral Mountain Desert Club
Description: A 400-acre private club community bringing an 18-hole David McLay Kidd-designed course and the largest pneumatic surf lagoon in the United States.
DescriptionLong: Coral Mountain Desert Club is a 400-acre private residential and recreation club in La Quinta, California, in the greater Palm Springs area, developed by Meriwether Companies as an experience-driven desert retreat. The club is anchored by an 18-hole championship golf course — the first Coachella Valley desert design from acclaimed architect David McLay Kidd — alongside a 48-chamber pneumatic surf lagoon billed as the largest wave basin of its kind in the United States. A wellness- and sport-focused amenity program rounds out the community with padel, pickleball and tennis courts, a clubhouse, pools, a pump track, a recreational lake and trails connecting to tens of thousands of acres of adjacent public land. Set against the striking backdrop of Coral Mountain, the club fuses championship golf, world-class surf and fitness into a distinctive Southern California desert lifestyle destination.

StatusHistory.note: Broke ground Apr 9 2026; first amenities/golf/wave basin complete 2028 (stored 2026) per Meriwether/PRNewswire

Title: Gallery at Marina Village
Description: Gallery at Marina Village is a market-rate apartment development rising along the Broadway corridor in Riviera Beach's emerging Marina Village waterfront district. Developed by Related Urban Development Group with partners BH Group and Tezral Partners, its twin 20-story towers pair studio-to-three-bedroom residences with ground-floor retail and office space and a multi-level parking structure, plus resident amenities including a fitness center, coworking lounge and pool deck. Set steps from the Riviera Beach marina and Peanut Island, it anchors a broader transformation of the city's downtown waterfront.
DescriptionLong: A market-rate rental complex with two, 20-story residential towers and a nine-level parking structure with 652 parking spaces with retail and office space.

Title: Jacksonville Jaguars Stadium
Description: A $1.4 billion transformation of EverBank Stadium, delivering a near-complete reinvention of the Jaguars’ home into a next-generation venue set to debut for the 2028 NFL season.
DescriptionLong: The Jacksonville Jaguars' "Stadium of the Future" is a comprehensive, more-than-$1.4-billion reinvention of the team's downtown Jacksonville home on the St. Johns River, delivered as a public-private partnership between the City of Jacksonville and the Jaguars. Designed by global sports architect HOK and built by a Hunt Construction–Barton Malow joint venture, the project reconstructs the open-air bowl beneath a signature shade canopy and a dramatic steel-truss roof structure engineered to cool the seating areas in Florida's climate. The phased renovation reshapes concourses, premium clubs and fan amenities while the team continues to play on site, transforming the aging venue into a modern, year-round entertainment destination capable of hosting marquee events. The Stadium of the Future is the centerpiece of a broader effort to anchor sports, tourism and development in downtown Jacksonville.

StatusHistory.note: In-bowl demo underway 2026; completion targeted Aug 2028 → refine delivery

Title: Panther National
Description: Palm Beach County’s first new golf community in nearly two decades with a Nicklaus-Thomas course.
DescriptionLong:
A 400-acre private golf and luxury residential community, designed around an 18-hole championship golf course created by golf legends Jack Nicklaus and Justin Thomas. Located within the Avenir master-planned community and bordering more than 2,400 acres of protected conservation land, Panther National combines contemporary architecture, expansive homesites, and a club-focused lifestyle in one of South Florida’s fastest-growing luxury markets.

The community features approximately 218 custom and signature estate homes, many designed by acclaimed architect Max Strang, with modern architecture that stands apart from traditional South Florida golf communities. Amenities include a private golf club, lifestyle club, racquet facilities, wellness offerings, resort-style pools, and a new clubhouse currently under construction that is expected to become a centerpiece of the development.

Title: Dubai Creek Tower
Description: A needle-like observation tower planned to surpass the Burj Khalifa, rising from Dubai Creek Harbour with a cantilevered observation deck inspired by a lily pad.
DescriptionLong: Dubai Creek Tower is a landmark observation tower planned as the centerpiece of Emaar Properties' Dubai Creek Harbour, an expansive waterfront master-development set around the historic Dubai Creek and the Ras Al Khor wildlife sanctuary. Conceived as a soaring, cable-stayed structure — originally designed by Spanish-Swiss architect Santiago Calatrava and inspired by the slender form of a lily and the minarets of Islamic architecture — the tower is intended to anchor a new district of residences, retail and public realm, crowned by observation decks offering panoramic views across the city. As one of Emaar's signature undertakings, Dubai Creek Tower is envisioned as a defining vertical icon of Dubai's skyline and a global tourism draw.

StatusHistory.note: Recorded "construction" but vertical never started — foundation (145 barrette piles) complete 2018 then stalled; Emaar relaunching with NEW design, construction tender to be issued ~2026, vertical begins once contractor appointed. Proposing correction to breaking-ground

Title: Rolex Tower
Description: Rolex Tower is a five-story luxury watch-and-jewelry flagship planned for 1117 East Las Olas Boulevard in downtown Fort Lauderdale, developed by Weston Jewelers and designed by Arquitectonica. Anchored by Rolex and joined by brands including Cartier, Omega, Breitling, Chopard and Tudor, the canal-front building stacks curated retail across multiple floors beneath a corporate office level and a rooftop cafe and restaurant. With floor-to-ceiling glass and a prominent Las Olas address, it is positioned to become one of the largest multi-brand luxury watch destinations in the United States.
DescriptionLong: Five-story tower with a bridal studio, champagne bar and service center, with exclusive cocktail parties on the top floor.

Title: The Setai, St. Maarten
Description: An ultra-luxury beachfront resort and branded residences in Indigo Bay, marking The Setai's Caribbean debut
DescriptionLong: The Setai St. Maarten is an ultra-luxury beachfront resort and collection of branded residences in Indigo Bay, developed by Altree Developments in partnership with The Setai Hotels (Nakash Family). Set along over 1,000 feet of pristine shoreline with views toward Saba and St. Barths, the property will feature a 205-key hotel plus 280 private residences ranging from approximately 620 to over 2,000 sq ft, with dual-key options up to 6,000 sq ft. Designed by HKS Architects with interiors by Studio Munge, amenities include a beach club with cabanas and butler service, three pools (including an adults-only oasis), a 30,000 sq ft destination spa, tennis and pickleball courts, five restaurant concepts, and a Les Clefs d'Or concierge team. It will be St. Maarten's first Leading Hotels of the World property. Construction is underway with completion expected in 2028.

StatusHistory.note: 205 keys plus branded residences/villas; opening 2028 (unchanged) per Luxury Travel Magazine
The Setai makes its Caribbean debut in St. Maarten, converting the Vie L'Ven development at Indigo Bay; developer Altree Developments (The Real Deal, Hotel Management).

Title: Arbor Coconut Grove
Description: A boutique 45-residence, 5-story building in the heart of Coconut Grove, steps from CocoWalk
DescriptionLong: Arbor Coconut Grove is a boutique 5-story, 45-residence building developed by Muse Groupe in the Village of Coconut Grove, Miami. Designed by Miami-based Behar Font & Partners, the low-density building offers spacious 2-, 3-, and 4-bedroom layouts with flexible den spaces, floor-to-ceiling impact-resistant windows, and nature-inspired modernist design. Located at 3034 Oak Avenue, the building is steps from CocoWalk, parks, marinas, top-rated schools, restaurants, and shops. Amenities include a pool, rooftop, lobby, pet-friendly community spaces, electric car charging stations, and ample resident parking. Move-in ready Q1 2026.

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Coconut Grove (per project name + official site)

Title: DELUX WeHo
Description: A 134-unit boutique wellness-focused apartment community in Wedgewood-Houston with saltwater pool, red light saunas, cold plunge, and Technogym fitness center.
DescriptionLong: A 134-unit boutique apartment community in Nashville's Wedgewood-Houston neighborhood, developed by The Bradley Projects. Following the same wellness-driven approach as the team's earlier project The Lanes, DELUX WeHo prioritizes natural light, livability, and intentional living over scale. Residences range from approximately 500 to 1,500 square feet across studio, one-, two-, and three-bedroom layouts, including loft-style floor plans that separate living and private spaces, with oversized windows and private balconies throughout. Wellness and recovery amenities include a saltwater pool, two red light saunas, a cold plunge, a dedicated recovery studio, and a Technogym fitness center. The property also features 5,000 square feet of street-level retail and sits within two blocks of Soho House Nashville, Michelin-starred Bastion, Hermès Nashville, and Pastis.
Developer: The Bradley Projects, Tinsley Co., Big Plan Holdings

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Wedgewood-Houston (WeHo), Nashville (per official site)
Auto-promoted: delivery date 2026-05 has passed

Title: Skyline OC
Description: Orange County's tallest residential building, a 25-story dual-tower condo conversion bringing resort-style ownership to South Coast Metro
DescriptionLong: Skyline OC is a 25-story, dual-tower condo conversion by Miami-based developer Crescent Heights, restoring ownership to Orange County's tallest residential building at 9 MacArthur Place in Santa Ana. Originally built in 2010 as condominiums and later operated as rentals, the towers have been fully reimagined with sophisticated interiors, hotel-caliber amenities, and an ownership-focused resident culture. Move-in-ready residences range from one- and two-bedroom homes to penthouses up to $3 million, with prices starting around $600,000. Average unit size is approximately 1,500 square feet — significantly larger than newly built condos in comparable markets — and homes feature floor-to-ceiling windows, open layouts, and chef-worthy kitchens with Viking, LG, and Miele appliances. The 60,000 square feet of amenity space includes an 80-foot pool with teak lounges and private cabanas, a fitness center with yoga and Pilates studios, spa treatment rooms, sauna and steam areas, a bocce court, putting green, co-working lounge, and chef's kitchen. Services include 24-hour security and an on-site concierge. Sales are led by Polaris Pacific. Located near John Wayne Airport, UC Irvine, and Newport Beach.

Title: DUOS Wynwood
Description: A new 49-unit, eight-story short-term rental hospitality concept rising in the heart of Miami's Wynwood Arts District
DescriptionLong: A new 49-unit, eight-story short-term rental hospitality concept in the heart of Miami's Wynwood Arts District. DUOS was purpose-designed from the ground up specifically for short-term rentals—allowing buyers to fully own their unit while renting it out for stays ranging from a single night up to 30 days. Architecture by MKDA and interiors by award-winning international hospitality design firm AvroKo create a non-branded, design-driven identity rooted in eclectic modernism. The 49-unit scale offers a curated, boutique alternative to Miami's larger 200-plus-unit developments, with studio to two-bedroom suites ranging from the $500,000s to over $1 million. Construction is expected to break ground in late 2026, with completion anticipated in early 2028.
Developer: Wave Group Development, Vitrium Capital, Zambala Luxury Apartments

StatusHistory.note: 8 stories, 49 units; construction expected to begin 2026, completion 2028 per TRD/Florida YIMBY

Title: Huntington Bank Field
Description: $2.4B HKS-designed enclosed dome stadium for the Cleveland Browns rising on a 178-acre Brook Park site, with a folded-plate transparent roof and the closest seating in the NFL, opening 2029.
DescriptionLong: Huntington Bank Field is the new domed home of the NFL's Cleveland Browns, rising in Brook Park, Ohio, just outside Cleveland. Designed by sports-architecture firm HKS as a year-round enclosed 'Super Theater,' the roughly $2.6 billion stadium seats about 67,500 fans — expandable to 75,000 for concerts and major events — and is engineered to host Final Fours, international soccer, and large-scale entertainment alongside Browns football. Its signature transparent, long-span 'trussless' roof and a seating bowl billed as the closest-to-field in the NFL anchor a broader mixed-use entertainment district developed by Haslam Sports Group with master-developer Lincoln Property Company.

StatusHistory.note: Mass excavation began Mar 2 2026 (80ft deep, ~2M cu yd); "officially starts construction" per NEOtrans. Completion spring 2029

Title: Cabot Revelstoke
Description: Cabot's first four-season mountain golf-and-ski resort — a 155-key alpine lodge, an 18-hole Rod Whitman course, a par-3 short course, and luxury chalet residences set among British Columbia's Monashees and Selkirks.
DescriptionLong: Anchored in the alpine town of Revelstoke — North America's largest vertical drop and self-styled 'heli-skiing capital of the world' — Cabot's first four-season mountain destination weaves a 155-room Mountain Lodge, an 18-hole championship course, a par-3 short course, and an exclusive collection of alpine chalets across a dramatic bench above the Columbia River valley. The Mountain Lodge (early 2027) features a modern steakhouse and bar, full spa and wellness with hot tubs and sauna, a fitness center, a European-inspired event venue for up to 400 guests, curated retail, and combined check-in for golf and heli-skiing. The 18-hole Cabot Revelstoke course (preview play fall 2026) winds along cliffs, creeks, and natural landforms with generous fairways and bold bunkering, certified through Audubon's Platinum Signature Sanctuary program. The Railyard, a par-3 short course paying homage to Revelstoke's railway heritage, anchors a vibrant clubhouse with dining, bowling, virtual golf, and shuffleboard. The Residences — Dolomites/Alps-inspired chalets with soaring ceilings, expansive terraces, and panoramic mountain views — debut with Chalet 1's nine four- and five-bedroom single-level homes (four-bedrooms from $7.6M CAD).

StatusHistory.note: 155-room Mountain Lodge unveiled; resort construction well underway with preview play this fall ahead of 2027 opening

Title: Eastpoint
Description: Parcel C of Eastpoint — gateway phase of Nashville's 30-acre East Bank neighborhood next to the new Nissan Stadium, anchored by a 1.5-acre public plaza with 50,000 sq ft of retail, a 600-room hotel, and residential.
DescriptionLong: Parcel C of Eastpoint is the gateway phase of a 30-acre East Bank neighborhood rising directly across the Cumberland River from downtown Nashville, adjacent to the new Nissan Stadium and the future Tennessee Performing Arts Center. The plan centers on a 1.5-acre public plaza framed by approximately 50,000 square feet of multi-level retail and restaurant space, tree-lined sidewalks, dedicated bike lanes, and below-ground parking that lifts pedestrian life to the street level. Above it, the parcel will deliver a 600-room full-service hotel alongside a residential component with 10% set aside as affordable housing — designed to serve full-time residents while absorbing the crowds drawn to the stadium and TPAC across the year.
ProjectType: Residences, Hotel, Entertainment, Park, Office, Retail
Architect: HKS, Michael Hsu Office of Architecture, Gresham Smith, Morris Adjmi Architects

Title: 520 Fifth Avenue
Description: KPF's 1,002-foot Midtown Manhattan supertall — a slender, stone-and-glass spiraling tower with arched cascading windows topped by a copper-finned crown, holding 100 condominiums above 25 boutique office floors and the members-only Moss Club.
DescriptionLong: On a tight Fifth Avenue parcel between 43rd and 44th Streets, Rabina's 520 Fifth Avenue rises 88 storeys / 1,002 feet (305 m) — the seventh-tallest Manhattan tower at completion. KPF's design wraps the 415,000-sq-ft tower in a spiraling masonry envelope of arched windows that step in cascading setbacks toward a ribbed copper crown. The program stacks 25 boutique-office floors and ground-floor retail beneath 100 condominium residences and amenities by Charles & Co. and AvroKO including the new Moss Club — a private members lounge spanning the two cellar levels and the first three floors above grade. Façade nearing completion late 2025; closings tracking 2026.

Title: The Brooklyn Tower
Description: SHoP Architects' bronze-and-black 1,066-foot Brooklyn supertall at 9 DeKalb — a cinematic interlocking-hexagon massing rising over the landmarked 1908 Dime Savings Bank, holding 398 condos and 150 rentals.
DescriptionLong: Brooklyn's first supertall climbs 1,066 feet / 93 stories above the landmarked Beaux-Arts Dime Savings Bank of Brooklyn (1908) at the convergence of DeKalb, Flatbush and Fleet, where Downtown Brooklyn meets Fort Greene. SHoP Architects designed an extruded interlocking-hexagon massing clad in shimmering bronze, blackened steel and copper — its convex/concave crown reading as a Gotham-City silhouette across the East River. JDS Development Group developed the 800,000-sq-ft tower; Silverstein Properties took control of the unsold inventory in June 2024. The mixed-tenure program holds 398 condominiums (interiors by Gachot Studios) above 150 rentals, with amenities by Woods Bagot, including a 27,000-sq-ft 'Society' wellness floor inside the historic banking hall. Topped out 2021, occupancy from late 2022.

Title: Six Senses Napa Valley
Description: Olson Kundig's heritage-led 750-acre Aetna Springs revival in northern Napa County — Six Senses' first West Coast wine-country resort with 95 keys, 16 branded residences, restored 1890s mineral springs and 1,000 acres of rewilded oak woodland.
DescriptionLong: Set deep in Pope Valley north of Calistoga, Six Senses Napa Valley reanimates the 1873 Aetna Springs resort — the oldest continuously operating resort in California and a Napa County Threatened Treasure — across a 3,000-acre estate. Olson Kundig leads the architectural restoration of nine historic buildings, with new pavilions, ten tented suites, 16 branded residences (interiors by 1508 London) and a wellness village around restored thermal mineral springs that include indoor and outdoor bathing, mud pools and herb-and-essential-oil therapies. Dining includes a communal-table Dining Hall and The Well — an open-air space celebrating the property's history with mineral-water bottling and distilling. Developer team: Weller Development Partners and Mohari Hospitality, operated by Six Senses (IHG Hotels & Resorts). Targeting 2026 opening.

StatusHistory.note: 95 keys (incl. 10 tent structures) + 16 residential estates at former Aetna Springs per Travelsort; opening 2026
95 keys (incl. 10 tent structures) + 16 residential estates at former Aetna Springs per Travelsort; opening 2026

Title: Naples Beach Club, A Four Seasons Resort
Description: Hart Howerton's 125-acre Gulf Coast revival of the historic Naples Beach Hotel — Four Seasons' only Florida Gulf Coast resort with 220 keys, 153 branded residences, a Tom Fazio-designed 18-hole course and 1,000 feet of private beach.
DescriptionLong: On 125 oceanfront acres on Gulf Shore Boulevard in Naples, the Athens Group's redevelopment of the legendary 1946 Naples Beach Hotel & Golf Club delivers Four Seasons' only Gulf Coast resort. Master-planned and designed by Hart Howerton with interiors by Champalimaud, the property reinterprets Naples' historic cottage vernacular at hotel scale — shellcrete walls, cypress and pecky-cypress millwork, white-glazed terra cotta and a hand-painted royal palm mural in the lobby. The program includes 220 hotel rooms and suites with 1,000 feet of private beach, 153 luxury residences across three new beachside buildings and five golfside buildings, eight restaurants and bars, two pools, a 27,000-sq-ft Spa & Wellness center, family programming and a re-designed 100-acre Tom Fazio championship golf course (The Gardens) opening 2026. Hotel debuted late 2025; further openings throughout 2026.

Title: Rosewood Half Moon Bay Antigua
Description: Studio Piet Boon's 132-acre ultra-luxury Caribbean enclave on Half Moon Bay's coral-sand crescent — 47 pavilion suites and a collection of two-to-five-bedroom Rosewood Residences set in landscapes by VITA across one of Antigua's most protected coves.
DescriptionLong: Sited on a private 132-acre headland enclosing Antigua's mile-long Half Moon Bay — a UNESCO-coved coral beach on the island's southeast coast — the Rosewood Half Moon Bay reimagines a long-dormant 1960s resort as a flagship Caribbean Rosewood. Studio Piet Boon designs the architecture and interiors of both the 47 pavilion-style hotel suites and the Rosewood Residences, ranging from two- to five-bedroom homes and four-bedroom beachfront villas with direct sand access; landscape architecture by VITA Inc. weaves restored coastal forest, lagoons and amenities including a beach club, kids club, spa village, signature restaurants and a marina-front yacht club. Developed by Replay Destinations with Replay Resorts; opening targeted, with Residences sales actively underway.

StatusHistory.note: 47 pavilion-style suites per TOPHOTELPROJECTS; opening date still unconfirmed

Title: Lucas Museum of Narrative Art
Description: MAD Architects' biomorphic 300,000-sq-ft 'spaceship' museum in LA's Exposition Park — George Lucas's $1B+ permanent home for narrative art with 1,500 hand-finished FRP cloud panels and a public canopy hovering above 11 acres of restored parkland.
DescriptionLong: Conceived by Ma Yansong of MAD Architects as an extension of Exposition Park's tree canopy, the five-story, 27,900-square-meter Lucas Museum of Narrative Art opens September 22, 2026 as the centerpiece of Los Angeles's Exposition Park, a few blocks from USC and the LA Memorial Coliseum. The undulating exterior is wrapped in more than 1,500 curved fiberglass-reinforced polymer (FRP) panels — each robotically fabricated and hand-finished — that lift at the building's center to form a sheltered public plaza. Inside are 9,290 sq m of galleries, two state-of-the-art theaters, ten education studios, library, restaurant, museum shop and a rooftop event space beneath a ribbed 'cloud' ceiling. The permanent collection holds more than 40,000 works — Norman Rockwell, Frida Kahlo, Jack Kirby, Gordon Parks, Dorothea Lange — alongside cinematic artifacts. Landscape architecture by Studio-MLA's Mia Lehrer; Stantec serves as executive architect; principal benefactors George Lucas and Mellody Hobson.
Developer: Lucas Museum of Narrative Art, Mellody Hobson)

StatusHistory.note: Opening date set for September 22, 2026 in Exposition Park

Title: Hudson's Detroit
Description: SHoP Architects' transformation of Detroit's long-vacant J.L. Hudson's Department Store site — a 1.5M-sq-ft mixed-use complex with a 49-story, 681-foot Hudson's Tower (Michigan's tallest) holding an EDITION hotel and condos above a 12-story office and event block anchored by GM's new global HQ.
DescriptionLong: Hudson's Detroit reimagines the long-vacant block of the demolished J.L. Hudson's Department Store at 1208 Woodward Avenue downtown — once the world's tallest department store. Bedrock-developed, SHoP Architects-designed, the 1.5-million-sq-ft complex pairs the recently completed 12-story, 232-foot Block Building (1240 Woodward) — anchored by General Motors's new global HQ on floors 8-11, with a 56,000-sq-ft event venue 'The Department at Hudson's' and a seven-story atrium capped by a Corvette-headlight skylight — with the 49-story, 681-foot Hudson's Tower, the tallest building in Michigan, holding an EDITION hotel (~227 rooms) and 97 private condominiums above retail. Materials reference Detroit's early-20th-century skyline: glass, steel and white-glazed terracotta echoing the Guardian and Penobscot Buildings. First phase completed October 2025; tower opening targeted 2027. New 'Nick Gilbert Way' pedestrian plaza opens November 2025.

StatusHistory.note: 49-story tower (681ft) with EDITION hotel + 97 luxury condos; tower completes 2027 (office bldg opened 2025/26) per Wikipedia/Detroit News
49-story tower (681ft) with EDITION hotel + 97 luxury condos; tower completes 2027 (office bldg opened 2025/26) per Wikipedia/Detroit News
Tower facade complete and office block + GM HQ already open; the 227-key EDITION hotel and residences open early 2027
Tower facade complete and office block + GM HQ already open; the 227-key EDITION hotel and residences open early 2027
Tower topped out (~10,500 tons of steel), Detroit's tallest in 50 years (Commercial Property Executive).
Office component (1240 Woodward, 400k+ sf Class A, GM anchor tenant) completed and opened; public plaza unveiled Nov 2025. Residential/hotel tower opening 2027 (Bedrock).

Title: Nu Stadium at Miami Freedom Park
Description: MANICA + Arquitectonica's $350M soccer-specific home for David Beckham's Inter Miami CF — a 26,700-seat single-tier bowl crowned by a single white membrane roof reading as Major League Soccer's largest canopy, opened April 4, 2026 as centerpiece of the 131-acre Freedom Park district.
DescriptionLong: Set on the site of the former Melreese Country Club golf course adjacent to Miami International Airport, Nu Stadium at Miami Freedom Park serves as Inter Miami CF's permanent home — the club David Beckham co-founded and Lionel Messi made global. MANICA leads design with Arquitectonica as architect of record (and master planner of the 131-acre Freedom Park district); Thornton Tomasetti structural. The 26,700-seat bowl is single-tiered with a glazed-edge white membrane roof that extends past the seating to cover an elevated concourse — the largest canopy in MLS. Pink-accented seating, a glass players' tunnel viewable from the premium Nu Club, retractable lower north stand, two large curved video boards above the goals, and a Leo Messi Stand on the east side. Brazilian digital bank Nubank acquired naming rights. Cost ~$350M; construction 2023-2026; first MLS match April 4, 2026 vs. Austin FC. Future Freedom Park phases add a 58-acre public park, hotels, retail and entertainment.
Developer: Miami Freedom Park, Jorge & Jose Mas)

Title: New Kansas City Royals Stadium
Description: Populous's $1.9B fountain-and-Crown-Vision downtown ballpark for the Kansas City Royals, breaking ground 2027 on Hallmark's Crown Center campus as the 85-acre, $3B+ centerpiece of a co-developed mixed-use district anchoring both Royals and Hallmark headquarters.
DescriptionLong: On April 22, 2026, the Kansas City Royals and Hallmark Cards jointly announced the new Crown Center ballpark district — an 85-acre, $3B+ mixed-use development to rise on the site of Hallmark's current corporate headquarters. The conceptual renderings (designed by hometown architecture firm Populous) feature a downtown ballpark with a central fountain plaza, a Crown Vision scoreboard and seamless integration with reimagined Hallmark and Royals headquarters. The $1.9B stadium itself is targeted for opening day 2030, with construction beginning in early 2027; the broader district will deliver more than 20,000 construction jobs and over $2B in private investment. Notably, the 85-acre site preserves the historic Our Lady of Sorrows Parish church directly adjacent to the new ballpark.

StatusHistory.note: The Royals selected Crown Center, near Washington Square Park, for their new downtown ballpark.

Title: Burj Jumeira
Description: SOM's 550-meter (1,804-foot) split-volume Dubai supertall designed for state-owned Dubai Holding — a curvilinear two-mass tower rising from a reflecting pool emblazoned with Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum's fingerprint, with a digital-display façade, sky restaurants and a pearl-evoking observation deck atop Downtown Jumeira.
DescriptionLong: Conceived as the centerpiece of the new SOM-masterplanned Downtown Jumeira mixed-use district in Al Sufouh, Burj Jumeira (also Burj Jumeirah) is a 550-meter / 1,804-foot supertall by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill's New York office. The aerodynamic split-volume tower features a large void between two curvilinear masses — a form referencing regional dunes and oases — rising from an oval-shaped pond emblazoned with the fingerprint of Dubai's ruler, Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, encircled by water-feature shops and a terraced outdoor amphitheatre. The façade is to carry an integrated digital display, with a 450-meter sky lounge and sky restaurant and a spherical observation deck evocative of the native Gulf pearl. State-owned Dubai Holding announced the project in January 2019; SOM provides architectural and structural services as well as the surrounding Downtown Jumeira master plan.

StatusHistory.note: Correction: never broke ground — project stalled since 2019, construction has not begun and references were removed from Dubai Holding's site

Title: Universal Epic Universe
Description: Universal's $7B fourth Orlando theme park — the first major new U.S. park in 25 years — opened May 22, 2025 with a hub-and-spoke layout of five worlds (Celestial Park, Super Nintendo World, Ministry of Magic, Dark Universe, How to Train Your Dragon — Isle of Berk) anchored by the Chronos icon tower.
DescriptionLong: Officially opened May 22, 2025, Universal Epic Universe is Universal Destinations and Experiences' fourth Orlando theme park and the most ambitious park the operator has ever built — the first all-new major U.S. theme park in 25 years, with cost estimates around $7B. The park employs a hub-and-spoke layout: at the center sits Celestial Park, a fountain-and-pond garden themed to interdimensional travel and crowned by the Chronos tower, the park's gateway icon. From the hub, four portals lead to themed lands — Super Nintendo World; The Wizarding World of Harry Potter — Ministry of Magic (a 1920s Parisian streetscape with the British Ministry of Magic); Dark Universe (Universal Classic Monsters); and How to Train Your Dragon — Isle of Berk. Approximately 50 attractions, entertainment, dining and retail experiences across the campus, plus the on-site Helios Grand Hotel. Headline rides include Stardust Racers, a dual-launch racing coaster reaching 62 mph and 133 ft. Universal won four 2025 Thea Awards for the park.

Title: Suzhou Museum of Contemporary Art
Description: BIG's 60,000-sq-m 'Chinese knot' of 12 pavilions and courtyards woven beneath an undulating ribbon-like roof of stainless tiles along Suzhou's Jinji Lake — a fifth-facade museum opening 2026 with the BIG-curated 'Materialism' inaugural exhibition.
DescriptionLong: Set along the Jinji Lake waterfront in Suzhou's Industrial Park district, the 60,000-square-meter Suzhou Museum of Contemporary Art (Suzhou MoCA) by BIG — Bjarke Ingels Group is conceived as a garden of 12 pavilions and courtyards woven together by glazed galleries and porticoes — what Ingels describes as a 'Chinese knot' of interconnected sculpture courts and exhibition spaces. The roof's gentle conical undulations recall traditional tiled eaves, and from above (particularly from the nearby Suzhou Ferris wheel) the warm-toned stainless tiles read as a distinctive fifth facade branching out like a rhizome to connect city and lake. Façades combine rippled curved glass with stainless steel. The museum will debut with 'Materialism,' a BIG-curated inaugural exhibition tracing a material journey from stone to recyclate, ahead of the official 2026 public opening.

StatusHistory.note: BIG-designed Suzhou MoCA ran trial operations Jan–Mar 2026; full public opening slated for end of June 2026

Title: Studio Museum
Description: Adjaye Associates + Cooper Robertson's $160M, 82,000-sq-ft, seven-story purpose-built home for The Studio Museum on West 125th Street — a brownstone-referencing facade with a stepped 'reverse stoop,' a Studio Zewde-designed rooftop terrace, and more than doubled gallery and Artist-in-Residence space.
DescriptionLong: Opened to the public on November 15, 2025, the new Studio Museum in Harlem is the first purpose-built home for the celebrated 1968-founded museum of African-American art, on West 125th Street between Lenox and Adam Clayton Powell, Jr. Boulevards. Designed by Adjaye Associates in collaboration with Cooper Robertson, the seven-story, 82,000-square-foot, $160-million building reinterprets the masonry patterns and window compositions of Harlem's brownstones and prewar apartment buildings; a series of glass doors and stepped entryways recall the social stoops that define the area's streetscape, with a generous double-height ground-floor window and a 'reverse stoop' descending into the museum to dissolve the boundary between sidewalk and lobby. Exhibition and Artist-in-Residence spaces have more than doubled, allowing larger-scale work; a rooftop terrace by Harlem-based Studio Zewde adds a landscaped gathering space with native plantings and panoramic Manhattan views.

Title: David Geffen Galleries at LACMA
Description: Peter Zumthor's $724M horizontal, free-flowing concrete-and-glass single-floor museum spanning Wilshire Boulevard at LACMA — a 220,000-sq-ft non-hierarchical gallery elevated 30 feet above street level on seven semi-transparent pavilions, opening April 19, 2026 to house the museum's permanent collection.
DescriptionLong: Designed by Pritzker Prize laureate Peter Zumthor in collaboration with Skidmore, Owings & Merrill, the David Geffen Galleries are a $724M concrete-and-glass single-floor building that curves along Hancock Park and spans Wilshire Boulevard on LACMA's 20-acre Miracle Mile campus — the culmination of a more than two-decade transformation. The free-flowing main floor is elevated nearly 30 feet above street level on seven semi-transparent pavilions that hold a theater, retail, dining, education facilities and outdoor programming around the W.M. Keck Foundation Plaza. The horizontal, single-level layout deliberately collapses traditional museum hierarchies, placing all works on the same plane with naturally lit perimeter galleries and fully enclosed interior rooms. Major construction wrapped at the end of 2024; portions of the lower levels began opening to the public in summer 2025; LACMA expands its gallery space from ~130,000 to 220,000 sq ft. Targets LEED Gold; over 95% construction-and-demolition waste diverted; grand public opening April 19, 2026.
Architect: Peter Zumthor with Skidmore, Owings & Merrill
Developer: Los Angeles County Museum of Art

StatusHistory.note: David Geffen Galleries opened April 19, 2026 with a ribbon-cutting; public grand-opening weekend June 18–22, 2026

Title: Kawonu Golf Club
Description: Andrew Green's first entirely original 18-hole course on a 294-acre Reedy River site in southern Greenville County — a private, golf-only club for ~500 members with a Joel Newman / Thomas and Denzinger clubhouse and on-site cottages, breaking ground 2025 for a 2027 opening.
DescriptionLong: Set on 294 rolling acres west of Fountain Inn at the intersection of S.C. Highway 418 and Fork Shoals Road, Kawonu Golf Club is the first new private golf club to be built in Greenville in roughly 30 years. Renowned course architect Andrew Green — best known for the championship-grade renovations of Congressional Country Club's Blue Course, East Lake Golf Club, Oak Hill, Inverness and Scioto — leads design of his first entirely original 18-hole course on a stunning piece of ground featuring rolling terrain, boulder-topped bluffs, woodlands and ponds bordering the Reedy River, with about 100 acres preserved as nature preserve and irrigation drawn largely from on-site ponds and harvested rainwater. Joel Newman of Thomas and Denzinger Architects designs the clubhouse, cottages and supporting facilities (Newman's portfolio includes Chechessee Creek Club and Ohoopee Match Club). Founded by Scott Ferrell (Greenville resident, 35-year golf veteran and former president of Gary Player Design) and real-estate developer Barton Tuck, the club is structured for ~500 memberships (350 local, 150 national) plus on-site cottage stays. Construction began early 2025; opening targeted for 2027.

StatusHistory.note: Kawonu Golf Club broke ground on its 18-hole Andrew Green-designed private course in southern Greenville County.
Kawonu Golf Club broke ground on its 18-hole Andrew Green-designed private course in southern Greenville County.

Title: Navy SEAL Museum San Diego
Description: An 85,000-square-foot, four-level waterfront museum proposed for San Diego's North Embarcadero, with an angular, ship-shaped form and perforated metal facade designed by ZGF Architects.
DescriptionLong: The Navy SEAL Museum San Diego is a four-level, 85,000-square-foot waterfront museum proposed for 1220 Pacific Highway along the city's North Embarcadero, alongside a new 150-foot public park extending Lane Field Park toward the harbor. The angular, ship-shaped structure features metallic volumes, ribboned facade window reveals, perforated metal screens that filter natural light into the interior, and a 65-foot-tall central atrium, with immersive technology-forward exhibits flowing from upper-level galleries down to a fourth-floor rooftop terrace with sweeping harbor views. The $256 million project is privately financed by the museum's nonprofit parent, with construction targeted to begin in early 2029 and completion projected for 2031–2032.
Developer: Navy SEAL Museum San Diego, Hensel Phelps

StatusHistory.note: The proposed $256M Navy SEAL Museum would rise on a 3.4-acre bayfront site at 1220 Pacific Highway on the North Embarcadero.

Title: 2 World Trade Center
Description: Foster + Partners' newly redesigned 1,224-foot supertall at the World Trade Center campus — a stepped, terraced glass tower set to become American Express's new global HQ, breaking ground spring 2026.
DescriptionLong: Rising 373 meters at 200 Greenwich Street in Lower Manhattan, Two World Trade Center is the final commercial tower of the World Trade Center master plan and the future global headquarters of American Express. Foster + Partners' updated design — released in February 2026 — presents a stepped composition of slender volumes rising from a rectilinear base, articulated by a sequence of landscaped terraces and green roofs that distribute more than an acre of outdoor space throughout the building. The 55-story, ~186,000-square-meter tower will accommodate up to 10,000 employees and is positioned directly across from Calatrava's Oculus and the Perelman Performing Arts Center. Planned as a fully electric building with advanced smart-building systems pursuing LEED certification, the project draws on BIG's stacked-block concept and Foster's earlier crown studies, merging into a hybrid composition. Construction is scheduled to begin in spring 2026, with completion targeted for 2031.

StatusHistory.note: NY YIMBY (May 2026) "resumes construction"; spring 2026 start, AmEx HQ, 55 stories, completion 2031 — proposing as just-starting/ambiguous
American Express signed as anchor tenant and sole occupant; construction resuming spring 2026 on the 55-story Foster + Partners tower
American Express signed as anchor tenant and sole occupant; construction resuming spring 2026 on the 55-story Foster + Partners tower
American Express signed as anchor tenant and sole occupant; construction resuming spring 2026 on the 55-story Foster + Partners tower
American Express signs on as anchor tenant for its new global HQ at 2 WTC

Possibly overstated as construction: only early site mobilization observed May 2026, vertical construction described as "to get underway soon" after AmEx anchor; reviewer to confirm — Commercial Real Estate Direct

Title: Atlantis The Royal
Description: KPF's quarter-mile-long Palm Jumeirah resort and residences — a 43-story stacked-block lattice with cantilevered sky pools, 795 guestrooms, 231 branded residences and Dubai's most extravagant launch event.
DescriptionLong: Set on Dubai's Palm Jumeirah, Atlantis The Royal is a 193,600-square-meter hospitality icon that reinvented the language of the tall building. Designed by Kohn Pedersen Fox's New York office (lead architects James von Klemperer and Elie Gamburg), the 43-story project organizes a series of shifted, stacked residential and hotel blocks into a sinuous quarter-mile lattice, with the gaps between volumes forming sky terraces, cabanas and the now-iconic cantilevered infinity pools — including an 88th-meter Cleopatra-style sky pool. The resort houses 795 guestrooms and suites alongside 231 branded Atlantis residences, 17 restaurants and bars (including outposts by José Andrés, Heston Blumenthal, Costas Spiliadis, Ariana Bundy and more), a vast spa, 90 swimming experiences and one of the largest fountain-and-pyrotechnic shows in the region by WET Design. Developed by Kerzner International with Besix–Ssangyong JV as main contractor and interiors by GA Design and SM Design, Atlantis The Royal opened in 2023 with a star-studded debut headlined by Beyoncé.

Title: Six Senses London at The Whiteley
Description: Six Senses' flagship UK debut inside Foster + Partners' £1bn restoration of The Whiteley in Bayswater — a 109-room hotel and 14 residences with EPR/AvroKO interiors above 139 luxury homes.
DescriptionLong: Set within The Whiteley — the Foster + Partners-led restoration of the 1911 grade II*-listed Whiteley's department store on Queensway in Bayswater — Six Senses London is the brand's first UK property and its global wellness flagship. EPR Architects serves as hotel architect with AvroKO leading interior design, contributing a restrained palette of warm woods, parquet flooring and Art Deco references that wrap 109 rooms and suites, 14 one- and two-bedroom branded residences, and the brand's first urban Six Senses Place social-wellness club. Amenities include a 20-meter pool, 24-hour gym, screening room, co-working lounges, a beauty studio, multiple dining rooms and a spa featuring sound healing, biohacking and longevity programming. The building's reborn marble grand staircase — deconstructed, lengthened and rebuilt by hand in Devon by Foster + Partners — ascends three floors to a restored glass-domed atrium. Six Senses London is part of the wider 1-million-square-foot Whiteley mixed-use district led by Valouran with MARK and C C Land, which also includes 139 Foster + Partners-designed residences, 60,000 sq ft of amenities and a curated collection of 19 commercial brands.

Title: One Bangkok
Description: SOM's $3.9bn, 41-acre 'city within a city' beside Lumphini Park — Thailand's largest private development, with five offices, three hotels, retail podiums and a future 437-meter Signature Tower.
DescriptionLong: One Bangkok is Thailand's largest private-sector development — a $3.9 billion, 16.7-hectare (41-acre) mixed-use district master-planned and master-architected by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill (SOM) at the intersection of Wireless Road, Rama IV and Sathorn, steps from Lumphini Park. Developed by TCC Assets and Frasers Property and built in collaboration with Architects 49 (A49), KPF, Chapman Taylor and Lead8, the first phase combines eight skyscrapers and a series of smaller pavilions around a central civic plaza, delivering five office towers, three hotels/serviced-apartment towers, an art-and-culture quarter, interconnected retail podiums, and 8 hectares of plazas and landscaped greenspace including the preserved Wireless House. Phase two will add SOM's 92-storey, 437-meter (1,430-ft) Signature Tower — set to become the tallest building in Thailand on completion. Climate-responsive design, a central utility plant, sponge parks engineered for a 1-in-500-year flood event, and Thailand's first LEED Platinum Neighborhood Development certification anchor the development, while a permanent public art program features Anish Kapoor, Sir Tony Cragg and Alex Face among others. Phased opening began October 2024 with full delivery through 2030.

StatusHistory.note: In final phase — the district reaches full completion in 2026, with The Forum event space and remaining retail opening this year
One Bangkok held its grand opening on October 25, 2024, debuting the district with a 500-performer celebration.

Title: Lusail Plaza Towers
Description: Foster + Partners' four aluminum-clad supertalls anchoring Lusail's new central business district — twisting elliptical forms targeting 4-star GSAS, with the tallest 301m and the tallest in Qatar.
DescriptionLong: Conceived as the catalyst for Lusail's new central business district — 10 miles north of Doha and at the end of the grand boulevard linking Lusail Stadium to the corniche — Lusail Plaza Towers is Foster + Partners' 1.1-million-square-meter quartet of high-rises arranged symmetrically around a central plaza. Two 70-story towers (the tallest at 301 m, making them Qatar's tallest buildings) flank two 50-story towers and a series of podium buildings filled with shops, cafés and restaurants. The elliptical footprints morph through 90 degrees as they rise, with a central concrete core and sixteen inclined perimeter columns generating the geometry; marine-grade aluminum cladding and twisting external shading fins reduce solar exposure by ~35%, while phase-change-material thermal storage, demand-controlled ventilation and high-pressure hydronic systems target a 4-star regional Global Sustainability Assessment System (GSAS) rating. The towers will house the headquarters of Qatar National Bank, Qatar Central Bank, the Qatar Investment Authority and Qatari Diar, alongside other global organizations, anchoring a new financial and civic core for the city.

Title: Shanghai Grand Opera House
Description: Snøhetta's helical-roofed riverside opera house with three auditoriums — opening late 2026 on the Huangpu River, with a spiraling rooftop pathway and 2,000-seat main hall by Nagata Acoustics.
DescriptionLong: Now nearing completion on the convex bank of the Huangpu River in Shanghai's Expo Houtan district, the Shanghai Grand Opera House is Snøhetta's competition-winning civic landmark — first awarded in 2017 and developed in consortium with East China Architectural Design & Research Institute (ECADI), Theatre Projects and Nagata Acoustics. A sweeping helical roof wraps the site in a single continuous gesture, extending beyond the building's enclosed volumes to form an elevated, publicly accessible civic platform; a spiraling pathway leads visitors from ground level to an observation deck overlooking the Huangpu and the Shanghai skyline. Beneath the roof, three auditoriums anchor the program: a 2,000-seat main hall designed for opera and large-scale productions and tuned to international acoustic standards by Nagata, a 1,200-seat secondary hall and a 1,000-seat flexible black-box theater. Public foyers feature expansive glazed facades, oak flooring and dark-stained wood that supports acoustic performance, alongside restaurants, exhibition spaces, education facilities and screening rooms. Opening is anticipated in the second half of 2026, with the project among ArchDaily's most-anticipated completions of the year.

StatusHistory.note: Snøhetta's helical-roofed opera house on the Huangpu riverfront nearing completion, set to open in second half of 2026

Title: The Whale
Description: Dorte Mandrup's Arctic cultural center on Andøya in northern Norway — a parabolic stone-and-vegetation roof that emerges from the coastal landscape as a whale-watching attraction.
DescriptionLong: Located approximately 300 kilometers north of the Arctic Circle on the island of Andøya near the village of Andenes — within sight of the Bleiksdjupa deep-sea canyon that draws sperm whales close to shore — The Whale is a cultural and scientific center designed by Danish practice Dorte Mandrup with Marianne Levinsen Landscape, Ralph Appelbaum Associates (exhibits), Norconsult and AT Plan & Arkitektur. The building emerges from the rocky shoreline as a single sculpted gesture: a parabolic roof formed by a series of curved steel-and-concrete ribs that span a column-free interior with limited points of support, engineered for the site's wind and snow loads and finished with locally sourced stone and vegetation so the structure visually extends the terrain. The accessible roof functions as a publicly walkable viewing platform sloping toward the Norwegian Sea and surrounding archipelago. Inside, exhibition galleries — devoted to whales and marine ecosystems — open through large glazed walls toward the water, alongside offices, a café, and a retail area, with outdoor paths and viewpoints extending into the landscape. The visitor attraction is currently under construction with an anticipated opening in 2027.

StatusHistory.note: Grand opening set for June 4, 2027 per the museum's official site

Title: Village Landing
Description: A massive 71-acre mixed-use walkable district with retail, restaurants, a 180-room hotel, and office space.
DescriptionLong: A massive 71-acre mixed-use walkable district with over 220,000 square feet of retail, more than 100,000 square feet of restaurant space, a 180-room hotel, 90,000 square feet of office space.
ProjectType: Entertainment, Park, Office, Hotel, Education, Retail

StatusHistory.note: Related Ross unveiled Village Landing, a 71-acre walkable mixed-use village (retail, dining, office, 180-room hotel) on the former K-Park site in Wellington.
Related Ross unveiled Village Landing, a 71-acre walkable mixed-use village (retail, dining, office, 180-room hotel) on the former K-Park site in Wellington.
Related Ross unveiled Village Landing, a 71-acre walkable mixed-use village (retail, dining, office, 180-room hotel) on the former K-Park site in Wellington.

Title: NoLi
Description: 150K+ sf adaptive-reuse retail/office/lifestyle district at Lincoln Road and Meridian Ave — Comras Company is reimagining a five-building Miami Beach assemblage into a pedestrian-first 'street off the street' micro-district anchored by Via NoLi and NoLi Plaza.
DescriptionLong: 150K+ sf adaptive-reuse retail/office/lifestyle district at Lincoln Road and Meridian Ave — Comras Company is reimagining a five-building Miami Beach assemblage into a pedestrian-first 'street off the street' micro-district anchored by Via NoLi and NoLi Plaza.

StatusHistory.note: Comras Company secures $117M from ACORE Capital to acquire five Lincoln Road buildings for the NoLi retail redevelopment; opening targeted Q1 2027
Comras Company secures $117M from ACORE Capital to acquire five Lincoln Road buildings for the NoLi retail redevelopment; opening targeted Q1 2027
Comras Company secures $117M from ACORE Capital to acquire five Lincoln Road buildings for the NoLi retail redevelopment; opening targeted Q1 2027

Title: LILLI
Description: A 53-story boutique waterfront condominium taking shape on Biscayne Bay, bringing just 117 private residences and a wellness-driven amenity program to Miami's Edgewater neighborhood.
DescriptionLong: A new 53-story boutique waterfront condominium from OKO Group rising directly on Biscayne Bay in Miami's Edgewater. Designed by Adrian Smith + Gordon Gill Architecture — the firm behind the Burj Khalifa and Miami's UNA Residences — the project will bring just 117 private one- to four-bedroom residences and penthouses, each with private elevator entry and expansive waterfront views. Sales are officially underway, with residences starting at $1.65 million.

StatusHistory.note: OKO Group's LILLI revealed as a 53-story, 117-residence tower at 717 NE 27th St, Edgewater
OKO Group's LILLI revealed as a 53-story, 117-residence tower at 717 NE 27th St, Edgewater
Neighborhood backfill: Edgewater (per official site)

Title: The Bellemont
Description: A boutique Robert A.M. Stern–designed condominium at Madison Avenue and East 86th Street with just 12 residences, including half-floor, full-floor, duplex, and penthouse homes.
DescriptionLong: A boutique condominium at the corner of Madison Avenue and East 86th Street on the Upper East Side, The Bellemont was designed by Robert A.M. Stern Architects and holds just 12 residences across half-floor, full-floor, duplex, and penthouse layouts. The roughly 13-story building totals about 62,700 square feet and continues Naftali Group's run of classically styled, limestone-clad Madison Avenue condominiums. Its homes sold out within five months of launch, including a record-setting combined penthouse sale, and the building's ground floor carries boutique retail. Now complete.

Title: The Benson
Description: A boutique limestone-clad condominium on Madison Avenue with just 15 classically inspired residences, the first ground-up condo on the avenue in over two decades.
DescriptionLong: A boutique, ground-up condominium on Manhattan's Upper East Side, The Benson brought just 15 classically inspired residences to Madison Avenue — the first new condo development on the famed avenue in more than two decades. Rising 21 stories to roughly 210 feet, the building is wrapped in hand-carved, hand-laid Indiana limestone reminiscent of the great historic apartment houses of the neighborhood. Residences span half-floor, full-floor, and duplex layouts in three- to seven-bedroom configurations, several with private outdoor space, and feature signature fireplaces with custom mantels anchoring expansive living rooms. The building has since sold out.

Title: The Henry
Description: An 18-story limestone-clad Robert A.M. Stern condominium on the Upper West Side with 45 residences, plus a collection of penthouses and townhouses.
DescriptionLong: A boutique condominium rising 18 stories to 209 feet on Manhattan's Upper West Side, The Henry spans nearly 250 feet of frontage along West 84th Street from the corner of Broadway. Designed by Robert A.M. Stern Architects — its first Naftali Group project where the firm shapes both exterior and interiors — with Hill West Architects as architect of record, the limestone-clad building holds 45 residences averaging roughly 3,370 square feet, ranging from three- to six-bedroom homes plus a collection of penthouses and townhouses. The design features setback terraces and balconies on the upper floors, ground-floor retail, and enclosed parking. Construction has topped out, with completion targeted for 2026.
Architect: Robert A.M. Stern Architects, Hill West Architects

Title: The Willow
Description: A 19-story boutique condominium in Gramercy with 69 residences, a COOKFOX-designed masonry facade, and Rockwell Group interiors and amenities.
DescriptionLong: A 19-story boutique condominium in Manhattan's historic Gramercy neighborhood, The Willow offers 69 finely crafted residences as a modern interpretation of classic New York architecture. Designed by COOKFOX Architects with a hand-laid masonry facade blending custom red brick, cast stone, and black metal accents, and with interiors and amenities by Rockwell Group, the building runs from one- to four-bedroom layouts plus a penthouse collection with half- and full-floor units. Residences feature white oak floors, open kitchens with quartz counters and Miele appliances, and Waterworks-fitted baths, with select homes and penthouses gaining private loggias and terraces. Amenities include a 24-hour attended lobby, a Future Green–designed courtyard, a ground-floor library, and a rooftop terrace with grilling and dining stations. Under construction, with completion targeted for 2026.

StatusHistory.note: On track for Q3 2026 completion — Naftali's 19-story, 69-unit Gramercy condo nears delivery
The Willow topped out (Naftali Group; arch COOKFOX) at 201 E 23rd St, Gramercy — official/COOKFOX

Title: House by Shigeru Ban
Description: Pritzker laureate Shigeru Ban's first Florida residential project — a biophilic 12-story condominium in Miami's Upper Buena Vista with roughly 320 residences, a wood-and-glass stacked-box facade, and a lushly landscaped central courtyard.
DescriptionLong: The first Florida residential project by Pritzker Prize–winning architect Shigeru Ban, House occupies nearly an entire city block in Miami's Upper Buena Vista, minutes from the Design District and Wynwood. The 12-story building holds roughly 320 residences and about 50,000 square feet of commercial space, conceived around biophilic principles that fold nature into the architecture — wood tree-columns, water walls, generous terraces, elevated planters, and a dynamic interplay of concrete, wood, and glass. Its mass is broken into smaller stacked "boxes" that step in and out and alternate wood and glass, giving each home its own distinct expression rather than a repetitive tower facade. A three-level podium incorporates screened parking, retail, food and beverage venues, and lifestyle amenities including a fitness center and padel courts, topped by a lushly landscaped courtyard with gardens that transitions to the residential levels above, with a rooftop pool deck, event spaces, shared kitchens, and children's rooms. Italian design group Dexelance serves as exclusive partner across interiors, lighting, kitchens, and residential amenities.

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Design District (per coverage)

Title: Eturna Beachside Residences
Description: A boutique beachfront condominium of 36 residences with ocean and Intracoastal views, floor-to-ceiling glass, private terraces, a resort-style pool and spa, and direct beach service steps from the Hollywood Beach Broadwalk.
DescriptionLong: Eturna Beachside is a seven-story, 36-residence beachfront condominium offering one- to four-bedroom homes and multi-level townhomes ranging from 900 to 2,700 square feet, each with ocean and Intracoastal views, open layouts, floor-to-ceiling glass, and private terraces designed for coastal living. Amenities include a resort-style pool and spa jacuzzi, fitness center, beach service, front-desk concierge, bike storage, EV charging, a gated entrance, and ground-level storage. Set within walking distance of the Hollywood Beach Broadwalk and minutes from Fort Lauderdale International Airport, the building permits short-term rentals on a 30-day minimum, up to twelve times per year.
Developer: Condra Property Group, Hansford Boca LLC

StatusHistory.note: Under construction on Hollywood Beach; 36-unit boutique nearing its 2026 delivery
Secured a $40M construction loan from S3 Capital
Official site: ~65% complete, delivery Q2 2026 (supersedes earlier "Fall 2025") — eturnabeachside.com
Official site: ~65% complete, delivery Q2 2026 (supersedes earlier "Fall 2025") — eturnabeachside.com

Title: Vista Harbor Residences
Description: A waterfront condominium and yacht club on the Miami River, two mid-rise towers of roughly 242 residences with 650 feet of river frontage, private superyacht dockage, and 37,000 square feet of amenities.
DescriptionLong: Vista Harbor Residences & Yacht Club rises along the Miami River as two mid-rise towers holding approximately 242 residences, from studios to four-bedroom homes between 498 and 2,330 square feet, each with floor-to-ceiling glass, nine-foot ceilings, expansive terraces, and views of the river and skyline. The development commands more than 650 feet of direct river frontage with private dockage for vessels up to 250 feet, and offers 37,000 square feet of amenities including a rooftop pool deck with hot and cold plunges and private cabanas, two pickleball courts, fitness centers, a yoga room and sauna, a lobby library and wine room, a club room, a co-working business center, and a self-service dog salon. Select units permit short-term rentals, and the towers sit minutes from Brickell and Downtown Miami.

StatusHistory.note: ALTA Development's Vista Harbor Residences & Yacht Club — 242 units in two 12-story towers with 650 ft of Miami River frontage; sales launched Feb 2026

Title: Continuum 12000 Sport & Wellness Residences
Description: A waterfront sport-and-wellness condominium on North Miami's bayfront, 262 residences anchored by the 150,000-square-foot Mermaid Club with watersports, a private marina, longevity spa, and multi-sport courts.
DescriptionLong: Continuum 12000 Sport & Wellness Residences brings the Continuum brand north from its South Beach roots to a North Miami bayfront site, a residential tower of 262 condominiums, penthouses, and townhomes from 1,000 to 3,500 square feet with panoramic bay views and direct marina access. At its core is The Mermaid Club, a 150,000-square-foot waterfront sport-and-wellness destination with immersive water experiences, a private marina of 20 boat slips, a beach deck, a 4,500-square-foot fitness center, indoor pickleball and multi-sport courts, golf and sport simulators, putting greens, and a spa and longevity center with contrast therapy, cold plunges, and infrared saunas. Paddleboards and watersports equipment are available to residents.

StatusHistory.note: Vocabulary cleanup: removing the 'Spa' tag from the TMW type registry (spas are an amenity, not a primary use). Stripping Spa from types[] across all affected projects.

Title: Mila Residences Bay Harbor
Description: An intimate collection of nine signature residences on Bay Harbor Islands, designed in tropical-modern style with private elevator entry, expansive east-to-west terraces, a skyline pool and solarium, and Italian kitchens.
DescriptionLong: Mila Bay Harbor is an intimate seven-story collection of nine signature residences in the tranquil enclave of Bay Harbor Islands, designed around tropical-modern architecture where light, materials, and views create refined yet calm interiors. Homes range from 2,100 to 3,900 square feet across two- to four-bedroom layouts, each with private elevator entry, expansive east-to-west terraces, flood-resilient construction, and Italian kitchens with premium Miele appliances. Amenities include a skyline pool and solarium, a fitness and garden sanctuary, a rooftop summer kitchen, a secure smart-access lobby, and pet-friendly design, with private marina slips nearby and Bal Harbour Shops, beaches, and top-rated schools just steps away.

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Bay Harbor Islands (per project name)

Title: Alma Residences Bay Harbor
Description: A rare boutique development of nine estate-style residences on Bay Harbor Islands, blending clean modern lines with soft curves and natural materials, with Italian kitchens, spa-inspired baths, and a rooftop pool and yoga area.
DescriptionLong: Alma Bay Harbor is a boutique seven-story development of just nine estate-style residences in the serene, exclusive Bay Harbor Islands community, its architecture blending clean modern lines with soft curves and natural materials that echo the calm rhythm of island life. Homes range from 2,100 to 4,400 square feet across two- to five-bedroom layouts and penthouses, with Italian-style kitchens and integrated Miele appliances, natural wood accents, imported Italian tile, spa-inspired bathrooms, and large windows opening living spaces to the outdoors. Amenities include a rooftop pool and yoga area, a rooftop summer kitchen, a secure smart-access lobby, and pet-friendly design. Set between Bal Harbour, Surfside, and Indian Creek, the homes sit within a quiet, tree-lined neighborhood steps from Bal Harbour Shops, parks, and Biscayne Bay.

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Bay Harbor Islands (per project name)

Title: The William Residences North Miami Beach
Description: A 374-residence tower bringing modern living to North Miami Beach, studios to three-bedrooms with floor-to-ceiling windows and Italkraft kitchens, anchored by two full amenity floors totaling more than 40,000 square feet.
DescriptionLong: The William is a 26-story, 374-residence tower in North Miami Beach offering studios to three-bedroom layouts from 552 to 1,412 square feet, each with floor-to-ceiling windows, expansive glass-railed balconies, imported porcelain flooring, and custom Italkraft cabinetry, with kitchens equipped with Bosch appliances, quartz counters, and Hansgrohe fixtures. Two full floors hold a combined 40,459 square feet of amenities including a resort-style pool and sun deck, an alfresco dining station with summer kitchen, an outdoor bar and terrace lounge with firepits, a fitness center, yoga studio, outdoor training and recovery zone, spa treatment rooms, a co-working lounge and library, an indoor café lounge, an interactive kids' playroom, and a pickleball court, plus seven floors of secure parking with EV charging and a rooftop pet station. The tower sits minutes from Aventura, Bal Harbour, Atlantic beaches, and Oleta River State Park, with quick access to Brightline and two international airports.
Architect: Carlos Ott, Behar Font & Partners, Urban Robot

StatusHistory.note: Blue Road & Ilia's 26-story, 374-unit Carlos Ott-designed tower at 2040 NE 163rd St targeting a mid-2026 groundbreaking

Title: 72 Carlyle Miami Beach
Description: A 134-residence tower in North Beach blending sophisticated Italian design with Miami's coastal lifestyle, every detail by Piero Lissoni, with more than 35,000 square feet of amenities including a resort pool, exhibition padel courts, and a rooftop sky terrace.
DescriptionLong: 72 Carlyle is a 20-story, 134-residence tower in Miami Beach's North Beach, positioned near the ocean, Intracoastal, and waterfront parks with easy access to Bal Harbour, Surfside, and the Faena District. Designed inside and out by Piero Lissoni, it blends sophisticated Italian design with Miami's coastal lifestyle across studios to three-bedroom homes and penthouses from 751 to 2,097 square feet, each with panoramic views and expansive private terraces. More than 35,000 square feet of curated amenities include a landscaped resort pool with cabanas overlooking the Intracoastal, outdoor exhibition padel courts, a Lido terrace and dining pergola, three interconnected social suites with an aperitivo lounge and wine cellar with chef's kitchen, a Technogym fitness center, yoga studio, residents' spa with steam room and sauna, outdoor hot and cold plunge, a Lissoni library, co-working lounge, kids' playroom, and a rooftop sky terrace with view deck, dining areas, and a wet bar.

StatusHistory.note: Completion now targeted Q4 2028 per Robb Report
Groundbreaking now targeted Summer 2026 (was 2027) per Haute Living & Robb Report; completion Q4 2028

Title: Altamira by Lennar
Description: A master-planned community of new single-family homes and townhomes in Homestead, built around a golf course with a clubhouse, swimming pool, and fitness center, now selling.
DescriptionLong: Altamira is a master-planned community in Homestead offering brand-new single-family homes and townhomes from 1,334 to 3,105 square feet across two- to five-bedroom layouts. The community is built around an extensive amenity package anchored by a golf course, plus a clubhouse, swimming pool, fitness center, and tot lot. Homes are now available for sale and delivering, with two-story floor plans designed for families seeking new construction in South Miami-Dade.

StatusHistory.note: Master-planned community now selling and delivering move-in-ready homes with clubhouse and golf amenities open

Title: Botanic Residences West Brickell
Description: A boutique eight-story condominium of 51 fully furnished residences in West Brickell, designed for flexibility with short-term rental policies, an outdoor pool with sunset views, fitness center, and co-working library.
DescriptionLong: Botanic is a boutique eight-story, 51-residence condominium in West Brickell, just west of Brickell, Downtown, the Miami River, and the Health District. Homes range from one- to three-bedroom layouts between 580 and 1,080 square feet, delivered fully furnished with open-plan layouts, smart keyless entry, and stainless steel appliances. The building is built for flexibility, supporting short-term and daily rentals for owners, investors, and vacationers. Amenities include an outdoor pool with sunset views and a sun deck, a state-of-the-art fitness center, a library and co-working space, secure bicycle storage, a pet-friendly community, controlled access, dedicated front-desk service, and landscaped grounds.

StatusHistory.note: Now under construction in West Brickell — 8-story, 51-unit boutique by Forth Development
Structure topped off in early 2026
Forth Development and MICL secure $17M construction loan for Botanic Residences

Title: Canal by IQ Concept Aventura
Description: A boutique six-story condominium of 34 tech-forward residences along the Oleta Canal in West Aventura, with direct water access for kayaking and paddleboarding, a canal-front sauna and Jacuzzi, and smart-home control via the IQ app.
DescriptionLong: Canal by IQ is a boutique six-story, 34-residence condominium along the scenic Oleta Canal in West Aventura, offering studios, one-, and two-bedroom homes from 474 to 1,177 square feet, delivered fully furnished and move-in ready. Residents enjoy direct water access for kayaking and paddleboarding, a canal-front deck with sauna and Jacuzzi, canal-side lounges with water-sport equipment, a fully equipped fitness center, pool, BBQ terrace, 24-hour concierge, covered parking, and smart-home integration managed through the IQ app. Interiors feature furniture packages by Breton and kitchens by Ornare, and the building permits flexible short-term rentals without restriction, minutes from Greynolds Park and the upcoming Brightline station.

StatusHistory.note: Neighborhood backfill: Aventura (per project name)

Title: 7 Park Residences Hallandale
Description: A LEED Gold condominium of 121 residences facing the 16-acre Peter Bluesten Park in Hallandale Beach, with 30,000 square feet of amenities, a clean-edge pool, wellness center, and content-creation room, on a short-term-rental-friendly model.
DescriptionLong: 7 Park Residences is an eight-story, 121-residence condominium in Hallandale Beach offering studios to three-bedroom homes from 500 to 1,645 square feet, with open layouts, floor-to-ceiling windows, Mia Cucina kitchens, Bosch appliances, Italian porcelain flooring, and smart-home readiness. The building carries 30,000 square feet of amenities including a clean-edge pool with two hot tubs, private cabanas, a 3,000-square-foot fitness center and sweat room, a wellness center with steam showers, sauna, and cold plunges, a lounge and party room, communal and private BBQ stations, an outdoor lawn with ping pong and a large sandbox, a co-working space with conference room, a content-creation room, and a media room. LEED Gold certified with EV charging and pet-friendly policies, it faces the 16-acre Peter Bluesten Park and a $35M YMCA and permits flexible short-term rentals.

StatusHistory.note: Kadima broke ground on the 121-unit Gold LEED tower at 218–220 SE 7th Street

Title: Roble One Coconut Grove
Description: An exclusive boutique development of just 15 residences at the intersection of Coconut Grove and Coral Gables, offering ultra-luxury serenity at accessible prices with an activated rooftop, heated plunge pool, and premium gym.
DescriptionLong: Roble One Residences is an exclusive boutique development of just 15 meticulously designed residences at the intersection of Coconut Grove and Coral Gables, a three-story building that delivers the serenity of ultra-luxury living at more accessible prices. Homes span one- and two-bedroom layouts from 582 to 986 square feet, with flexible ownership permitting both short- and long-term rentals for passive income. Amenities include an activated rooftop with grills, a heated plunge pool with jets, a premium gym with trainer-on-demand interactive displays, sidewalk bike racks, and ground-floor package storage. The location sits minutes from Cocowalk, Biscayne Bay, Miracle Mile, and The Biltmore, with completion anticipated by the end of 2026.

StatusHistory.note: Coconut Grove boutique condo on track for summer 2026 completion

Title: 255 East 77th Street
Description: A 36-story, 500-foot limestone skyscraper at Second Avenue and East 77th Street with 62 condominiums and a deep amenity collection including an indoor pool and health club.
DescriptionLong: A 36-story residential skyscraper rising 500 feet at the corner of Second Avenue and East 77th Street in Lenox Hill, 255 East 77th Street is designed by Robert A.M. Stern Architects with Hill West Architects as architect of record. The warm limestone tower spans about 170,481 square feet and holds 62 condominiums in two- to five-bedroom layouts, its facade carrying refined classical proportions and oak-leaf motifs with setback terraces near a multifaceted crown. Amenities include a full-time doorman and concierge, an outdoor garden, a fitness center and health club with a yoga studio, indoor swimming pool, sauna and steam room, plus a lounge, game room, pet spa, children's playroom, and screening room. Construction has topped out, with completion targeted for 2026.
Architect: Robert A.M. Stern Architects, Hill West Architects

StatusHistory.note: Façade complete and nearing completion; RAMSA's 62-unit UES tower on track for fall 2026 delivery

Title: 200 East 83rd
Description: A 35-story limestone tower at Third Avenue and East 83rd Street, among the Upper East Side's tallest residential buildings, with 86 bespoke one- to six-bedroom condominiums.
DescriptionLong: Rising 35 stories to 489 feet at the corner of Third Avenue and East 83rd Street in Yorkville, 200 East 83rd is among the Upper East Side's tallest residential towers. Designed by Robert A.M. Stern Architects and co-developed by Naftali Group and Rockefeller Group, the limestone-clad building holds 86 bespoke condominiums ranging from one- to six-bedroom layouts averaging roughly 2,400 square feet, with a robust collection of club-like wellness and entertainment amenities set within monumental spaces. The tower includes ground-floor retail and parking, and stands a short walk from the Met, Central Park, and the 86th Street subway. Now complete and sold out.
Architect: Robert A.M. Stern Architects, SLCE Architects

Title: Mandarin Oriental Punta Negra
Description: Mandarin Oriental's first Balearic resort, set across five low-rise buildings on Mallorca's western coast with 131 keys, two private coves, and six dining concepts led by Nobu Matsuhisa and Dani García.
DescriptionLong: Mandarin Oriental Punta Negra opened in spring 2026 as the brand's first resort in the Balearic Islands and its third property in Spain, set on one of Mallorca's most cherished stretches of coastline in Calvià near Puerto Portals. Spread across five low-rise buildings tucked among Aleppo pines that frame uninterrupted Mediterranean views, the resort opens with 131 keys — including 26 suites and nine intimate casitas, many with private plunge pools — and two secluded coves accessible only to guests. Architecture by HKS and Madrid-based Estudio Lamela pairs with interiors by Laura Gonzalez in a soft neutral palette of natural materials and hand-placed stone, with curated works by local and international artists threading through the guestrooms, shared spaces, gardens, and terraces. The dining program is led by two of the most recognizable names in global gastronomy: Matsuhisa from Nobu Matsuhisa for Nikkei-style sushi and sashimi at sea, and Leña from Dani García as a fire-and-smoke steakhouse, joined by Jacinta (contemporary Mexican), Leppoc (Mediterranean and Levantine all-day), Sobretaula (alfresco tapas), and Thalos (Greek-inspired poolside). A seaside spa spans nine treatment cabins with couple's suites and a tea ceremony, layered over a full thermal circuit of hydro-massage pool, steam rooms, cold plunges, and aromatherapy showers — alongside two adult outdoor pools, a children's pool, an indoor pool, and a fitness centre.

StatusHistory.note: Vocabulary cleanup: removing the 'Spa' tag from the TMW type registry (spas are an amenity, not a primary use). Stripping Spa from types[] across all affected projects.

Title: Ónix Delray Beach
Description: A boutique four-story condominium of just 26 residences three blocks from Atlantic Avenue, inspired by white onyx with interiors by Paris-based designer Tristan Auer.
DescriptionLong: Ónix Delray Beach is a four-story boutique condominium of just 26 two- and three-bedroom residences rising at 318 SE 5th Avenue, three blocks from the heart of Atlantic Avenue in downtown Delray Beach. The roughly $50 million project takes its name and aesthetic cue from white onyx — a semi-precious stone prized for its soft iridescence and translucency — carried through the façade, lobby, and interior accents alongside fluted natural wood, glass balconies, and a contemporary palette of light tones and clean lines. Residences range from approximately 1,400 to 2,200 square feet across five floor plans (named for South Florida cities — Aventura, Boca Raton, Coral Gables, Palm Beach, and Delray Beach), with 10.5-foot ceilings, floor-to-ceiling impact windows and sliders, expansive glass-railed terraces, GE Monogram kitchens, and spa bathrooms with Porcelanosa tile. Architecture is by Arch 4 Design under principal Norberto Loianno, with interiors by Paris-based Tristan Auer — the celebrated French interior architect known for the Hotel de Crillon, Carlton Cannes, and Le Scribe Paris. Amenities include a pool with cabanas, summer kitchen, fire pit, clubroom, and fitness center, alongside roughly 5,000 square feet of ground-floor commercial space. Developed by 1112 Development Group (Mike Bokzam, Boca Raton-based) with general contractor Black Star Construction Group, and backed in part by Wellington-based investors August "Gussie" and Haley Busch, great-great-grandchildren of Anheuser-Busch co-founder Adolphus Busch. Vertical construction began in May 2025 with completion targeted for summer 2026.
Developer: 1112 Development Group, Black Star Construction Group

StatusHistory.note: Vertical construction underway at Ónix, three blocks from Atlantic Avenue
Vertical construction begun (1112 Development; GC Black Star Construction); ~30% sold — PR Newswire

Title: Four Seasons Hotel Sevilla
Description: Four Seasons' debut hotel in Sevilla, a 55-room landmark conversion on Plaza Nueva in the Arenal Quarter, opening in 2028.
DescriptionLong: Four Seasons Hotel Sevilla will occupy a landmark mid-20th-century building on Plaza Nueva in the city's Arenal Quarter, developed in partnership with Spanish real estate investment firm Blasson. The five-story hotel will offer approximately 55 guest rooms ranging from 430 to 1,260 square feet, including a Presidential Suite, with architecture led by Madrid-based Estudio Lamela and interiors by AD100 honoree Belén Domecq. Plans include three dining and gathering outlets — a street-level restaurant on Plaza Nueva, a rooftop restaurant and bar, and a ground-floor lounge — alongside a spa, fitness center and a rooftop swimming pool. The hotel builds on Four Seasons' existing properties in Madrid and Mallorca and is slated to open in 2028.
