Title: The Wolseley Hotel New York
Description: The Wolseley Hotels' first property worldwide, a 76-room luxury hotel opening in early 2027 in the landmark 1905 building at 130 West 44th Street in Midtown Manhattan.
DescriptionLong: The Wolseley Hotel New York will be the inaugural property of The Wolseley Hotels, a new luxury brand from Minor Hotels, opening in early 2027 as the brand's global flagship and Minor Hotels' entry into the United States luxury segment. The hotel occupies the landmark building at 130 West 44th Street, built in 1905 as the clubhouse for The Lambs and designed by Stanford White of McKim, Mead & White; the building reopened as The Chatwal New York in 2010 and is now being repositioned by owner BJGH. It will offer 76 rooms and suites, the first US location of The Wolseley restaurant, a cellar-level speakeasy bar and a wellness center.

StatusHistory.note: Minor Hotels converting the former Lambs Club at 130 W 44th St into 76-key Wolseley flagship near Bryant Park
Minor Hotels converting the former Lambs Club at 130 W 44th St into 76-key Wolseley flagship near Bryant Park

Title: Ruby New York City
Description: An 187-room urban lifestyle hotel rising inside a converted 1930s building on Avenue of the Americas, anchored by a 24/7 lobby bar open to guests and locals.
DescriptionLong: A flagship conversion of an 18-story, 1930s-era building on Manhattan's Avenue of the Americas into 187 guest rooms built around a lean, design-forward "everything you need, nothing you don't" philosophy — signature beds, rainfall showers, and premium finishes in efficiently scaled spaces. The social heart of the property is a 24/7 lobby bar serving both overnight guests and neighborhood regulars, set steps from Herald Square, the Empire State Building, and Madison Square Garden.
Developer: Ac Developers, IHG Hotels & Resorts, Ruby Hotels, Aimbridge Hospitality

StatusHistory.note: IHG's Ruby converting an 18-story 1930s building on Sixth Ave near Herald Square; 187 keys, 2027 debut
IHG's Ruby converting an 18-story 1930s building on Sixth Ave near Herald Square; 187 keys, 2027 debut
IHG's Ruby converting an 18-story 1930s building on Sixth Ave near Herald Square; 187 keys, 2027 debut

Title: Mandarin Oriental, Desaru Coast
Description: A beachfront resort set within 128 acres of rainforest on the South China Sea, with 44 suites — each with a private plunge pool, courtyard and veranda — plus a four-bedroom pool villa.
DescriptionLong: A low-rise luxury beachfront resort folded into 128 acres of rainforest along the South China Sea. The property comprises 44 secluded suites, each with its own private plunge pool, courtyard and veranda framing views of either the sea or the canopy, anchored by a four-bedroom pool villa. Guests have a choice of five dining destinations, including a beach club and a coastal lounge bar, while a spa brings a yoga pavilion, fitness centre and beauty salon. Sporting facilities include tennis and padel courts, with championship golf nearby. A collection of branded residences is planned alongside the resort.
Developer: Desaru Development Holdings, Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group

StatusHistory.note: Vocabulary cleanup: removing the 'Spa' tag from the TMW type registry (spas are an amenity, not a primary use). Stripping Spa from types[] across all affected projects.
Restore Resort tag stripped by deprecated resort→Hotel worker synonym (fixed in worker/src/mcp.js, deployed 2026-06-17).

Title: Olana Naples
Description: A six-story Gulf-front enclave of just 12 estate-sized residences on Gulf Shore Boulevard North, anchored by two Beachfront Villas priced from $35 million.
DescriptionLong: Olana Naples introduces a first-of-its-kind residential model on the Gulf Coast at 1121 Gulf Shore Boulevard North — just 12 estate-sized homes, each exceeding 10,000 square feet, set within a six-story enclave. The development's recently debuted Beachfront Villas anchor the second floor with two grand-scale residences priced from $35 million, each spanning over 11,500 square feet with five bedrooms, 2,000-plus-square-foot terraces, eighty linear feet of Gulf frontage, and private pools set directly above the beach. Architecture by Randall Stofft Architects and SB Architects with interiors by Champalimaud Design balances Gulf-facing scale with multigenerational programming: presentation kitchens paired with chef's kitchens, climate-controlled wine rooms, soundproof theaters, dedicated wellness suites with steam room, sauna, and cold plunge. Developed by Kolter Urban and BH Group, with residences pricing from $30 million.
Architect: Randall Stofft Architects, SB Architects, Champalimaud Design

StatusHistory.note: Kolter Urban is pre-selling Olana's 12 beachfront residences; groundbreaking is scheduled for 2026, not yet underway.
Kolter Urban is pre-selling Olana's 12 beachfront residences; groundbreaking is scheduled for 2026, not yet underway.

Title: Miramar Cove
Description: A 125-acre mixed-use waterfront community in west Broward County built around a 5.5-acre inland basin with translucent blue water, designed to create a beach experience 30 minutes from the Atlantic — 2,874 residences, 400,000 square feet of retail, 125,000 square feet of Class A office, and a 185-key hotel at the intersection of Miramar Parkway and Red Road.
DescriptionLong: Miramar Cove is a $1 billion, 125-acre master-planned waterfront community at the intersection of Miramar Parkway and Red Road in west Broward County, one block from the Florida Turnpike and minutes from Hard Rock Stadium. The centerpiece is a 5.5-acre "Blue Mar Basin" water feature engineered with a proprietary Martin Aquatic system to create a beach-like inland waterfront environment surrounded by restaurants, retail, residential buildings, office components, hospitality uses, and pedestrian promenades. At full buildout, the community will deliver 2,874 residential units (including 200 townhomes and approximately 100 affordable units), 400,000 square feet of retail with a 35,000-square-foot grocery, 125,000 square feet of Class A office, and a 185-key hotel — supporting an anticipated population of roughly 8,000 residents. Amenities include five miles of walking paths, a 10-acre Founders Park, the 25-acre "Estuary" natural preserve, a residents-only beach club, and a public entertainment district programmed for daily and evening activation. The site sits adjacent to Miramar Park of Commerce, one of South Florida's major business hubs with more than 15,000 employees across 165 companies. Construction is planned in two phases with a grand opening targeted for the fourth quarter of 2028.

StatusHistory.note: Sunbeam Properties and Stiles broke ground on the $1B, 125-acre Miramar Cove on May 27, 2026; plan includes 2,874 homes and a 185-room hotel, with grand opening targeted Q4 2028.

Title: Saravela
Description: A two-tower luxury condominium development rising on North Tamiami Trail near Sarasota Bay — downtown Sarasota's first new high-rise designed for short-term rental freedom, with 282 fully furnished residences, 11 townhomes, and more than 47,000 square feet of amenities across six dedicated levels.
DescriptionLong: Saravela is a two-tower luxury condominium development rising on North Tamiami Trail at the northern gateway to downtown Sarasota, distinguished as the city's first new high-rise designed for short-term rental freedom — letting owners rent their fully furnished residences for stays as short as three days through a professionally managed program. The 1.7-acre site straddles two zoning districts, with one tower rising 18 stories in the Downtown Bayfront and the other stepping down to 11 stories in the Downtown Core. Together the towers deliver 282 condominiums and penthouses, 11 two-story townhomes, 40 attainable rental units, and more than 11,000 square feet of ground-floor retail. Inside each residence, 12-foot ceilings, floor-to-ceiling glass, expansive terraces, Italian cabinetry, Sub-Zero and Wolf appliances, and furnishings curated by Steven G. create a layered, modern coastal aesthetic. The amenity program spans more than 47,000 square feet across six dedicated levels — two rooftop pools overlooking Sarasota Bay, two spas, club lounges, wine vaults, fitness studios, saunas, hot and cold plunge pools, a golf simulator, putting green, bocce court, an attended Kids' Club with duckpin bowling and splash pool, game lounges, co-working spaces, pet amenities, and rooftop firepits, all backed by concierge, valet, optional housekeeping, grocery delivery, 24-hour security, and a complimentary one-year sports membership at TPC Prestancia. The development sits within walking distance of The Quay, The Bay Park, waterfront dining, galleries, marinas, performing arts venues, and St. Armands Circle.
Architect: Kimmich Smith Architecture, ID & Design International

StatusHistory.note: GSP Development unveils Saravela, a two-tower luxury condominium with a flexible short-term-rental model in downtown Sarasota.
GSP Development unveils Saravela, a two-tower luxury condominium with a flexible short-term-rental model in downtown Sarasota.
GSP Development unveils Saravela, a two-tower luxury condominium with a flexible short-term-rental model in downtown Sarasota.
Confirmed Residences: 282 condos plus townhomes (ancillary ground-floor retail) in downtown Sarasota.

Title: Aria Reserve
Description: Twin 62-story glass towers rising along five acres of Biscayne Bay waterfront in Edgewater — set to become the tallest waterfront residential twin towers in the United States, with 782 condominium residences across two acres of indoor and outdoor amenities.
DescriptionLong: Aria Reserve is a twin-tower luxury condominium development on five acres of Biscayne Bay waterfront in Miami's Edgewater neighborhood — twin 62-story glass towers rising approximately 649 feet, set to become the tallest waterfront residential twin towers in the United States. Together the towers deliver 782 condominium residences across 547 linear feet of water frontage, behind an iconic zig-zag facade pattern designed to read as twin sinuous curves rising from bay to sky. The South Tower opened in 2025 and the North Tower follows in 2026. Floor categorizations include Panoramic Residences on floors 3–50 (10-foot ceilings), Skyview Residences on floors 51–56 (11-foot ceilings), Penthouses on floors 57–59, and three Tri-Level Penthouse Mansions on floors 60–62 — seven-bedroom three-story residences ranging from 7,790 to 8,385 square feet, each with a private rooftop terrace, summer kitchen, and private pool. Every residence features two private elevators, a dedicated office or bonus room, flow-through layouts with east- and west-facing waterfront views, and 11-foot-deep outdoor terraces. The amenity program spans more than two acres of indoor and outdoor space — a semi-Olympic pool, full-service spa, fitness and yoga studios, tennis and basketball courts, children's play zones, screening rooms, co-working lounges, a private marina component, a luxury bayview restaurant, a dog park, meditation deck, and podium and bayfront decks tied into the Miami Baywalk, anchored by a residents-only sky lobby with wine cellar and business center.
Architect: Arquitectonica, MORADA Haute Furniture Boutique, ArquitectonicaGEO

StatusHistory.note: Aria Reserve's North Tower surpassed the halfway mark in construction.

Title: White Elephant Aspen
Description: A 54-key boutique hotel on Main Street in Aspen's historic West End, with mountain-contemporary architecture in wood, stone, and brass — anchored by Lola 41 in a glass-enclosed pool pavilion and a 125-piece art collection threaded through the public spaces and guest rooms.
DescriptionLong: White Elephant Aspen is a 54-key boutique hotel on Main Street in Aspen's historic West End, blending mountain-contemporary architecture with an interior signature of warm rust, cognac, charcoal, and warm-white tones layered over leather, wood, and stone. The room program runs from King Rooms to Luxury Hideaways, mountain- and town-view King Suites, the Highlands Suite, and the West End Suite, with the Ajax Penthouse at the top — a three-bedroom, 1,560-square-foot retreat with a chef's kitchen, a log fireplace, and a private mountain-view terrace with a fire pit overlooking Aspen Mountain. Flexible adjoining layouts let families and groups connect rooms into multi-bedroom suites. Public spaces include a glass-enclosed pool pavilion that houses Lola 41, the property's seafood and sushi restaurant, and the 41 Below private dining space. Brass elephant details, a brass doorknocker on every guest-room door, and a 125-piece curated art collection thread throughout the property, complemented by a chauffeured BMW fleet and an interior palette designed to harmonize with the alpine landscape rather than dominate it.

Title: Magic City Innovation District
Description: A 17-acre mixed-use master plan in Little Haiti spanning 17 parcels and 7.8 million square feet of office, residential, hotel, and retail — programmed around a proposed Brightline transit station with more than 2,600 residential units, 432 hotel rooms, and roughly 2 million square feet of office space across phased delivery.
DescriptionLong: Magic City Innovation District is a 17-acre, 17-parcel mixed-use master plan in Little Haiti, just north of Wynwood and the Design District and adjacent to the MiMo district. The Special Area Plan, approved by the Miami City Commission in 2019 and revived in June 2026, calls for 7.8 million square feet of programmed development including more than 2,600 residential units, 432 hotel rooms, approximately 2 million square feet of office space, and 344,548 square feet of commercial-retail. Roughly 165,528 square feet — about 21% of the total lot area — is reserved for open space. The site is generally bounded by Northeast 2nd Avenue, Northeast 4th Court, Northeast 59th Terrace, and Northeast 64th Terrace, centered around a proposed Little Haiti Brightline station. The signature project Station M, modeled on Paris's Station F, will deliver a two-tower innovation center with 215,000 square feet of incubator and accelerator space across more than 500,000 square feet of office, retail, and restaurant uses. Phase 1 anchors the district with an office campus and a 25-story residential tower carrying 349 rental apartments and 10,000 square feet of ground-floor retail. The phased master plan integrates adaptive reuse of existing factories and warehouses with new construction, and includes a $31 million commitment to the Little Haiti Revitalization Trust supporting affordable housing, education, small-business development, and public parks.

StatusHistory.note: Magic City Innovation District, a ~$1B master-planned mixed-use development, advanced in Miami's Little Haiti.

Title: Waterline
Description: At 1,025 feet and 74 stories, Waterline is Texas' tallest building — a mixed-use tower beside Waterloo Greenway combining 352 luxury apartments, a 1 Hotel, Class AA offices and retail, with residences opening fall 2026.
DescriptionLong: Waterline is a 74-story, 1,025-foot mixed-use supertall at 98 Red River Street in Austin's Rainey Street district, now the tallest building in Texas. Developed by Lincoln Property Company and Kairoi Residential and designed by Kohn Pedersen Fox, the tower stacks 352 luxury apartments across its top 33 floors above roughly 703,000 square feet of Class AA office space, a 252-key 1 Hotel occupying 13 floors, and 24,000 square feet of ground-level retail and restaurants. The 3-plus-acre campus integrates with the Waterloo Greenway park system along Waller Creek. Waterline broke ground in September 2022 and topped out in August 2025; the office and hotel are targeted to open in spring 2026 with the residences following in fall 2026.

StatusHistory.note: Construction began on Waterline, set to become Texas' tallest tower at 1,025 ft / 74 stories, on Austin's Rainey Street.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: 352 residences + 251-key 1 Hotel Austin + 700,000 sf office + retail in Texas' tallest tower.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: 352 residences + 251-key 1 Hotel Austin + 700,000 sf office + retail in Texas' tallest tower.
Waterline topped out at 1,025 ft / 74 stories, officially becoming Texas' tallest tower.
1 Hotel Austin opened reservations ahead of its August 2026 opening within the Waterline tower.

Title: The Motley
Description: A three-tower, ~1,200-home mixed-use development rising on a former dairy-factory site in Midtown Nashville, with the 26-story Phase 1 north tower under construction since early 2025.
DescriptionLong: The Motley is a three-tower mixed-use development by IPB Properties transforming a former dairy-factory site at 1401 Church Street into a new gateway to Midtown Nashville. Designed by Nashville-based HASTINGS Architecture with construction by Axiom Builders, the full project will bring nearly 1,200 rental homes and more than 70,000 square feet of locally inspired commercial space, arranged around a central landscaped plaza with 'dancing' arches and a grand stair-ramp 'stramp.' Industrial heritage is echoed in precast facade panels imprinted with silo impressions. The Phase 1 north tower — which officially began construction in February 2025 — rises 26 floors and roughly 300 feet with 326 residential units, 27,000 square feet of amenities and 14,000 square feet of retail; the two later phases add a 32-story tower and a third tower of undetermined height. Phase 1 completion is anticipated in 2027.

StatusHistory.note: IPB Properties (Bosa) advanced The Motley, a multi-tower residential community on the former Dairy Farmers plant in Midtown Nashville.
The Motley officially started construction on its 26-story Phase 1 tower in Midtown Nashville.

Title: Flats Flagler Gateway
Description: A 12-story, 215-unit Class A Opportunity Zone apartment development in Fort Lauderdale's Flagler Village blending market-rate and workforce housing, which broke ground in April 2026.
DescriptionLong: Flats Flagler Gateway is a 12-story, 215-unit Class A multifamily development by CEDARst Companies at 745 N Andrews Avenue in Fort Lauderdale's Flagler Village, sitting in a federally designated Opportunity Zone. Spanning about 350,000 square feet on 1.46 acres and capitalized at roughly $100 million, it mixes market-rate and workforce studio through three-bedroom units with structured parking, a fitness center, resort-style pool and coworking space. CEDARst held its groundbreaking on April 28, 2026, with construction starting in May 2026 and completion targeted for mid-2028.

StatusHistory.note: CEDARst Companies unveiled Flats Flagler Gateway, a 215-unit Opportunity Zone multifamily tower in Fort Lauderdale's Flagler Village.

Title: Soren
Description: A downtown Salt Lake City mixed-use development anchored by Soren, a 28-story, 367-unit residential tower paired with a 10-story, 209-room hotel, with site excavation underway since early 2026.
DescriptionLong: Soren is the residential centerpiece of Domain Companies' mixed-use development at 370 South West Temple, at the corner of West Temple and 400 South in downtown Salt Lake City. Designed by Solomon Cordwell Buenz, the 278-foot, 28-story tower will hold 367 apartments above roughly 6,400 square feet of retail, alongside a separate 129-foot, 10-story hotel of 209 rooms with a rooftop bar and ground-floor restaurant. Estimated costs run about $164 million for the tower and $109 million for the hotel. Excavation and site-preparation work got underway in February 2026, with more extensive excavation to follow pending permit approval; completion is anticipated in late 2028 or early 2029.

StatusHistory.note: Domain Companies unveiled a mixed-use residential tower + hotel at 370 South West Temple in downtown Salt Lake City.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: 367-unit residential tower + separate 209-key hotel + ground-floor retail per Building Salt Lake.

Title: 711 North Pearl
Description: A 35-story, 370-unit luxury apartment tower under construction on the edge of the Dallas Arts District, developed by Switzerland's Empira Group with delivery targeted for 2026.
DescriptionLong: 711 North Pearl is a 35-story residential tower rising at 711 North Pearl Street on the edge of the Dallas Arts District and Central Business District. Developed by Switzerland-based Empira Group and designed by GFF Architects, the tower delivers 370 market-rate apartments — studios through three-bedrooms, each with a private balcony — plus eight exclusive penthouses, about 9,000 square feet of ground-floor retail and a seven-level, 515-space parking garage. A ninth-floor amenity level offers a pool with city views, a fitness center, co-working space, an art makers studio and a resident lounge, with a landscaped streetscape connecting to nearby cultural venues and Klyde Warren Park. The tower broke ground in 2024 with delivery targeted for 2026.

StatusHistory.note: Swiss firm Empira Group unveiled a 35-story, 370-unit residential tower at 711 N. Pearl St. in downtown Dallas.
Located in the downtown Dallas core near the Arts District.

Title: Four Seasons Hotel & Residences Charleston
Description: South Carolina's first Four Seasons — a three-building development in Charleston's Historic District replacing a former Days Inn, with a 139-room hotel and 36 branded residences, targeted for 2028.
DescriptionLong: The Four Seasons Hotel & Residences Charleston will be South Carolina's first Four Seasons property, rising on a 1.9-acre former Days Inn site at 155 Meeting Street in Charleston's Historic District, near Horlbeck Alley and the City Market. Developed by Tampa-based Strategic Property Partners, the project comprises three buildings reaching as high as eight stories, designed by Morris Adjmi Architects (who also lead the residences' interiors), with hotel interiors by Lark Design Studios and landscape architecture by Hollander Design. The 139-room hotel will offer four restaurants and bars, an outdoor pool with private cabanas, a full-service spa, a fitness center, curated retail and 7,000 square feet of event space. Alongside it, 36 branded residences range from two to five bedrooms (roughly 2,000–3,700 square feet), priced from $5 million, with Four Seasons services including in-residence dining and concierge. Completion is targeted for 2028.

StatusHistory.note: Four Seasons and Strategic Property Partners unveiled a new-build hotel (139 keys) + 36 branded residences in Charleston's historic district.

Title: The Lily River Oaks
Description: A $270M mixed-use development in Upper Kirby anchored by The Lily River Oaks, a 38-story, 331-residence luxury rental tower paired with a 10-story trophy office building, which broke ground April 2026.
DescriptionLong: The Lily River Oaks is a 38-story luxury rental tower at the heart of Southern Land Company's $270 million mixed-use development at 2811 Kirby Drive in Houston's Upper Kirby district, near River Oaks. Designed by Solomon Cordwell Buenz, The Lily delivers 331 rental residences — including 18 penthouses — with rooftop indoor and outdoor amenity spaces, a seventh-floor garden deck, a residential spa and guest suites. It is connected by a shared six-level parking podium to 2811 Kirby, a 10-story building with roughly 107,000 square feet of Class AA office space, alongside about 15,000 square feet of ground-level restaurant and retail space. The 953,000-square-foot project broke ground on April 15, 2026, with Southern Land targeting record office and apartment rents, and completion is slated for 2028.

StatusHistory.note: Southern Land Co. broke ground on The Lily, a 38-story, 331-unit tower paired with a 10-story office building on Kirby Drive in Upper Kirby.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: 331-unit residential high-rise + 10-story Class AA office + ground-floor restaurant/retail on a shared podium.

Title: First & Island
Description: A planned 35-story, 215-residence luxury condominium tower by Bosa Development on one of the last empty lots in downtown San Diego's Marina District, just south of Horizons Towers.
DescriptionLong: First & Island is a planned 35-story luxury condominium tower, rising roughly 415 feet with 215 residences, on a long-vacant parking-lot parcel at the corner of First Avenue and Island Avenue in downtown San Diego's Marina District, immediately south of Horizons Towers. Developed by Bosa Development and designed by Vancouver-based James KM Cheng Architects, the roughly 273,000-square-foot project sits at the convergence of the Marina District, East Village, Little Italy and Cortez Hill. Sales and permitting activity advanced through late 2025, with construction anticipated to begin in the 2026–2027 window and delivery targeted around 2027; amenities, floor plans and pricing have not yet been released.

StatusHistory.note: Bosa's First & Island, a 35-story downtown San Diego tower, advanced through permitting; later repositioned from for-sale condos to ~211 rental apartments.

Title: The Launiu Ward Village
Description: A 40-story, 486-residence luxury tower that broke ground in October 2025 as the 11th tower in Howard Hughes' Ward Village, with front-row views over Kewalo Basin and Diamond Head.
DescriptionLong: The Launiu Ward Village is a 40-story luxury condominium tower, plus a six-story podium, that broke ground on October 21, 2025 at 928 Ward Avenue near the corner of Ala Moana Boulevard in Honolulu's Kakaako district. The 11th tower in Howard Hughes' master-planned Ward Village, it delivers 486 market-rate residences ranging from studios to three-bedrooms, roughly 440 to 1,677 square feet, with views over Kewalo Basin, the Pacific coastline, Diamond Head and the Koolau mountains. Designed by Miami-based Arquitectonica with interiors by ODADA and landscape architecture by VITA, the tower features approximately 78,000 square feet of resort-style amenities.

StatusHistory.note: Howard Hughes broke ground on The Launiu, Ward Village's 11th residential tower (40 stories, 486 units) in Kakaako.
Within Howard Hughes' Ward Village master-planned community in Kakaako.

Title: Apiary Hotel & Residences
Description: South Denver's debut extended-stay lifestyle hotel, a 175-suite Marriott Bonvoy property in Belleview Station paired with luxury Apiary Residences, opened in January 2026.
DescriptionLong: Apiary Hotel & Residences is a beehive-inspired, mixed-use hospitality property in Denver's Belleview Station district near the Denver Tech Center, which opened to guests on January 27, 2026. Part of the Marriott Bonvoy portfolio, the all-suite hotel offers 175 spacious suites with kitchenettes as South Denver's first extended-stay lifestyle hotel. Alongside it, Apiary Residences provides one- to three-bedroom homes — including thirteen penthouses — with a separate entrance and amenities such as co-working spaces, meeting rooms, a residential lounge and spa-caliber wellness areas. Community-facing spaces include two dining concepts, Keepers Cocktail Lounge and June Gap Market & Café, plus the 900-square-foot Nectar Room meeting space, with light-rail access to downtown. The project was developed by Stonebridge and Copford Capital Management with The Beck Group as architect and general contractor.

Title: Chapter SLC
Description: A 252-unit, 696-bed student housing development that broke ground April 2026 on the former OfficeMax site near the University of Utah, with completion targeted for June 2028.
DescriptionLong: Chapter SLC is a 252-unit, 696-bed student housing development that broke ground on April 9, 2026 at 900 East 400 South in Salt Lake City's East Central neighborhood, on the former OfficeMax site near the University of Utah. Developed by Cole West and CRG and designed by Lamar Johnson Collaborative, the project rises five wood-frame residential stories over 2.5 concrete parking levels, with a unit mix of 41 studios, 21 one-bedrooms, 64 two-bedrooms and 126 four-bedrooms, plus ground-floor commercial space, 170 parking stalls and bicycle parking. Amenities are pitched at students, including a soda shop, ski simulator, Pilates studio, library, jumbotron and rooftop pool. Completion is expected in June 2028.

StatusHistory.note: CRG and Cole West unveiled Chapter SLC, a student-focused apartment community at 410 S. 900 East near the University of Utah.

Title: Andia by Bosa
Description: A 40-story downtown San Diego condominium tower by Bosa Development — the city's first new for-sale luxury high-rise since 2019, with nearly 400 residences now selling.
DescriptionLong: Andia by Bosa is a 40-story luxury condominium tower planned for 1160 9th Avenue at the edge of downtown San Diego's Cortez Hill, Core District and East Village. Developed by Bosa Development, it marks the first new for-sale luxury high-rise in downtown San Diego since 2019, offering close to 400 residences in a mix of one-, two- and three-bedroom layouts emphasizing light, airy interiors and flexible floor plans. Sales are underway, with the project positioned as a more attainable entry into Bosa's downtown portfolio.

StatusHistory.note: Bosa Development converted its full downtown San Diego block to for-sale condos, launching Andia (~399 residences, 40 stories).
Bosa Development converted its full downtown San Diego block to for-sale condos, launching Andia (~399 residences, 40 stories).
Andia is under construction (started 2024) with a public sales gallery open since June 2025 — record still showed "announced".

Title: Halsted Pointe
Description: A 46-story, 460-unit mixed-use tower under construction on Goose Island's southeastern tip, the first phase of Onni's Halsted Pointe redevelopment of a former Greyhound bus facility.
DescriptionLong: Halsted Pointe is a 46-story, roughly 500-foot mixed-use residential tower under construction at 931 North Halsted Street on the southeastern tip of Goose Island in Chicago. The first phase of a larger Onni redevelopment of a former Greyhound bus maintenance facility, the tower will hold 460 residential units across one-, two-, three- and four-bedroom layouts, with 92 designated affordable. A five-story podium contains roughly 18,900 square feet of commercial retail space and 200 parking spaces, with amenities including a fitness center, wellness space, lounge and game room. The dual-tone glass tower features cantilevered and inset balconies. Designed by Hartshorne Plunkard Architecture, construction began in April 2026 on an approximately 24-month timeline.

StatusHistory.note: Onni Group began construction (caisson drilling) on the 46-story first tower of Halsted Pointe on Goose Island.

Title: 566 West Van Buren
Description: A 12-story, 199-unit apartment building rising on a former parking lot in Chicago's West Loop, with vertical construction underway and delivery targeted for early 2027.
DescriptionLong: 566 West Van Buren is a 12-story, 199-unit apartment building rising on a former surface parking lot in Chicago's West Loop, at the edge of Greektown. Developed by Riverside Investment & Development and designed by Antunovich Associates, the project includes 42 parking spaces and ground-level amenities. As of June 2026 the foundation is complete, the tower crane is installed and concrete columns are forming, marking the start of vertical construction. Delivery is targeted for early 2027.

StatusHistory.note: Riverside Investment & Development began construction on the 199-unit, 12-story tower in Chicago's West Loop after permits were issued.
566 West Van Buren completed caissons and foundation and planted its tower crane.

Title: Manor Riverwalk District
Description: A 24-story luxury tower with 369 residences on the west bank of the Hillsborough River — Phase 1 of a six-acre, five-building master plan featuring a nearly half-acre elevated amenity deck, a 3,000-square-foot wellness program with infrared sauna and cold plunge recovery suite, and approximately 18,000 square feet of ground-floor retail along a planned woonerf.
DescriptionLong: Manor Riverwalk District is the first phase of a six-acre, four-parcel mixed-use master plan reshaping the west bank of Tampa's Hillsborough River. The 24-story Phase 1 tower delivers 369 luxury residences, anchored by a nearly half-acre elevated amenity deck — one of the largest private outdoor amenity offerings in the Tampa Bay multifamily market. The wellness program spans nearly 3,000 square feet of fitness and recovery space, anchored by a 750-square-foot recovery suite with an infrared sauna, cold plunge, and spa-caliber treatment room. At street level, the project reimagines South Parker Street as a woonerf — a pedestrian-priority shared street activating approximately 18,000 square feet of ground-floor retail and creating a continuous pedestrian connection between downtown Tampa, Hyde Park, the University of Tampa, and the Tampa Riverwalk. The broader six-acre master plan ultimately carries five buildings and more than 1,000 residences across market-rate rentals, for-sale condominiums, and a boutique residential building, alongside a parking structure with more than 800 spaces, up to ten boat slips, a kayak launch, and a Riverwalk underpass tying the district into the West Riverwalk Expansion.

StatusHistory.note: Related Group breaks ground on Manor Riverwalk District, the 369-unit Phase 1 of a six-acre, $1 billion master plan on Tampa's west riverbank.
Announced/assembled: Related Group acquires 6-acre Hillsborough riverfront assemblage (~$55.5M) for a downtown Tampa mixed-use district, ~1,079 units (Florida YIMBY).

Title: The55
Description: A six-story, 140-unit residential and mixed-use building on La Cienega Boulevard — set to become the largest residential property in Beverly Hills, with 13,300 square feet of ground-floor retail and restaurant space, a rooftop pool deck, theater, conference facilities, private workspaces, lounge, saunas, and a shared kitchen.
DescriptionLong: The55 is a six-story, 140-unit residential and mixed-use development planned for 55 North La Cienega Boulevard in Beverly Hills — the largest residential property in the city by unit count once delivered. The 297,770-square-foot building pairs 118 luxury market-rate residences with 22 income-restricted units, secured through California's state density bonus provisions that expanded the project from an earlier 105-unit concept. Ground level carries 13,300 square feet of commercial space across retail and restaurant uses, with a rooftop amenity program that includes a pool deck, theater, conference facilities, private workspaces, communal gathering areas, restaurant, coffee shop, lounge, saunas, and a shared kitchen. The project replaces a long-vacant restaurant property and is the result of more than a decade of evolving entitlement work that walked the proposal through multiple iterations — including an earlier hotel concept — before landing on the final residential-led program.

StatusHistory.note: The55, a 140-unit residential project with ground-floor retail at 55 N La Cienega Blvd, advances toward construction in Beverly Hills.
Secures $85M construction loan via Marcus & Millichap as the project moves into construction.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: 140 residences over ~13,300 sq ft of ground-floor retail/restaurant space.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: 140 residences over ~13,300 sq ft of ground-floor retail/restaurant space.

Title: Alton Delray
Description: A 386-unit Class A apartment community along Delray Beach's South Congress Avenue corridor, with five buildings totaling 542,078 square feet anchored by a two-story clubhouse — 154 of the 386 units reserved as workforce housing under Florida's Live Local Act.
DescriptionLong: Alton Delray is a 386-unit Class A apartment community rising on a former industrial site along Delray Beach's South Congress Avenue corridor, west of I-95 with direct access to Atlantic Avenue and broader South Florida. The project spans five residential buildings — three six-story and two five-story — totaling 542,078 square feet across a single campus, anchored by an 8,348-square-foot, two-story clubhouse and 1,748 square feet of ground-floor commercial space. The unit mix splits between 232 market-rate apartments and 154 workforce-housing apartments reserved under Florida's Live Local Act, where rents are pegged below market. The development drops into one of Palm Beach County's most active rental pipelines, joining a wave of multifamily projects along the same Congress Avenue corridor that have brought more than 1,300 new units to the submarket inside two years.

StatusHistory.note: Kolter closes a $92M construction loan from Madison Realty Capital and kicks off construction.
Confirmed single-use Residences: 386-unit multifamily community (amenities only, no other primary use).

Title: 725 Randolph
Description: A 1-million-square-foot, 41-story 'lifestyle office' high-rise at the gateway to Chicago's Restaurant Row in Fulton Market — Kohn Pedersen Fox's stepped tower combines premium office floor plates with a flagship Equinox Fitness Club and Spa, 17,000 square feet of restaurant space, a podium rooftop pool, an 11,500-square-foot public plaza, and private terraces and loggias on multiple floors.
DescriptionLong: 725 Randolph is a 1-million-square-foot, 41-story office high-rise in Chicago's Fulton Market district, positioned as Related Midwest's flagship lifestyle office building and one of the city's largest new commercial projects of the post-pandemic era. Designed by Kohn Pedersen Fox under design principal Trent Tesch, the tower's stepped form references the iconic high-rises of the Downtown Loop while its industrial-style facade — black metal fins and extruded frames — draws inspiration from the site's meatpacking history. The amenity program centers on a podium rooftop pool, a flagship Equinox Fitness Club and Spa, 17,000 square feet of restaurant space, and private terraces and loggias on multiple floors offering outdoor work settings with unobstructed views across the West Loop. At ground level, an 11,500-square-foot public plaza opens onto Randolph Street and integrates with the Fulton Market streetscape, with ample bike parking and direct access to Chicago's Green and Pink CTA lines a block away and the Kennedy Expressway adjacent.

StatusHistory.note: Related Midwest reveals a redesigned 725 W Randolph as a roughly 1M-sq-ft KPF office tower in Fulton Market.
Related Midwest reveals a redesigned 725 W Randolph as a roughly 1M-sq-ft KPF office tower in Fulton Market.
Sidley Austin signs as anchor tenant, taking more than half the office space and clearing the way to break ground.
Confirmed single-use Office: ~1M sq ft KPF office tower (ground-floor retail and gym are amenities).

Title: Mandarin Oriental Esencia, Puerto Rico
Description: Mandarin Oriental's debut on Puerto Rico's southwestern coast — a 2,000-acre Esencia development with 106 guest rooms, 83 villas, and ~200 branded residences, plus an 18-hole Rees Jones golf course. Opening 2028.
DescriptionLong: Mandarin Oriental Esencia, Puerto Rico is the brand's debut on the island, a luxury resort and residences slated for 2028 on Boquerón Bay in Cabo Rojo. The Residences feature 83 exclusive villas and approximately 200 private branded residences across a six-story boutique building. The resort itself adds 106 guest rooms and suites, some with private gardens, terraces, or plunge pools. Designed by Studio MK27 with master planning by Álvarez Díaz & Villalón and EDSA, the project sits within Esencia — a 2,000-acre cosmopolitan coastal community by Reuben Brothers and Three Rules Capital with three miles of beaches. Amenities include five distinctive dining venues, a beach club, the Spa at Mandarin Oriental, an 18-hole Rees Jones-designed golf course, a children's club, and an outdoor adventure center.
Architect: Studio MK27, Álvarez Díaz & Villalón, EDSA
Developer: Reuben Brothers, Three Rules Capital, Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group

StatusHistory.note: Mandarin Oriental to debut in Puerto Rico with a resort and residences at Esencia, Boquerón Bay.
Re-classified to Resort: Mandarin Oriental resort with branded residences, spa, and golf at Esencia.
Re-classified to Resort: Mandarin Oriental resort with branded residences, spa, and golf at Esencia.

Title: NoMad Singapore
Description: Hilton's first NoMad Hotel in Asia — a 173-room luxury lifestyle hotel by WOHA on Singapore's Orchard Road, joining UOL Group's Orchard Road portfolio. Opening early 2027.
DescriptionLong: NoMad Singapore marks Hilton's first NoMad Hotels property in the Asia-Pacific region, scheduled to debut in early 2027. The 173-room luxury lifestyle hotel is being developed in partnership with UOL Group, a prominent Singapore-based property and hospitality company. Architecture is by WOHA, the renowned Singaporean firm known for sustainable and innovative designs. NoMad Singapore will sit on Orchard Road — Singapore's premier shopping and entertainment district — and forms part of UOL Group's broader Orchard Road development plans alongside the upcoming UPPERHOUSE luxury residences and the Pan Pacific Orchard hotel. The hotel will participate in Hilton Honors.

StatusHistory.note: UOL and Hilton to open NoMad's first Asia-Pacific hotel on Orchard Road, redeveloping Faber House.
UOL and Hilton to open NoMad's first Asia-Pacific hotel on Orchard Road, redeveloping Faber House.

Title: Kempinski Grace Bay
Description: Europe's oldest luxury hotel brand makes its Caribbean debut — an 11-acre beachfront resort and residential project on Turks & Caicos's Grace Bay with 119 residences and 4 oceanfront villas.
DescriptionLong: Kempinski Grace Bay marks Europe's oldest luxury hotel group's debut in the Turks and Caicos Islands — a low-density 11-acre beachfront resort and residential project on Grace Bay. Developed by JTRE (the global real estate firm behind numerous European projects), the property features 119 private residences and 4 oceanfront villas arranged around lush courtyards. Residences range from studios overlooking the ocean or golf course to one-, two-, and three-bedroom layouts, with expansive five-bedroom suites and six-bedroom oceanfront villas. Amenities include two signature restaurants, a European-style spa and ocean-view fitness center, a 3,000-square-foot wine cellar, boutique retail, padel and pickleball courts, and a dedicated children's club. Construction began with November 2025 groundbreaking; completion anticipated early 2028.

StatusHistory.note: Kempinski and developer JTRE unveil Turks & Caicos's first Kempinski resort and branded residences on Grace Bay Beach.
Kempinski and developer JTRE unveil Turks & Caicos's first Kempinski resort and branded residences on Grace Bay Beach.
Groundbreaking ceremony held on Grace Bay Beach with the TCI Premier; construction begins.
Re-classified to Resort: Kempinski-branded beachfront resort with branded residences and villas (Resort is the umbrella category).
Re-classified to Resort: Kempinski-branded beachfront resort with branded residences and villas (Resort is the umbrella category).

Title: Faraway Jackson Hole
Description: Faraway Hotels' first alpine property — a transformation of the historic Snake River Lodge & Spa at the base of Teton Village, with Surehand, a restaurant-lounge-cocktail club anchor. Opening summer 2026.
DescriptionLong: Faraway Jackson Hole marks Faraway Hotels' first move away from the coast — a project at the base of Teton Village transforming the historic Snake River Lodge & Spa into an alpine retreat. The reimagined property blends Wyoming heritage with the brand's narrative-driven ethos, with new guestrooms and interiors marked by warmth and texture alongside inviting communal areas. Surehand — a restaurant, lounge, and cocktail club — anchors the retreat with a mix of conviviality and mountain spirit. Faraway is developed by Blue Flag Capital with Collared Martin Hospitality and Bain Capital Real Estate, and managed by Collared Martin Hospitality. Opening planned for summer 2026.
Developer: Blue Flag Capital, Bain Capital Real Estate, Collared Martin Hospitality

StatusHistory.note: Faraway Hotels to debut in Teton Village, rebranding the historic Snake River Lodge & Spa, opening summer 2026.
Faraway Hotels to debut in Teton Village, rebranding the historic Snake River Lodge & Spa, opening summer 2026.
Faraway Hotels to debut in Teton Village, rebranding the historic Snake River Lodge & Spa, opening summer 2026.
Re-classified to Resort: Teton Village mountain resort (Faraway brand) with spa, dining, and residences.
Re-classified to Resort: Teton Village mountain resort (Faraway brand) with spa, dining, and residences.

Title: Faraway Sag Harbor
Description: Faraway Hotels' first Hamptons property — a 67-room reimagining of the historic Baron's Cove in Sag Harbor, with Zagara, an Amalfi-inspired flagship restaurant. Opening summer 2026.
DescriptionLong: Faraway Sag Harbor reimagines the historic Baron's Cove as Faraway Hotels' first Hamptons property, opening summer 2026. The 67-room property takes shape as an elegant compound layered with character, evoking the feeling of a place collected over time — with intimate gardens, sunlit patios, and poolside spaces designed for quiet moments as well as social gatherings. At the center of the experience is Zagara, a flagship restaurant inspired by Amalfi coastal living that shifts seamlessly from day to night. Faraway is developed by Blue Flag Capital with Collared Martin Hospitality and Bain Capital Real Estate, and managed by Collared Martin Hospitality.
Developer: Blue Flag Capital, Bain Capital Real Estate, Collared Martin Hospitality

StatusHistory.note: Faraway Hotels to open in Sag Harbor, transforming the historic Baron's Cove; the brand's first Hamptons location, opening summer 2026.

Title: Equinox Resort Anguilla Port Nimara
Description: Equinox Hotels' first Caribbean resort — anchoring the new Port Nimara waterfront development on Anguilla's southwest coast with 62 rooms, 36 villas, 35 branded residences, and a 118-berth superyacht marina.
DescriptionLong: Equinox Resort Anguilla Port Nimara marks Equinox Hotels' first Caribbean resort and second integrated marina destination globally. The project anchors Port Nimara, a new waterfront development on Anguilla's southwest coast between Cap Juluca and Altamer. The resort includes 62 guest rooms, 18 oceanfront villas, 18 upland villas, and 35 branded Equinox residences, alongside a 118-berth superyacht marina designated as a port of entry with on-site customs and immigration clearance. Amenities include an Equinox Club, an all-day dining concept centered on health-driven cuisine, The Spa by Equinox Hotels, and curated retail. The broader Port Nimara development is delivered by South West Harbour Inc., a joint venture between SF Investments Ltd., Time Equities Inc., and Antillean Developments Inc. Marina opens to first vessels in 2027; resort expected 2029.
Developer: South West Harbour Inc., SF Investments Ltd., Time Equities Inc., Antillean Developments Inc.

StatusHistory.note: Equinox Hotels announces its first Caribbean resort, anchored by a luxury marina at Port Nimara, Anguilla.
Equinox Hotels announces its first Caribbean resort, anchored by a luxury marina at Port Nimara, Anguilla.
Re-classified to Resort: Equinox Caribbean resort with branded residences and a luxury marina.
Re-classified to Resort: Equinox Caribbean resort with branded residences and a luxury marina.

Title: Nikki Beach Resort & Spa Antigua
Description: The first Nikki Beach resort and branded residences in the Western Hemisphere — a $400M development on Antigua's Jolly Beach with 84 hotel rooms, 134 turnkey residences, and 7 four-bedroom beachfront villas.
DescriptionLong: Nikki Beach Resort & Spa Antigua is the first Nikki Beach resort and branded residences in the Western Hemisphere — a $400 million development on Jolly Beach. The project features 84 hotel rooms and suites alongside 134 turnkey residences (studios to three-bedroom) and 7 four-bedroom beachfront villas, all integrated with a full-service beach club, poolscape, dining venues, and wellness sanctuaries. Architecture by HKS Architects with interiors by ROAM Interior Design. Amenities include Cafe Nikki, Escape Beach Restaurant, beachfront pools, sunset lounges, and an 11,000 sq ft Nikki Spa + Tone Gym. Sales begin early 2026; resort opening planned for 2030. Branded residences start around $1.2M with villas from $5.5M.

StatusHistory.note: Ayre Group and Nikki Beach partner to build a resort, spa, beach club, and residences at Jolly Beach, Antigua.
Ayre Group and Nikki Beach partner to build a resort, spa, beach club, and residences at Jolly Beach, Antigua.
Ayre Group and Nikki Beach partner to build a resort, spa, beach club, and residences at Jolly Beach, Antigua.

Title: The Stockman, Auberge Collection
Description: Steamboat Springs' first ultra-luxury slopeside resort — a 9-story Auberge Collection development with 59 hotel rooms and 95 private residences at the base of Steamboat Resort.
DescriptionLong: The Stockman, Auberge Collection is Steamboat Springs' first true ultra-luxury ski-in, ski-out resort and residences — a nine-story slopeside development at the base of Steamboat Resort with 59 hotel rooms and 95 private residences. Designed by OZ Architecture with interiors by Nunzio Marc DeSantis Architects, the property blends contemporary mountain design with a refined interpretation of Steamboat's Western heritage. Residences range from one- to five-bedroom homes including 13 rooftop "Barn" residences with double-height ceilings. Amenities include three culinary venues, a full-service Wellbeing by Auberge spa with thermal bathing, ski-butler service, and slope-side après-ski spaces. Groundbreaking is set for 2026 with a planned 2030 debut.
Architect: OZ Architecture, Nunzio Marc DeSantis Architects

StatusHistory.note: Auberge Collection to open The Stockman, a ski-in/ski-out resort and residences in Steamboat Springs, opening 2030.
Auberge Collection to open The Stockman, a ski-in/ski-out resort and residences in Steamboat Springs, opening 2030.
Re-classified to Resort: Auberge ski-in/ski-out resort with branded residences and spa.
Re-classified to Resort: Auberge ski-in/ski-out resort with branded residences and spa.

Title: Janu Residences Dubai
Description: Aman Group's first Janu hotel and branded residences in the Middle East — a Herzog & de Meuron-designed tower with a 150-key hotel, the world's first Janu Club, and 57 branded residences in the heart of Dubai's DIFC.
DescriptionLong: Janu Residences Dubai marks Aman Group's first residential offering in the Middle East and the second Janu destination globally (after Janu Tokyo 2024). Designed by Pritzker Prize-winning architects Herzog & de Meuron, the sculptural tower in Dubai International Financial Centre integrates a 150-key Janu hotel, the world's first global Janu Club, and 57 branded residences (two- to five-bedroom homes plus a signature penthouse). The tower rises from a reimagined souk-style courtyard anchored by a 59-foot-high lobby and overlooks Zabeel Palace Gardens with sweeping views toward the Burj Khalifa. The entire 34th floor is reserved exclusively for residents, with an infinity-edge pool and private lounge.

StatusHistory.note: H&H and Aman Group unveil Janu Residences Dubai at DIFC, designed by Herzog & de Meuron.
H&H and Aman Group unveil Janu Residences Dubai at DIFC, designed by Herzog & de Meuron.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: DIFC tower combining a Janu hotel, residences, members' club, offices, and retail.

Title: Rosewood Blue Palace
Description: Rosewood's debut hotel in Greece — a redesigned 154-room luxury resort overlooking the Gulf of Elounda on Crete's northeast coastline.
DescriptionLong:
Rosewood Blue Palace is the full-scale transformation of one of Crete's most iconic waterfront resorts into Rosewood's first Greek property, opening mid-2026. The 154-room redesigned resort rises along Crete's northeast coastline between Plaka and Elounda on a hillside overlooking the Gulf of Elounda with direct views of Spinalonga Island. Owned by Phāea and redesigned by Athens-based K-Studio with a refined residential feel rooted in Cretan materials and craftsmanship. 85 rooms feature private pools; suites and villas span the remainder. Three outdoor pools, a private beach, six distinct dining and bar concepts (interiors by London-based Afroditi Krassa), and the Asaya spa anchor the experience.</description_long>
<parameter name="types">["Hotel", "Resort"]

StatusHistory.note: Rosewood announces its Greece debut, reimagining the Blue Palace resort in Elounda, Crete.
Re-classified to Resort: Rosewood-branded Crete resort with branded residences and spa.
Re-classified to Resort: Rosewood-branded Crete resort with branded residences and spa.

Title: Janu Turks & Caicos
Description: Aman Group's first Caribbean property — Janu's fifth destination, a beachfront hotel and branded private residences on the northwest edge of Providenciales bordering the 18,000-acre Northwest Point Marine National Park.
DescriptionLong: Janu Turks & Caicos marks Aman Group's first Caribbean project and the fifth destination for the contemporary sister brand to Aman (after Janu Tokyo's 2024 debut and Janu Dubai). Set on the northwest edge of Providenciales bordering Northwest Point Marine National Park — an 18,000-acre protected expanse known for white-sand beaches, rocky coves, and rich marine ecosystems. The development pairs a beachfront Janu hotel with a collection of branded private residences, with open-air architecture and organic materials emphasizing connection to the surroundings. Amenities include a Beach Club, several culinary venues, a Spa & Wellness Center, and a 50-meter swimming pool — all designed around Janu's social wellness ethos. Future Janu destinations confirmed in AlUla, Diriyah, and Montenegro.

StatusHistory.note: Aman Group announces Janu Turks & Caicos, the brand's debut in the Americas, on Providenciales' northwest coast.
Re-classified to Resort: Janu (Aman) beachfront resort with serviced residences on Providenciales.
Re-classified to Resort: Janu (Aman) beachfront resort with serviced residences on Providenciales.

Title: Limelight Charleston
Description: Aspen One's first East Coast Limelight Hotel — a 2028 boutique hotel and private branded residences in Charleston's Historic District, by Bittoni Architects.
DescriptionLong: Limelight Charleston marks Aspen One's first East Coast Limelight Hotel, set to debut in 2028 in Charleston's Historic District. The project pairs a boutique hotel with private branded residences and immersive social spaces — including a rooftop pool, restaurant, and the signature Limelight "living room" designed as a gathering space for guests and locals. Developed in partnership with Category Company. The expansion builds on Limelight's growth from three open properties in early 2023 to seven open or in-development locations, including recent launches in Snowmass, Denver, and Ketchum and upcoming openings in Boulder and Mammoth.

StatusHistory.note: Aspen One announces Limelight Charleston, its first East Coast hotel, with hotel rooms and private residences on King Street, opening 2028.
Aspen One announces Limelight Charleston, its first East Coast hotel, with hotel rooms and private residences on King Street, opening 2028.

Title: Velara Resort & Residences
Description: Costa Rica's first biologically-optimized resort and residences — a 17-acre development on the Nicoya Peninsula's Blue Zone with 52 keys, 44 condos, 32 villas, and the 25,000 sq ft Raíz Wellbeing Center.
DescriptionLong: Velara Resort & Residences introduces Costa Rica's first biologically-optimized resort — a 17-acre development along the remote coastline of Playa Azul in Costa Rica's Nicoya Peninsula Blue Zone. The only high-rise residential property permitted in the region. Features 44 ocean-view condominiums (including four penthouses), 32 standalone villas, and a 52-key resort. The on-site Raíz Wellbeing Center spans 25,000 square feet with advanced diagnostics, regenerative therapies, biometric optimization, recovery treatments, sleep-enhancing environments, and guided health programs. Each residence connects to Velara's harmonization platform integrating wearable data and environmental sensors. Two culinary concepts built around nutritional optimization, plus Marea Beach Club with direct Pacific access. Phase 1 (residences + resort) completes Q2 2026; final delivery early 2027.

StatusHistory.note: Vocabulary cleanup: removing the 'Spa' tag from the TMW type registry (spas are an amenity, not a primary use). Stripping Spa from types[] across all affected projects.

Title: One&Only Hudson Valley
Description: One&Only's second U.S. resort and first in the Northeast — a 21-acre 2028 retreat in Hyde Park's Hudson Valley with 61 accommodations, a CIA-collaborated culinary program, and a Longevity Hub by Clinique La Prairie.
DescriptionLong: One&Only Hudson Valley marks the brand's first property in the Northeast U.S. and second confirmed U.S. resort — set to open 2028 across 21 acres in Hyde Park, just steps from the Culinary Institute of America (CIA). Developed by Nolan Reynolds International in partnership with Kerzner International and the CIA. The resort features 61 accommodations across freestanding cabins, guest rooms, and suites, alongside a curated collection of private homes. Signature dining experiences will be crafted in collaboration with the CIA, offering guests immersive opportunities in food and flavor. The resort will also host a Longevity Hub by Clinique La Prairie, integrating science-backed wellness treatments and holistic health journeys into the guest experience. Follows One&Only Moonlight Basin (Big Sky, Montana) — the brand's first U.S. resort.
Developer: Nolan Reynolds International, Kerzner International, Culinary Institute of America

StatusHistory.note: Nolan Reynolds, Kerzner, and the Culinary Institute of America to develop One&Only Hudson Valley resort and private homes in Hyde Park.
Nolan Reynolds, Kerzner, and the Culinary Institute of America to develop One&Only Hudson Valley resort and private homes in Hyde Park.
Re-classified to Resort: One&Only resort with private homes and a longevity/wellness hub in Hyde Park.
Re-classified to Resort: One&Only resort with private homes and a longevity/wellness hub in Hyde Park.

Title: Casa Cipriani Miami Beach
Description: Casa Cipriani's first Florida location — a 17-story oceanfront tower on Collins Avenue with 40 hotel suites, 23 private residences (entry-level $25M+), and a members-only club.
DescriptionLong: Casa Cipriani Miami Beach marks the celebrated hospitality brand's first Florida location and its third globally after Milan and New York — a 17-story oceanfront development at 3611 Collins Avenue. The tower features 40 luxurious hotel suites, 23 private residences (entry-level pricing estimated at ~$25 million), and a members-only club designed for ultra-discerning clientele. Architect Brandon Haw delivers a contemporary interpretation of Miami Beach's iconic Art Deco style. Developed by 13th Floor Investments (Miami) and Midtown Equities (New York) in partnership with the Cipriani family, whose legacy traces back to Giuseppe Cipriani's 1931 founding of Venice's Harry's Bar. Sales were expected to commence in 2025, followed by groundbreaking.

StatusHistory.note: 13th Floor Investments and Midtown Equities reveal Casa Cipriani Miami Beach, with residences, an all-suite hotel, and a private members' club.

Title: The Hoxton Nashville
Description: The Hoxton's first Tennessee debut — a ground-up 200+ room boutique hotel in Pie Town from Ennismore, opening 2027 with four distinct food & beverage venues plus a rooftop pool.
DescriptionLong: The Hoxton Nashville marks the boutique British brand's sixth U.S. location and first in Tennessee — a ground-up development at 616 Ewing Street in the emerging Pie Town neighborhood. The 200+ room hotel is designed by Chicago-based GREC Architects in collaboration with Ennismore's AIME Studios, with four distinct dining venues: a restaurant, bar, and coffee shop integrated into the open-plan lobby, plus a rooftop poolside restaurant and bar with downtown views. The lower level features a speakeasy with late-night cocktails. The Apartment — a collection of private rooms around a communal kitchen — supports events and intimate gatherings. Developed in partnership with local investors Bryan Fort and Taylor Preston, who have spent nearly a decade assembling properties across Pie Town. The Hoxton is part of Ennismore's global hospitality portfolio. Anticipated 2027 debut.

StatusHistory.note: Announced: Ennismore signs The Hoxton, Nashville (Pie Town district; ~200+ keys; dev partner Magellan), 2027 opening targeted (Hospitality Net).

Title: Hatai Bangkok
Description: Heatherwick Studio's first Thailand project — a landmark mixed-use development inspired by the warm glow of traditional Thai lanterns, housing the reimagined Narai Hotel (Unbound Collection by Hyatt) and Six Senses Bangkok, opening 2028.
DescriptionLong: Hatai is Heatherwick Studio's first project in Thailand — a landmark mixed-use development in Bangkok's Silom district inspired by the warm glow of traditional Thai lanterns. The project houses two hotels: the reimagined 250-room Narai Hotel (debuting in 2028 as part of The Unbound Collection by Hyatt) and Six Senses Bangkok. Alongside the hotels, 56,000 square feet of public space includes a green canopied plaza, restored canal, elevated walkways, and gathering spaces such as a shrine, open-air market, and event venues. Designed to achieve LEED and WELL Gold certification, the project blends heritage and contemporary design with a strong sustainability focus. The Narai Hotel will feature all-day dining, rooftop dining, lounge, pool, fitness center, and event spaces. Located near Chong Nonsi BTS station and the Chao Phraya River with seamless access to Bangkok's business, diplomatic, and cultural districts.

StatusHistory.note: Heatherwick Studio unveils Hatai, a lantern-inspired mixed-use development in Bangkok's Silom district anchored by Six Senses Bangkok.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: two hotels (incl. Six Senses), retail, and public/cultural space in Silom.

Title: Eco-Floating Hotel Qatar
Description: A floating, rotating off-grid hotel concept off the coast of Qatar by HAADS — a 152-room circular structure that completes a full rotation every 24 hours, powered by 55 vertical-axis wind turbines and rainwater harvesting.
DescriptionLong: The Eco-Floating Hotel is a floating, rotating off-grid hospitality concept being developed off the coast of Qatar by Hayri Atak Architectural Design Studio (HAADS). The circular structure — inspired by oceanic vortexes — is engineered to rotate slowly via a dynamic positioning system using thrusters and propellers, completing a full rotation every 24 hours. Designed for zero energy loss and waste, it employs 55 Vertical Axis Wind Turbine and Umbrella (VAWTAU) modules generating up to 25 kW each, plus a vortex-shaped roof that collects rainwater for irrigation. Seawater is purified for hotel use; wastewater is treated; organic waste becomes fertilizer. 152 guest rooms with private balconies surround a central 700-square-meter lobby illuminated by natural light through the transparent roof. Amenities include indoor/outdoor pools, spa, gym, sauna, beach club, and mini-golf course. Access via 140-degree floating pier, boats, or helipad. The structure is mobile and can be relocated to coastal areas with suitable water currents. Launched in March 2020; feasibility and technical studies ongoing.

StatusHistory.note: Vocabulary cleanup: removing the 'Spa' tag from the TMW type registry (spas are an amenity, not a primary use). Stripping Spa from types[] across all affected projects.

Title: The Barai Hua Hin
Description: An Unbound Collection by Hyatt — a 90-room wellness-focused property in Hua Hin extending the legacy of Lek Bunnag's award-winning Barai spa, opening late 2026.
DescriptionLong: The Barai Hua Hin marks one of two Thailand debuts for The Unbound Collection by Hyatt, opening late 2026 beachfront next to the existing Hyatt Regency Hua Hin. The 90-room property extends the legacy of the award-winning Barai spa designed by renowned master architect Lek Bunnag, with 18 treatment rooms, eight residential spa suites, and therapies rooted in earth, water, wind, and fire. Amenities include a dedicated wellness center, central pool oasis, lush gardens, and dining at the historic McFarland House and The White House. Hua Hin sits three hours from Bangkok and is celebrated for its long beaches, royal heritage, and relaxed coastal charm. Joins Hyatt's expanding 15-property Thailand portfolio.

StatusHistory.note: Vocabulary cleanup: removing the 'Spa' tag from the TMW type registry (spas are an amenity, not a primary use). Stripping Spa from types[] across all affected projects.

Title: Gaylord Opryland Expansion
Description: A $224M three-phase renovation and expansion marking Gaylord Opryland's 50th anniversary in 2027 — adding the $40M Magnolia District, 133,000 sq ft of revitalized ballrooms, and 108,000 sq ft of new meeting space on Briley Parkway.
DescriptionLong: Ryman Hospitality Properties' $224 million three-phase renovation and expansion of the iconic Gaylord Opryland Resort & Convention Center marks the property's 50th anniversary in 2027. Phase 1 revitalizes the Governor's and Presidential ballrooms (133,000 sq ft) and reimagines the former Magnolia Pool Courtyard as the $40 million Magnolia District — featuring a 6,200-sq-ft event lawn, 3,000-sq-ft pavilion, and a multistory sports bar with seating for 550 plus a tasting room. Phase 2 (year-end completion) continues with modern amenities. Phase 3 introduces a 108,000 sq ft structure along Briley Parkway with premium carpeted meeting spaces. Once complete in 2027, Opryland will feature 765,000 sq ft of state-of-the-art meeting facilities — among the most expansive convention destinations in the country. Renderings by Blur Workshop.

StatusHistory.note: Ryman Hospitality announces a $131M Gaylord Opryland expansion adding roughly 108,000 sq ft of meeting and event space.

Title: 515 Fern
Description: A 25-story, 471,000-square-foot office tower on the northwest corner of Fern Street and the Florida East Coast Railway tracks — expected to become the largest office building in downtown West Palm Beach, with 456,000 square feet of Class A office space, 15,000 square feet of retail, a new connector road parallel to the Brightline tracks, and a partially covered pedestrian pathway linking Brightline's downtown station to The Square.
DescriptionLong: 515 Fern is a 25-story, 471,000-square-foot Class A office tower planned for the northwest corner of Fern Street and the Florida East Coast Railway tracks in downtown West Palm Beach. Once delivered, it will become the largest office building in the city's downtown core, with 456,000 square feet of office space and 15,000 square feet of ground-floor retail. The 386-foot tower's facade pairs terracotta, metal, and glass in an industrial-style grid that echoes the language of 360 Rosemary, the adjacent 20-story Related office building completed in 2021 and anchored by Goldman Sachs, BlackRock, Elliott Management, and Point72. As part of its site plan, the project introduces a new connector road parallel to the Brightline tracks from Evernia Street to Fern Street, alongside a partially covered pedestrian pathway running along the building's east facade that links Brightline's downtown West Palm Beach station to The Square — the former CityPlace district.
Architect: Elkus Manfredi Architects, Leo A Daly

StatusHistory.note: Related (Stephen Ross) proposes 515 Fern, set to become downtown West Palm Beach's tallest office tower.
Related (Stephen Ross) proposes 515 Fern, set to become downtown West Palm Beach's tallest office tower.
Confirmed single-use Office: downtown WPB's tallest office tower (ancillary ground-floor retail).

Title: Zannier Île de Bendor
Description: Zannier takes over a private French Riviera island — 93 keys across three settings (Delos, Soukana, Madrague) with cuisine from Lionel Levy. Opened May 1, 2026.
DescriptionLong: Zannier Île de Bendor marks Zannier Hotels' takeover of a private 7-hectare island off Bandol on the French Riviera, opened May 1, 2026. The island — purchased and developed in the 1950s by pastis magnate Paul Ricard — has been reimagined by Belgian hotelier Arnaud Zannier across three distinct settings: Delos (the main hotel), Soukana (cabins clustered in the pines), and Madrague (private villas by the sea). Total inventory is 93 keys, ranging from rooms to multi-bedroom villas. The culinary program is led by Marseille-based chef Lionel Levy. The island includes multiple beaches, a marina, a chapel, art exhibition spaces, and the island's historic museum collections — all accessible by private boat shuttle from Bandol harbor.

Title: Four Seasons Hotel Mykonos
Description: Four Seasons debuts in Greece — 94 keys above Kalo Livadi Bay on Mykonos' south coast, designed by Nicos Valsamakis. Opened June 26, 2026.
DescriptionLong: Four Seasons Hotel Mykonos marks Four Seasons' Greek debut, opened June 26, 2026 above Kalo Livadi Bay on the south coast of Mykonos. The resort spans 94 guest rooms, suites, and private bungalows distributed across the hillside, with the architecture by celebrated Greek modernist Nicos Valsamakis (1924–2024) — one of his final works. The property includes multiple restaurants and pools, a destination spa, beach club, kids' and teens' clubs, and event spaces. Owned and developed by AGC Equity Partners in partnership with Four Seasons Hotels and Resorts. The opening expands Four Seasons' Mediterranean portfolio alongside its Athens (Astir Palace), Taormina, and Mallorca properties.
Developer: AGC Equity Partners, Four Seasons Hotels and Resorts

Title: The Park Gstaad, A Four Seasons Hotel
Description: Four Seasons' first Swiss hotel — a 75-key conversion of the historic Park Gstaad with Penthouse Residences and Clinique La Prairie spa. Opening late 2026 for ski season.
DescriptionLong: The Park Gstaad, A Four Seasons Hotel marks Four Seasons' Switzerland debut, scheduled to open in late 2026 in time for the alpine ski season. The historic Park Gstaad — long one of the Bernese Oberland's grandes dames — is being repositioned under Four Seasons management by owner Squircle Capital. The property includes 75 guest rooms and suites along with Four Seasons Private Residences (Penthouses), a destination spa operated in partnership with Clinique La Prairie, multiple restaurants and bars, ski-in/ski-out access via the Gstaad Mountain Rides network, and event spaces. The opening expands Four Seasons' alpine portfolio alongside its Megève and Vail properties.
Developer: Squircle Capital, Four Seasons Hotels and Resorts, Clinique La Prairie

StatusHistory.note: Four Seasons to manage the historic Park Gstaad, reopening the Swiss Alps landmark after a full renovation.
Re-classified to Resort: Four Seasons alpine resort with branded residences and spa.
Re-classified to Resort: Four Seasons alpine resort with branded residences and spa.

Title: Six Senses Milan
Description: Six Senses' Italian debut — 69 rooms in the heart of Milan's Brera district with interiors by Tara Bernerd & Partners. Opening late 2026.
DescriptionLong: Six Senses Milan marks Six Senses Hotels Resorts Spas' Italian debut, scheduled to open in late 2026. Located on Via Brera in the heart of one of Milan's most cultured districts — steps from the Pinacoteca di Brera, the Brera Botanical Garden, and the Quadrilatero della Moda — the hotel occupies a historic building being transformed under the brand's signature wellness-led approach. The property includes 69 guest rooms and suites, a destination spa with hammam and pools, multiple restaurants and bars, and an Alchemy Bar where guests blend natural skincare. Interiors are by London-based Tara Bernerd & Partners, layering local craft, sustainable materials, and biophilic design. Operated by Six Senses (part of IHG Hotels & Resorts).
Developer: Six Senses Hotels Resorts Spas, IHG Hotels & Resorts

StatusHistory.note: IHG's Six Senses to debut in Milan's Brera district with owner Gruppo Statuto.
Re-classified to Resort: Six Senses-branded hotel with extensive spa/wellness programme in Brera.
Re-classified to Resort: Six Senses-branded hotel with extensive spa/wellness programme in Brera.

Title: Baccarat Hotel Florence
Description: Baccarat's first European hotel — a restoration of the 14th-century Villa Camerata at Viale Augusto Righi 4, set in 12 acres of Florentine hills. Opening 2026.
DescriptionLong: Baccarat Hotel Florence marks Baccarat Hotels & Resorts' European debut, scheduled to open in 2026 after several years of restoration. The property occupies the historic Villa Camerata at Viale Augusto Righi 4, a 14th-century Florentine estate set on roughly 12 acres of gardens, olive groves, and vineyards in the hills above Florence. The hotel will include suites, restaurants, a destination spa, and event spaces — all infused with the signature Baccarat crystal language seen at the brand's flagship Baccarat Hotel New York. Operated by SH Hotels & Resorts, the Barry Sternlicht hospitality platform that also operates 1 Hotels and Treehouse Hotels.
Developer: Baccarat Hotels & Resorts, SH Hotels & Resorts

StatusHistory.note: SH Hotels' Baccarat brand to open a hotel at the historic Villa Camerata estate in Florence.
SH Hotels' Baccarat brand to open a hotel at the historic Villa Camerata estate in Florence.
Re-classified to Resort: Baccarat-branded estate hotel at Villa Camerata with spa and grounds.
Re-classified to Resort: Baccarat-branded estate hotel at Villa Camerata with spa and grounds.

Title: Waldorf Astoria London Admiralty Arch
Description: Hilton restores Sir Aston Webb's 1912 Admiralty Arch into a 96-key Waldorf Astoria, with restaurants by Clare Smyth and Daniel Boulud. Opening July 2026 between Trafalgar Square and The Mall.
DescriptionLong: Waldorf Astoria London Admiralty Arch marks the conversion of one of London's most prominent civic landmarks into a luxury hotel, opening July 2026. Designed in 1912 by Sir Aston Webb and built as a memorial to Queen Victoria, Admiralty Arch sits at the head of The Mall connecting Trafalgar Square to Buckingham Palace. Restored under a 250-year lease by Spanish developer Prime Investors Capital and operated by Hilton's Waldorf Astoria Hotels & Resorts, the property includes 96 guest rooms and suites, three Grade I-listed residences, restaurants from three-Michelin-starred chefs Clare Smyth (Core) and Daniel Boulud, a Guerlain Spa, and members' club. Architectural restoration by Blair Associates Architecture; interiors by Pierre-Yves Rochon.
Architect: Sir Aston Webb, Blair Associates Architecture, Pierre-Yves Rochon

StatusHistory.note: Waldorf Astoria appointed to operate London's landmark Admiralty Arch as a hotel with branded residences.
Waldorf Astoria appointed to operate London's landmark Admiralty Arch as a hotel with branded residences.
Waldorf Astoria appointed to operate London's landmark Admiralty Arch as a hotel with branded residences.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: Waldorf Astoria hotel plus branded residences in the Admiralty Arch conversion.
Re-classified to Mixed-Use: Waldorf Astoria hotel plus branded residences in the Admiralty Arch conversion.

Title: Four Seasons Hotel Danieli
Description: Four Seasons restores the legendary 14th-century Hotel Danieli on the Riva degli Schiavoni — 120 rooms (eventually 176) overlooking the lagoon, with interiors by Pierre-Yves Rochon. Opening August 26, 2026.
DescriptionLong: Four Seasons Hotel Danieli marks Four Seasons' Venice debut and the rebirth of one of the city's most storied hotels, reopening August 26, 2026 after a multi-year restoration. The property combines three palazzi on the Riva degli Schiavoni — including the 14th-century Palazzo Dandolo — with views across the lagoon to San Giorgio Maggiore. The first opening phase includes 120 guest rooms and suites, expanding to 176 keys upon full completion. Interiors are by Pierre-Yves Rochon, paired with restoration of the property's iconic gilded grand staircase and Gothic-revival lobby. Includes Restaurant Quadri direction by Alajmo, a rooftop terrace restaurant, spa, and event spaces. Owned by Gruppo Statuto and operated by Four Seasons Hotels and Resorts.
Developer: Gruppo Statuto, Four Seasons Hotels and Resorts

StatusHistory.note: Four Seasons to operate Venice's historic Hotel Danieli following a multi-year restoration.
Four Seasons to operate Venice's historic Hotel Danieli following a multi-year restoration.
Reservations open ahead of the August 2026 debut.

Title: Bvlgari Resort Ranfushi
Description: Bvlgari's first Maldives resort — 54 ultra-private villas across Ranfushi Island in the remote Raa Atoll, designed by ACPV ARCHITECTS Antonio Citterio Patricia Viel. Opening 2026.
DescriptionLong: Bvlgari Resort Ranfushi marks Bvlgari Hotels & Resorts' debut in the Maldives and the brand's first standalone resort destination, scheduled to open in 2026. Set on a private island in Raa Atoll — one of the Maldives' northern and least-developed atolls — the resort spans 54 ultra-spacious villas designed by ACPV ARCHITECTS Antonio Citterio Patricia Viel, the architectural firm responsible for every Bvlgari hotel worldwide. The villas combine Italian craftsmanship with Maldivian materials and feature private pools, direct lagoon and beach access. Amenities include a Bvlgari Spa, multiple restaurants and bars, an over-water yacht club, kids' and teens' clubs, and a marine biology center. Developed in partnership with Marriott International (Bvlgari Hotels' operator) and a local development partner.

StatusHistory.note: Bvlgari Hotels & Resorts to debut in the Maldives on Ranfushi island, Raa Atoll.
Re-classified to Resort: Bvlgari private-island villa resort in Raa Atoll with spa.
Re-classified to Resort: Bvlgari private-island villa resort in Raa Atoll with spa.

Title: Orient Express Palazzo Donà Giovannelli
Description: Orient Express debuts in Venice — a 47-room restoration of the 1436 Palazzo Donà Giovannelli by Filippo Calendario (architect of the Doge's Palace), in the Cannaregio sestiere. Opened April 2026.
DescriptionLong: Orient Express Palazzo Donà Giovannelli marks Orient Express' second hotel and Venice debut, welcomed first guests in April 2026. The hotel occupies the historic noble Palazzo Donà Giovannelli, built in 1436 to a design by master architect Filippo Calendario (the visionary mind behind the Doge's Palace), at the intersection of two canals in Cannaregio — one of Venice's most authentic sestieri. The 47-room property includes 29 rooms, 16 suites (including 6 Signature Suites), and 2 Orient Express Apartments, ranging from 30 to 148 square meters. Rates start at €1,320 for a superior room with breakfast included; Signature Suites and Apartments from €4,400 up to €20,000. Part of Accor's Orient Express brand expansion.

Title: COMO Le Beauvallon
Description: A Belle Époque icon reborn on the French Riviera — COMO's 42-room reopening on a 10-acre estate above the Gulf of Saint-Tropez, with chef Yannick Alléno's culinary direction. Opened April 24, 2026.
DescriptionLong: COMO Le Beauvallon marks COMO Hotels and Resorts' restoration of a Belle Époque landmark above the Gulf of Saint-Tropez, opened April 24, 2026.
