Originally opened in 1914, the property occupies a 10-acre (4-hectare) elevated estate combining Mediterranean gardens, umbrella pines, and palm-lined terraces overlooking the bay. The reopened hotel includes 42 rooms and suites (with 25 sea-view suites), a beach club, private pontoon for yachts, proximity to the Golf Club de Beauvallon, and a culinary program led by chef Yannick Alléno (who holds 17 Michelin stars across his portfolio). The 2026 reopening continues COMO's European expansion alongside its London, Bhutan, Bali, and Dolomites properties.

Title: Villa San Michele, A Belmond Hotel
Description: Belmond's legendary Renaissance retreat in the Fiesole hills overlooking Florence reopened April 28, 2026 after an 18-month renovation — 39 reimagined rooms, a Spa by Guerlain, and Michelangelo-attributed façade preserved.
DescriptionLong: Villa San Michele, A Belmond Hotel reopened April 28, 2026 after an 18-month renovation — a former 15th-century Franciscan monastery perched in the Fiesole hills overlooking Florence, attributed in part to Michelangelo. The reimagined property introduces 39 reimagined rooms and suites, a Spa by Guerlain, revitalized gardens and woodlands, a new gastronomic offering, and a curated program of energy-raising experiences in collaboration with La DoubleJ. Redesign was led by Luigi Fragola Architects in collaboration with Belmond's design team and Luca Ghezzi Garden Design, preserving the villa's Michelangelo-inspired façade, arched terrace, and original chapel, cloister, and loggias.
Architect: Luigi Fragola Architects, Luca Ghezzi Garden Design

Title: Delano Miami Beach
Description: The historic 1947 Delano Hotel reopened May 1, 2026 after a six-year renovation — 171 keys on Collins Avenue, reimagined by Elastic Architects with Ennismore + Cain International, including a revived Rose Bar.
DescriptionLong: Delano Miami Beach reopened May 1, 2026 after a six-year renovation — the original 1947 Robert Swartburg-designed Art Deco landmark at 1685 Collins Avenue, restored under Ennismore in partnership with Cain International (who took a minority stake in the brand in 2024). The 171-key property includes guestrooms, suites, signature Poolside Bungalows, and Penthouse Suites with views of both the Miami skyline and the Atlantic Ocean. Renovation design led by Elastic Architects with Ennismore's lifestyle team, leaning minimalist and airy with organic furniture. Four signature restaurant and bar concepts include a revival of the legendary Rose Bar, once a gathering place for Hollywood luminaries and Miami's creative elite.

Title: Capella Kyoto
Description: Capella's debut in Japan — an 89-key hotel in Kyoto's historic Miyagawa-cho geisha district, designed by Kengo Kuma & Associates with Brewin Design Office. Opened March 22, 2026.
DescriptionLong: Capella Kyoto marks the first Capella Hotels & Resorts property in Japan, opened March 22, 2026. The 89-key hotel reimagines Kyoto's traditional machiya (townhouse) typology in the city's historic Miyagawa-cho kagai (geisha district), steps from Kenninji temple and the Kamo river. Designed by Kengo Kuma & Associates with Singapore-based Brewin Design Office, the building reinterprets Kyoto's characteristic spatial composition — layered sequences of narrow alleys, hidden gardens, and interior thresholds. Room types span Deluxe City Rooms, Premier Theatre Rooms, two Gion Suites with direct Kenninji temple views, six Onsen Suites with private hot spring baths, and the 206-sqm top-floor Capella Suite.
Architect: Kengo Kuma & Associates, Brewin Design Office

Title: Tierras Villas
Description: A five-villa retreat on Mononaftis Beach in northern Crete — villas named after natural elements (Roē, Phos, Ryāki, Terra, Anemi), each with a private pool, terrace, and sea views, with the architecture following the hillside contours in pale stone, raw wood, and earthy tones.
DescriptionLong: Tierras Villas is a five-villa boutique retreat opening on Mononaftis Beach near Agia Pelagia in northern Crete, designed to read as a quiet extension of the hillside it sits on. Each villa is named after a natural element — Roē for water, Phos for light, Ryāki for fire, Terra for earth, and Anemi for air — with the architecture following the slope's contours in pale stone, raw wood, and earthy tones. Every villa carries a private pool, outdoor terrace, and sea views, organized around indoor-outdoor living. The experience centers on chef-led dining with daily breakfast, foraging excursions, and in-villa wellness treatments, with guests also given access to the Bath House Spa at neighboring Acro Suites.

StatusHistory.note: Public unveiling: Tierras Villas (5 villas, Omicron Hotels) to open summer 2026 on Crete's Mononaftis coast, Agia Pelagia (Hospitality Design).

Title: MOMI Slow Living
Description: A 100-key boutique adult-friendly retreat in Hersonissos, Crete, designed to feel like a traditional Cretan village among olive trees — with a spa, outdoor cinema, pool bar, on-site nail salon, and dining at the property's Elia restaurant.
DescriptionLong: MOMI Slow Living is a 100-key boutique adult-friendly retreat in Hersonissos on Crete's northern coast, designed to feel like a traditional Cretan village set among olive trees. Following a 2026 expansion that added 30 rooms — 20 standard and 10 with private pools — the property now spans 100 keys total, alongside a new pool bar and an on-site nail salon. The amenity program centers on slow living and the unhurried feel of a Cretan stone village, anchored by Elia restaurant, a spa, an outdoor cinema, and a generous pool deck. The 2026 additions deepen the property's existing F&B and wellness offering rather than reshape it.

Title: Acro Suites
Description: A cliffside, adults-only resort in northern Crete and a 2025 Michelin Key winner — with cave-style suites, the Bath House Spa and a new wellness treatment house, the new Caléa restaurant from Michelin-starred chef Gerardo Metta, and tennis, pickleball, and paddle courts.
DescriptionLong: Acro Suites is a cliffside, adults-only resort overlooking the Aegean on Crete's northern coast in Mononaftis, with a roster of suites that includes the Thalos suite with private pool, the Kamares suite, the Dione suite, and the AcroTerra Villa. The property carries a 2025 Michelin Key designation. The 2026 season brings two major additions: Caléa, a new restaurant from Michelin-starred chef Gerardo Metta; and a new wellness treatment house that complements the existing Bath House Spa with services ranging from Japanese-inspired scalp treatments to hydrotherapy and pilates. New pickleball courts join the existing tennis and paddle courts.

Title: Gallery at SoMi Parc
Description: A 15-story, 350-unit affordable housing development in the heart of South Miami — 273,000 rentable square feet serving households earning between 20% and 80% of AMI, including 51 Link Units for residents with special needs, steps from the South Miami Metrorail station and Sunset Drive.
DescriptionLong: Gallery at SoMi Parc is a 15-story, 350-unit affordable housing development rising in the heart of South Miami, delivering 273,000 rentable square feet of apartment homes serving households earning between 20% and 80% of the Area Median Income (AMI). The development includes 51 Link Units designated for individuals with special needs, alongside working families across the income spectrum. Each home features quartz countertops, luxury vinyl tile flooring, porcelain tile in bathrooms, modern plumbing fixtures, window treatments, and Energy Star-rated appliances including an in-unit washer and dryer and dishwasher. The amenity program includes a resort-style pool, fitness center, multipurpose room, and a private landscaped courtyard with BBQ. The location places residents steps from the South Miami Metrorail station on Sunset Drive, walking distance from South Miami Hospital, and blocks from the tree-lined commercial heart of South Miami's downtown — with the University of Miami's main campus bordering the city to the east, and Coral Gables, Pinecrest, and Coconut Grove all within a few minutes.

StatusHistory.note: Announced: Related Urban (RUDG) proposes Phase 2 of SoMi Parc ("Gallery") at 5949 SW 68th St, South Miami — Live Local Act, ~390 apartments, 11 stories (The Real Deal).

Recorded "construction" appears overstated: trusted press (TRD Mar 2025, Florida YIMBY Jul 2025) shows only proposal/site-plan review as of mid-2025; no groundbreaking confirmed. Propose walking status back to announced for review.

Title: The Residences Claude Pepper
Description: A 15-story, 428-unit affordable, workforce, and supportive housing development in Allapattah — 312,992 square feet of mixed-AMI residences anchored by 83 Link Units for residents with special needs, a 4,400-square-foot community center, and resident amenities including a fitness center, co-working lab, and landscaped courtyard with tot lot.
DescriptionLong: The Residences Claude Pepper is a 15-story, 428-unit affordable, workforce, and supportive housing development rising in Miami's Allapattah neighborhood. The 312,992-square-foot high-rise delivers a mix of residences for households earning between 20% and 80% of the Area Median Income (AMI) — weighted to approximately 60% AMI — alongside 30 workforce units and one staff unit serving households at 120% AMI. Eighty-three of the homes are designated as Link Units reserved for residents with special needs. Apartments feature quartz countertops, luxury vinyl tile flooring, porcelain tile in bathrooms, walk-in closets in two-bedroom units, and Energy Star-rated appliances including in-unit washers and dryers. The amenity program includes a fitness center, co-working lab, multipurpose room, package room, bike storage, and a private landscaped courtyard with a BBQ area and tot lot, alongside a roughly 4,400-square-foot community center on site.

StatusHistory.note: Announced: Related Urban (RUDG) proposes Live Local Act redevelopment of the Mildred & Claude Pepper Tower, 750 NW 18th Terrace, Miami — ~398 apts replacing the 1970 senior building (The Real Deal).

Recorded "breaking-ground" appears overstated: trusted press (TRD Jan 2025, Florida YIMBY Mar 2025) shows only proposal/entitlement as of mid-2025; no groundbreaking confirmed. The recorded 2026-06-23 start is unsourced. Propose walking status back to announced for review.

Title: Amangani
Description: Aman's first U.S. resort, set on top of East Gros Ventre Butte 7,000 feet above the Snake River Valley — 40 suites carved into a low-slung sandstone and Oklahoma-stone form, anchored by a 35-meter heated infinity pool that runs out toward the Tetons, an Aman Spa, and direct access to Jackson Hole's ski and outdoor program.
DescriptionLong: Amangani is Aman's first U.S. resort and one of the brand's most distinctive properties — a 40-suite hilltop retreat set 7,000 feet above the Snake River Valley on East Gros Ventre Butte, with sweeping views of the Tetons and the Snake River below. The architecture is a long, low-slung form clad in sandstone and Oklahoma stone, designed to read as part of the butte itself rather than something placed on top of it, with redwood, oak, and slate carrying through the interior program. Every suite carries panoramic valley views, a wood-burning fireplace, and a freestanding tub. The amenity anchor is a 35-meter heated outdoor infinity pool that runs out toward the Tetons, alongside an Aman Spa, a fitness center, multiple restaurants, and a year-round outdoor program that ranges from skiing at Jackson Hole and Snow King in winter to fly fishing, riding, hiking, and Grand Teton and Yellowstone excursions in the warmer months.

Title: The Cooper
Description: The Cooper is a five-star waterfront hotel on the downtown Charleston peninsula, set on Charleston Harbor at the edge of the historic French Quarter beside Joe Riley Waterfront Park. Owned and operated by Ben Navarro's Beemok Hospitality Collection — the group behind Charleston Place — it offers 191 rooms and suites with harbor and skyline views, plus the city's first private hotel marina. Interiors by Champalimaud Design reinterpret Charleston's historic architecture, complemented by a rooftop infinity pool, a full spa, generous event space, a charter-boat fleet and four dining venues led by the signature restaurant The Crossing.
DescriptionLong: Charleston's first waterfront luxury hotel — a 191-key five-star property on Concord Street with the city's first private hotel marina.

StatusHistory.note: Lowe & Dart break ground on the ~225-key downtown Charleston waterfront hotel (GC BL Harbert)
Lowe & Dart break ground on the ~225-key downtown Charleston waterfront hotel (GC BL Harbert)
Lowe & BL Harbert top out the downtown Charleston waterfront hotel
The Cooper opens to guests under Beemok Hospitality Collection; built out to 191 keys (down from the original 225-key plan after redesign)

Title: Tribune Tower Addition
Description: The Tribune Tower Addition, also known as the Tribune East Tower, is a proposed mixed-use supertall planned for the eastern parcel of the landmark Tribune Tower property in Chicago's Streeterville, on North Michigan Avenue. Designed by Adrian Smith + Gordon Gill Architecture for developers Golub & Company and CIM Group, the roughly 1,440-foot, 113-story tower would rank among Chicago's tallest, blending luxury condominium and rental residences with a five-star hotel and ground-floor retail. Its sculpted form would rise dramatically beside the Gothic-revival Tribune Tower, creating one of the city's most prestigious addresses.
DescriptionLong: A proposed 113-story supertall rising behind the landmark Tribune Tower on Michigan Avenue, pairing 560-plus luxury residences with hotel uses and ground-floor retail.

StatusHistory.note: Golub & CIM unveil ~1,440-ft, 113-story supertall (Adrian Smith + Gordon Gill) behind the historic Tribune Tower
Program includes ~200 five-star hotel rooms alongside residences; Streeterville site at 421 N Michigan Ave
Program includes ~200 five-star hotel rooms alongside residences; Streeterville site at 421 N Michigan Ave

Title: Six Senses Grand Bahama
Description: Six Senses Grand Bahama is a sustainability-led luxury resort and branded-residence community on Grand Bahama near Freeport, developed by Weller Development Partners with Pegasus Capital Advisors under the Six Senses banner. Set across a waterfront site with roughly 2,400 feet of beach frontage and water on three sides between the Atlantic and the Grand Lucayan Waterway, the regenerative enclave pairs a village of resort villas with a collection of Six Senses Branded Residences designed by Olson Kundig. Guests and owners share a Six Senses Spa, a marina and jetty bar, a dive and marine-research center, an Earth Lab and signature dining, all woven into protected land-and-sea preserves.
DescriptionLong: Six Senses' second Caribbean resort — 70 villas and 28 residences set between the Atlantic and the Grand Lucayan Waterway on Grand Bahama, with a Six Senses Spa, beach club and jetty bar.
Developer: Six Senses Hotels Resorts Spas, IHG Hotels & Resorts

StatusHistory.note: Weller Development & Six Senses announce Grand Bahama resort, villas and branded residences focused on regenerative design

Title: Gajoen Tokyo, LXR Hotels & Resorts
Description: Gajoen Tokyo, LXR Hotels & Resorts brings Hilton's ultra-luxury LXR brand to Tokyo, transforming the storied Hotel Gajoen in the Meguro district into a heritage-rich luxury hotel owned by Brookfield. Drawing on nearly a century of Japanese artistry and craft, the property pairs 60 generously scaled guest rooms and suites with five dining destinations, a spa and fitness center, and thousands of square meters of event space. Its centerpiece is the Hyakudan Kaidan, the 'Hundred-Step Staircase' — a designated Tokyo Metropolitan Tangible Cultural Property — preserved as the hotel's last historic wooden structure amid contemporary luxury interiors.
DescriptionLong: Hilton's LXR brand debuts in Tokyo at the storied Hotel Gajoen in Meguro — a landmark luxury address reopening as Gajoen Tokyo, LXR Hotels & Resorts in 2027.

StatusHistory.note: Hilton signs Gajoen Tokyo for LXR Hotels & Resorts, converting the storied Meguro property (debut 2027)
60 guest rooms (suites over 100 sqm), ~5,700 sqm event space, five dining venues; Meguro, Tokyo

Title: 262 Fifth Avenue
Description: 262 Fifth Avenue is an ultra-slender supertall residential tower in Manhattan's NoMad district, at the corner of Fifth Avenue and West 29th Street. Developed by Five Points Development and designed by Moscow-based Meganom in its first U.S. commission — with SLCE Architects as executive architect and interiors by Copenhagen's Norm Architects — the 860-foot tower is among the skinniest of its height in New York. An exoskeleton structure and perimeter-shifted core yield column-free, full-floor and duplex residences with open north-south window walls and distinctive porthole windows, in a boutique collection of just 26 light-filled homes crowned by a multi-floor penthouse.
DescriptionLong: A 52-story, 860-foot slender condominium tower at Fifth Avenue and 29th Street — poised to be Manhattan's tallest all-residential building, with just 26 full-floor and duplex homes priced from $7.5 million.

StatusHistory.note: Meganom unveils ultra-skinny NoMad supertall for Five Points Development (design architect Meganom, AOR SLCE)
Foundation / below-grade work underway on the 860-ft NoMad supertall
262 Fifth Avenue structurally tops out at 860 ft over NoMad
NY AG approves the condo offering plan, clearing the way for sales of the 26 residences

Title: Orange Hill Course, Kiawah Island Club
Description: A new private golf course planned by Beau Welling for the Kiawah Island Club, with construction anticipated to begin in summer 2026 pending approval.
DescriptionLong: A new private golf course planned by Beau Welling for the Kiawah Island Club.

StatusHistory.note: South Street Partners plans private Kiawah Island Club golf course (Beau Welling design) on 933-acre Johns Island tract
933-acre tract on Johns Island between River and Bohicket Roads

Title: Mandarin Oriental Cristallo, Cortina d'Ampezzo
Description: Mandarin Oriental's debut in the Italian Dolomites — the extensive transformation of the legendary Grand Hotel Cristallo into an alpine luxury hotel, opening in 2026.
DescriptionLong: Mandarin Oriental's debut in the Italian Dolomites — the extensive transformation of the legendary Grand Hotel Cristallo into an alpine luxury hotel.

StatusHistory.note: Mandarin Oriental announces its first mountain resort, rebranding the historic Hotel Cristallo in Cortina d'Ampezzo

Title: Westside Village
Description: Westside Village is a $1.7 billion, 37-acre master-planned mixed-use district on the western edge of Fort Worth's Cultural District, set along the West Fork of the Trinity River on the former Fort Worth ISD headquarters site. Developed by Larkspur Capital and The Keystone Group and master-planned by Michael Hsu Office of Architecture with Corgan as architect of record, the walkable district weaves roughly 880,000 square feet of office, 238,000 square feet of retail, 1,785 residences and a 175-key boutique hotel through paseos, public plazas and landscaped courtyards. Its anchor phase pairs Class AA office space with a luxury residential community of resort-style amenities, chef-driven dining and a private social club linked to the Trinity River trail system.
DescriptionLong: A $1.7 billion, 37-acre mixed-use district at the edge of Fort Worth's Cultural District — 880,000 sf of office, 238,000 sf of retail, 1,785 residences and a luxury hotel — that broke ground in March 2026.
Architect: Michael Hsu Office of Architecture, Corgan

StatusHistory.note: Larkspur Capital & The Keystone Group reveal $1.7B, 37-acre Westside Village mixed-use district on former FWISD HQ site
Fort Worth approves economic-development incentive package (~$80M performance grants + ~$45M TIF)

Title: The Knox
Description: The Knox is a luxury hotel and branded-residences development anchoring Dallas's Knox Street mixed-use district, set along the Katy Trail at the edge of Highland Park. Part of a roughly one-million-square-foot district developed by a joint venture led by Trammell Crow Company with BDT & MSD Partners, The Retail Connection and Highland Park Village Associates, it brings together a hotel operated by Auberge Resorts Collection and private residences designed by Woods Bagot with interiors by Martin Brudnizki. Residents and guests enjoy curated dining, boutique retail and a half-acre park connected directly to the Katy Trail in one of Dallas's most prestigious neighborhoods.
DescriptionLong: A luxury hotel and branded-residences development anchoring Dallas's Knox Street mixed-use district, with private residences nearly sold out ahead of a late-2026 debut.

StatusHistory.note: MSD Capital, Trammell Crow & The Retail Connection announce luxury Knox Street mixed-use development
Trammell Crow-led JV begins construction on the Knox Street development (GCs Balfour Beatty & Andres)
Trammell Crow-led JV begins construction on the Knox Street development (GCs Balfour Beatty & Andres)
Beal Bank originates ~$619M construction-and-permanent loan for the Knox Street development
Knox Street JV announces topping out; on track for late-2026 delivery

Title: Luminary
Description: Luminary is a planned 35-story luxury condominium tower on the northwest corner of 14th and Guadalupe streets in downtown Austin, near the Texas Capitol. Developed by Northland Living — the U.S. residential arm of Canada's Northland Properties — and designed by Page Southerland Page, the roughly 400-foot tower pairs a bright, neutral aesthetic with copper-toned metallic accents and arched, retail-lined street frontage. Its collection of roughly 280 one- to three-bedroom residences is served by a pool, fitness facilities and generous indoor-outdoor amenity space.
DescriptionLong: A 35-story luxury condominium tower rising near the Texas Capitol in downtown Austin, with roughly 280 residences ranging from under $1 million to more than $3 million.

StatusHistory.note: Northland Living unveils 35-story, ~400-ft Luminary condo tower at 14th & Guadalupe in downtown Austin
Status overstated: Luminary never broke ground — Northland paused groundbreaking and sales in Dec 2025 for market conditions (TRD, KXAN). Corrected construction → announced.

Title: Aman Sveti Stefan
Description: An ultra-luxury single-property resort occupying a fortified 15th-century island village on Montenegro's Adriatic coast — 50 cottages, rooms, and suites woven through the historic stone village, plus Villa Miločer on the mainland with private gardens, two beach clubs, an Aman Spa, and a Pasquale Bruni boutique.
DescriptionLong: Aman Sveti Stefan is one of the most singular hotels in the Aman portfolio: an ultra-luxury single-property resort spread across a fortified 15th-century fishing village built on a small island off Montenegro's Adriatic coast and connected to the mainland by a narrow stone causeway. Fifty cottages, rooms, and suites are woven through the historic stone village, retaining the original streets, courtyards, and rooflines while delivering full Aman-level interiors. The property extends to the mainland with Villa Miločer — the former summer residence of Queen Marija Karađorđević — set in private parkland with eight additional rooms and suites. The amenity program includes two beach clubs on the surrounding bays, an Aman Spa, multiple restaurants, and a Pasquale Bruni boutique, with the entire island village itself accessible only to guests of the hotel.

Title: The St. Regis London
Description: A 193-key hotel returning to the heart of Mayfair on a landmark corner site at Bond Street and Conduit Street — 66 suites, two wellness suites, a 1,615-square-foot Penthouse with private terrace and 360-degree London views, an intimate central courtyard, Le Perroquet by Chef Daniel Rose, and a curated 1,000-piece art collection from Lee Alexander McQueen's Sarabande Foundation.
DescriptionLong: The St. Regis London marks the brand's return to the heart of Mayfair, following a full renovation of The Westbury that delivers 193 guestrooms — including 66 suites — across a landmark site at the corner of Bond Street and Conduit Street. The architectural transformation adds new floors and expanded public spaces, alongside two exclusive wellness suites. At the top of the hotel, the Penthouse Suite carries a 1,615-square-foot private terrace and 360-degree panoramic views across the London skyline. Interiors by Richmond International reference Mayfair's distinctive character — the bespoke craftsmanship of Savile Row, the quiet glamour of Bond Street, and the cultural legacy of the neighborhood's galleries and auction rooms. The food and beverage program debuts with Le Perroquet, an all-day French brasserie marking the London debut of acclaimed Chef Daniel Rose, whose career spans Paris's La Bourse et La Vie and Michelin-starred Le Coucou in New York. The hotel's entire 1,000-piece art collection — featuring more than 80 artists across guestrooms, suites, and public spaces — is curated for the first time by the House of Bandits gallery at the Sarabande Foundation, established by the late Lee Alexander McQueen. The signature St. Regis Butler Service anchors the experience, with an intimate central courtyard at the heart of the property.

StatusHistory.note: St. Regis brand's London debut announced via Cola Holdings management agreement — a redevelopment of the former Westbury Mayfair hotel.
195 rooms (incl. 64 suites) in Mayfair, at Conduit Street and New Bond Street.

Title: One London
Description: A 74-storey, 309.6-meter tower in the City of London's Eastern City Cluster — set to become the UK capital's joint tallest building alongside the Shard, with weathering steel structural pillars, the highest bar in London at the top, an 11th-floor public garden, and London Museum education spaces on the top two floors.
DescriptionLong: One London (formerly known as 1 Undershaft) is a 74-storey, 309.6-meter commercial tower planned at the heart of the City of London's Eastern City Cluster. Once complete in 2033, the building will become the UK capital's joint tallest, matching the height of the Shard across the Thames. The bulk of the program serves as commercial workplace for between 7,000 and 10,000 workers, with the top of the building dedicated to civic and public uses — including the highest bar in London at the summit and, on the top two floors, London Museum education spaces where school children and young people can experience London from on high. The 11th floor carries a public garden integrated into the building's expressive structural language: a forest of weathering steel pillars that architect Eric Parry calls 'giant redwoods,' slim but powerful, branching to support the garden while continuing up to carry the 300-meter tower above. The building targets BREEAM and NABERS UK sustainability ratings, with a landscape strategy delivering an advertised 960 percent biodiversity net gain across the site. The white-skinned, passively shaded facade replaces the typical sealed glass tower with a more open, vertically gardened expression — a high-rise that, in Parry's framing, addresses the sky rather than the urban block.

StatusHistory.note: Eric Parry's revised 74-storey 1 Undershaft scheme approved by City of London planning sub-committee.

Title: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Puerto Rico
Description: An ultra-luxury Four Seasons resort and branded-residence estate set within a private 483-acre nature reserve on Puerto Rico's northeastern coast at Bahía Beach, pairing 139 rooms with 85 residences, an 18-hole Robert Trent Jones Jr. golf course, a ten-room spa, and ten dining concepts along two miles of protected beach.
DescriptionLong: Cradled within a private 483-acre nature reserve and stretching along nearly two miles of palm-lined golden beach, Four Seasons Resort and Residences Puerto Rico pairs 139 refreshed guest rooms and suites with 85 private branded residences on Puerto Rico's lush northeastern coast. The estate is anchored by an 18-hole Robert Trent Jones Jr. championship golf course, alongside a full-service spa with ten treatment rooms, two resort pools, an expanded racquet centre for tennis, pickleball and padel, two fitness centres, and a beachside watersports hub for kitesurfing and windsurfing. Ten distinct dining and bar concepts range from refined international cuisine to toes-in-the-sand island fare, while families gravitate to a waterpark and the kids' club. Interiors by Meyer Davis lean into a tropical-elegant aesthetic that frames the surrounding rainforest, river and ocean, and the property holds the distinction of being the Caribbean's only Certified Gold Audubon International Signature Sanctuary, home to more than 240 species of flora and fauna including leatherback turtles and Caribbean manatees. The combination of conservation-grade landscape, championship golf and full Four Seasons branded-residence services makes it a standout among the region's ultra-luxury beachfront estates.
Architect: Meyer Davis, Robert Trent Jones Jr.

Title: Waldorf Astoria Tokyo Nihonbashi
Description: Japan's first Waldorf Astoria hotel and Asia-Pacific's first Waldorf Astoria branded residences, crowning a 284-meter, 52-story Nikken Sekkei / Pelli Clarke & Partners tower in Tokyo's historic Nihonbashi district above the preserved 1930 Nomura Building.
DescriptionLong: Rising roughly 284 meters over the historic Nihonbashi waterfront, this 52-story glass tower anchors one of central Tokyo's largest urban-redevelopment schemes, its "swinging setback" silhouette — designed by Nikken Sekkei with Pelli Clarke & Partners — evoking the folds of a kimono, the flow of the river, and the soft layering of a noren curtain. The upper crown of the building holds Japan's debut Waldorf Astoria: a 197-room hotel across nine floors offering signature restaurants, the brand's hallmark Peacock Alley lounge, an indoor pool, a spa and fitness center, and a ballroom-and-chapel events suite. Above the hotel sit just 71 Waldorf Astoria Residences — the first Waldorf Astoria branded homes in the Asia-Pacific region — spanning expansive layouts from roughly 60 to 430 square meters, with residents enjoying a sky lobby lounge, private kitchen and party rooms, and a salon perched some 250 meters above the city. At street level the development weaves new retail, business and event facilities around the meticulously preserved 1930 former Nomura Building, knitting a landmark of pre-war Tokyo into a contemporary international financial hub. Directly connected to Nihombashi Station and minutes from Ginza, Tokyo Station and the Marunouchi business core, the tower pairs rarefied vertical living with one of the capital's most storied addresses.
Developer: Mitsui Fudosan, Nomura Real Estate Development

StatusHistory.note: Mitsui Fudosan and Hilton launched Waldorf Astoria Residences Tokyo Nihonbashi, APAC's first Waldorf Astoria residences
Nihonbashi 1-Chome Central District redevelopment (284m tower housing the Waldorf) began construction Dec 2021
Waldorf Astoria Residences sales launched Sept 2025; 71 units on floors 48-51, move-in fall 2027

Title: Andaz Turks & Caicos at Grace Bay
Description: Hyatt's first Turks & Caicos resort and the debut Andaz in the Caribbean — a seven-storey beachfront hotel-and-residences retreat on 5.5 acres of Grace Bay with 59 rooms, 73 branded residences, multiple pools, three restaurants, and an indoor-outdoor garden spa.
DescriptionLong: Spread across 5.5 acres of the celebrated Grace Bay shoreline, Andaz Turks & Caicos at Grace Bay rises as a seven-storey, twin-building beachfront retreat pairing a boutique hotel with a collection of branded residences ranging from studios to penthouses. Guests move between multiple swimming pools overlooking the turquoise water, three restaurants and three bars, and an indoor-outdoor spa wrapped around a garden, complemented by a yoga pavilion, fitness studio, tennis, and watersports along one of the Caribbean's most famous beaches. Designed by RAD Architecture and Modus Operandi with locally inspired interiors — including penthouses by designer Francisco Jove — the property fuses contemporary island architecture with Andaz's signature residential, design-forward sensibility. A kids' activity club and full residential ownership with hotel services broaden its appeal to both vacationers and second-home buyers. As Hyatt's first hotel in Turks & Caicos and the debut of the Andaz brand in the Caribbean, it marks a notable luxury-lifestyle milestone for the region.

StatusHistory.note: Hyatt announced Andaz Turks & Caicos at Grace Bay, Andaz brand's Caribbean debut
Broke ground August 2023; construction at one floor every 45 days per Newsline TCI
Date correction: Hyatt announced Andaz Turks & Caicos at Grace Bay on Sept 25, 2018
Topping-off ceremony, delivered ahead of schedule per Hospitality Net
Now bookable, taking reservations for stays from June 1, 2027 per One Mile at a Time
Now bookable, taking reservations for stays from June 1, 2027 per One Mile at a Time

Title: The Dallas EDITION Hotel & Residences
Description: The EDITION brand's first Texas hotel — a 29-story, 214-key five-star hotel paired with 60 ultra-luxury branded residences, anchoring Kaizen's Chalk Hill development at the gateway to Uptown Dallas.
DescriptionLong: Rising twenty-nine stories above a 2.5-acre site at the gateway to Uptown Dallas, the Dallas EDITION pairs a 214-key five-star hotel with just 60 branded residences, anchoring the Chalk Hill mixed-use development. Architecture by BOKA Powell frames a crystalline tower crowned by a glowing apex, while Bonetti/Kozerski Architecture — the design studio behind EDITION properties worldwide — shapes serene, light-filled residential interiors, each home opening onto a private terrace. Amenities read like a wellness-forward resort: a cinematic pool deck, a full-service spa with hydrotherapy, a longevity-focused wellness center, a signature restaurant and bar, a ballroom, and dedicated Wellness Concierge service, with residents granted preferred access to hotel dining and services. Landscape architecture by SWA in partnership with SCDA threads terraces, gardens and elevated outdoor rooms through the property, with direct connections to the Katy Trail and Dallas North Tollway tying it into the city. The crowning penthouse spans nearly 6,900 square feet with more than 3,300 square feet of outdoor space, placing the project among Dallas's most exclusive addresses and bringing Ian Schrager's hushed-luxury hospitality vision to the Harwood District for the first time.

StatusHistory.note: EDITION's first Texas hotel & residences anchor Kaizen's Chalk Hill in Uptown Dallas; per Dallas Innovates, 2026-03-26

Title: The Nell New York
Description: The first hotel ever within landmarked Rockefeller Center — an intimate Midtown debut of Aspen's legendary Little Nell, set across nine floors of 10 Rockefeller Plaza with a destination restaurant, spa, and Relais & Châteaux–caliber service.
DescriptionLong: The Nell New York is the first hotel ever to open within landmarked Rockefeller Center, carved into the upper floors of 10 Rockefeller Plaza above the NBC Today show studios and set behind the campus's iconic Art Deco limestone facade. Spanning nine guest floors with roughly 130 rooms and suites, it brings the intimate, residential, ski-lodge-meets-grand-hotel sensibility of Aspen's Little Nell to Midtown, with a seventh-floor arrival sequence of reception, lobby lounge and signature restaurant, and a spa and fitness center one level above. Interiors lean into warm woods, soft lighting and art-filled lounges that echo Rockefeller Center's original spirit as a place to surround oneself with the best of the human spirit. Affiliated with Relais & Châteaux and built on a Five-Diamond legacy of personalized service, it offers guests a rarefied perch at the cultural crossroads of New York — steps from the galleries, studios, dining rooms and skating rink that define the district. As the only hotel inside one of the world's most famous urban complexes, it pairs landmark architecture with the exacting hospitality that made its Aspen original a benchmark for American luxury.
Developer: Aspen Hospitality, Tishman Speyer, The Georgetown Company

StatusHistory.note: Aspen Hospitality unveiled plans for The Little Nell / The Nell at 10 Rockefeller Plaza, NYC's first Rockefeller Center hotel
Aspen Hospitality unveiled plans for The Little Nell / The Nell at 10 Rockefeller Plaza, NYC's first Rockefeller Center hotel

Title: 310 Greenwich Street
Description: A Morris Adjmi–designed residential supertall woven into Tribeca's Independence Plaza, pairing premium waterfront-adjacent homes with a permanent affordable-housing component atop a brick-clad, neighborhood-scaled podium.
DescriptionLong: Set within Tribeca's Independence Plaza along the Lower Manhattan waterfront, 310 Greenwich Street is planned as a slender residential supertall whose footprint sits at the center of a three-winged podium woven into the existing complex. The tower's lower levels are clad in gray paneling framed by large square windows, while its upper shaft is articulated by a lattice of converging columns that taper toward the crown; brick-toned podium wings are scaled and detailed to echo the historic masonry character of surrounding Tribeca. Designed by Morris Adjmi Architects, the project layers hundreds of new market-rate and affordable homes above reimagined street-level frontage, with landscape interventions intended to soften the complex's original brutalist edges and improve the pedestrian experience along Greenwich Street. Its integration of a permanently affordable housing component alongside premium residences distinguishes it within the neighborhood's luxury landscape. The result reads as a contemporary high-rise carefully knitted into a beloved low-rise district rather than imposed upon it.

StatusHistory.note: Vornado Realty Trust & Stellar Management revealed plans for 1,090-ft, 72-story, 976-unit supertall at Independence Plaza (Parcel 3C); EAS filed with DCP. Per The Real Deal, Commercial Observer, NY YIMBY, May 2026.

Title: The St. Regis Nashville
Description: A 46-story contemporary Art Deco tower by Turnberry on downtown Nashville's Demonbreun Street, pairing a St. Regis hotel with branded residences and condominiums behind a "tower in a garden" facade of tiered plantings.
DescriptionLong: The St. Regis Nashville rises forty-six stories above Demonbreun Street as a contemporary Art Deco tower whose facade of cast cementitious and masonry panels carries a distinctive radial articulation — a motif of unfurling, semi-inverted columns that nods to the historic Frist Art Museum directly across the street. Designed by New York's Morris Adjmi Architects around a "tower in a garden" concept, it layers tiered plantings and landscaped terraces up the building to create a verdant, sculptural presence on the skyline. Inside, the program blends a St. Regis hotel with furnished branded residences and for-sale condominiums on the upper floors, served by hallmark St. Regis butler service, a full-service spa, indoor and outdoor pools, two amenity decks, a fitness center, a golf simulator and residential lounges. Dining and gathering spaces include a signature restaurant overlooking Demonbreun, a lobby bar and double-height ballrooms, with retail at street level and below-grade parking connected to the neighboring JW Marriott. Completing Turnberry's "Marriott block," the tower stands among the tallest in Nashville, fusing Southern hospitality with world-class luxury.
Architect: Morris Adjmi Architects, Smallwood Reynolds Stewart Stewart

StatusHistory.note: Turnberry to develop St. Regis Nashville & Residences at 805 Demonbreun, Morris Adjmi design; per Turnberry/PRNewswire, 2024-01-23

Title: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Caye Chapel
Description: Four Seasons' first private-island resort and residences, set on the 280-acre Caye Chapel off the coast of Belize with overwater bungalows, a reversible golf course, and oceanfront estates.
DescriptionLong: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Caye Chapel is the brand's first private-island destination, occupying a 280-acre coral island ringed by roughly six miles of white-sand beach within Belize's barrier-reef seascape, a short boat or helicopter transfer from Belize City. The resort pairs 104 oceanfront rooms — including a collection of overwater bungalows — with a portfolio of beachfront private residences and estates on prime oceanfront lots, designed for seamless indoor-outdoor living with private pools and gourmet kitchens. Amenities span a dramatic beach club, multiple restaurants and bars, a cenote-inspired five-star spa, and the Sunrise Sanctuary wellness hub with yoga and meditation studios, a reflection pool, an indoor-outdoor gym, a sports court, and a stargazing observatory. A nine-hole reversible golf course with a practice range, a marina village, and a protected nature reserve laced with hiking trails round out one of the Caribbean's most ambitious private-island luxury developments.

StatusHistory.note: Four Seasons broke ground on Caye Chapel Resort and Residences Sept 2, 2023; US$200M project by Thor Urbana / Inmobilia / GFA
Four Seasons announced Resort and Residences Caye Chapel, its first private island in the Western Hemisphere
Four Seasons announced Resort and Residences Caye Chapel, its first private island in the Western Hemisphere

Title: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Deer Valley
Description: A ski-in/ski-out Four Seasons resort and branded residences in Deer Valley's new East Village, pairing slope-side guestrooms with fully serviced mountain homes by ODA Architecture.
DescriptionLong: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Deer Valley anchors the East Village — North America's first major new alpine ski village in more than four decades — with a slope-side, ski-in/ski-out retreat developed by Extell Development Company and designed by ODA Architecture. The property combines 134 guest rooms and suites with 123 fully serviced private residences: 55 set directly above the resort and a further 68 owner-exclusive homes in an adjacent building, ranging from intimate layouts to expansive multi-bedroom estates. Owners and guests share signature Four Seasons hospitality alongside a destination spa, indoor and outdoor pools, fitness facilities, ski valet and concierge service, and direct access to Deer Valley's vastly expanded terrain. Positioned among the highest tier of North American mountain resorts, it offers a polished basecamp for skiing in winter and hiking, biking, and alpine leisure through the warmer months.

StatusHistory.note: Four Seasons and Extell announced Resort and Residences at Deer Valley East Village, Park City
Construction began spring 2025 per TownLift; completion expected 2028
Extell closed $600M construction loan from JVP Management, believed largest hotel/condo construction loan in Utah history

Title: Pendry Barbados
Description: Pendry's first Caribbean resort and residences on the Platinum Coast of Barbados, pairing oceanfront guestrooms with branded homes around a private marina and the brand's debut Pendry Yacht Club.
DescriptionLong: Pendry Barbados brings the contemporary-luxury brand to the Caribbean for the first time, rising along the calm northwest Platinum Coast near historic Speightstown in the parish of Saint Peter. Designed by architect Robert Glazier with interiors by Studio Munge, the marina-front resort pairs 80 oceanfront guestrooms and suites with 46 Pendry Residences featuring bespoke interiors and private plunge pools. At its heart is a private 110-berth marina that serves as home to the brand's inaugural Pendry Yacht Club, ringed by a signature restaurant, dockside dining and social venues, Spa Pendry, a fitness center, and the Paintbox children's and Compass Sports programs. A dedicated celebration building anchors weddings and gatherings, making the property both a destination resort and a branded-residence enclave along one of the island's most coveted stretches of coast.

StatusHistory.note: Pendry unveils first international resort & residences in St. Peter, Barbados, reimagining Port Ferdinand marina; per Hotel News Resource, 2023-04-19

Title: Six Senses Myoko
Description: A Kengo Kuma–designed mountain resort and branded residences at the foot of Mount Myoko in the Suginohara ski area, blending Six Senses wellness with private onsen and year-round alpine experiences.
DescriptionLong: Six Senses Myoko is a slopeside luxury hotel and branded-residence development planned for the Suginohara slopes of Mt. Myoko in Niigata Prefecture, one of Japan's most storied and snow-rich ski regions. Operated under IHG's Six Senses brand for developer Patience Capital Group, the resort pairs a 57-key alpine hotel with 21 private branded residences, all designed by acclaimed Japanese architect Kengo Kuma in collaboration with Kume Sekkei. The wellness-focused retreat draws on Six Senses' signature sustainability and rejuvenation ethos, weaving natural timber, onsen hot-spring bathing and an expansive spa into a contemporary mountain setting framed by Myoko's legendary powder snowfall and long, scenic ski runs. Within reach of Tokyo by bullet train, Six Senses Myoko is poised to become a landmark anchor for the wider redevelopment of this historic Japanese mountain destination.
Developer: Patience Capital Group, IHG Hotels & Resorts

StatusHistory.note: Six Senses Myoko announced — IHG/Six Senses management agreement with Patience Capital Group for a slopeside 57-key hotel + 21 branded residences at Mt. Myoko, Kengo Kuma design

Title: Raffles Tokyo
Description: Raffles' first hotel in Japan, occupying the upper floors of a redeveloped landmark tower in Tokyo's Hamamatsuchō district with sweeping views toward Tokyo Bay and the city skyline.
DescriptionLong: Raffles Tokyo marks the celebrated brand's debut in Japan, crowning the top floors of a comprehensively redeveloped landmark tower in the Hamamatsuchō district between the Shiodome cluster and Tokyo Bay. Designed by Melbourne studio Layan, the hotel pairs an intimate collection of 130 guestrooms and suites — including 27 suites — with Raffles' hallmark personalized service and signature butler program. Elevated high above the city, the property offers panoramic outlooks across Tokyo Bay, the Hamarikyu Gardens, and the skyline, anchored by a spread of dining venues, a spa and bathhouse, a fitness studio, and a pool. Its lofty perch and refined, residential-style interiors aim to bring a rarefied, gracious sense of arrival to one of Tokyo's most connected waterfront gateways.
Developer: World Trade Center Building, Inc., Tokyo Tatemono

StatusHistory.note: Raffles to debut first Tokyo hotel in 2028 atop redeveloped World Trade Center, Hamamatsucho; per Hotel News Resource, 2024-07-22

Title: 14501 W. Dixie Highway
Description: A proposed 235,065-square-foot mixed-use redevelopment on 2.66 acres in the Arch Creek Estates neighborhood near North Miami, with three buildings — a nine-story parking and commercial structure linked by sky bridges to two six-story residential and retail buildings — containing 110 condominium units, 25,250 square feet of commercial space, and a rooftop pool deck.
DescriptionLong: 14501 W. Dixie Highway is a proposed nine-story mixed-use building from RG Realty Investments on a 2.66-acre assemblage along West Dixie Highway at NE 145th Street in the Biscayne Gardens area of unincorporated Miami-Dade County. Designed by The Gado Group, it pairs 110 one- to three-bedroom apartments behind floor-to-ceiling impact glass over roughly 25,250 square feet of retail, distinguished by interlocking structures linked by two sky bridges and crowned by a rooftop pool and lounge. The project is advancing through standard zoning rather than the Live Local Act.

StatusHistory.note: RG Realty Investments and The Gado Group file a Miami-Dade pre-application for a 9-story, 110-unit mixed-use building at 14501 W. Dixie Highway (Biscayne Gardens area)
RG Realty Investments and The Gado Group file a Miami-Dade pre-application for a 9-story, 110-unit mixed-use building at 14501 W. Dixie Highway (Biscayne Gardens area)

Title: PGA Office Center
Description: An 86-acre corporate campus at I-95 and PGA Boulevard, anchored by twin six-story office buildings totaling about 515,000 square feet, designed to accommodate 2,000 employees with 150 EV charging stations, a rooftop solar PV array, and Category 5 hurricane resilience.
DescriptionLong: This 86-acre corporate campus at I-95 and PGA Boulevard in Palm Beach Gardens comprises twin six-story office buildings — Phase 1 at 266,000 square feet (opened 2022) and Phase 2 at 249,000 square feet (approved December 2022) — connected by a glass-enclosed walkway and supported by twin three-story parking garages with 150 EV charging stations and a rooftop solar photovoltaic array. The site includes a helistop, a 3,000-gallon emergency fuel station for company vehicles during storms, native plantings across 90 percent of the landscape, and is engineered to withstand Category 5 hurricanes for operational continuity. The original 2013 zoning approval cleared up to one million square feet of office development across the site, leaving roughly half a million square feet of future capacity beyond the two completed phases — enough for another 2,000 employees.

StatusHistory.note: Palm Beach Gardens City Council approves Phase 1: 266,000 sqft six-story FPL office building + garage on 86-acre I-95/PGA Blvd campus
First FPL office building began vertical construction in 2020 (opened 2023); second twin building still in planning

Title: The Monarch San Antonio
Description: A $185 million, 17-story luxury hotel in San Antonio's Hemisfair district, part of Hilton's Curio Collection, steps from the River Walk and the Alamo.
DescriptionLong: The Monarch is a $185 million, 17-story luxury hotel anchoring San Antonio's reimagined Hemisfair district, steps from the River Walk and the Alamo and overlooking Hemisfair's Civic Park. Developed by Zachry Hospitality as part of Hilton's Curio Collection, the tower blends contemporary design with a sense of place drawn from the city's history and the monarch-butterfly migration that gives it its name. The signature rooftop restaurant, Aleteo, is wrapped in butterfly-wing glass with sweeping views across downtown, joined by a lively lobby bar, refined guest rooms and suites, and event and gathering spaces. Its location at the heart of the Hemisfair revitalization places guests within walking distance of the park, the convention district and the river's cultural corridor.

Title: Chalet Alpina
Description: An ultra-luxury resort and 32 residences at the base of Aspen Mountain, anchoring the revival of the historic Lift One corridor with a new lift, ski museum and public park.
DescriptionLong: Chalet Alpina is a ski-in/ski-out luxury resort and residences at the original Lift One base on the west side of Aspen Mountain, spanning roughly two and a half blocks along South Aspen Street. Developed by Aspen-based HayMax Capital with Los Angeles resort developer Irongate Group, it combines six whole-ownership Mountain Homes and 26 fractional Resort Residences with a hotel, restaurants and retail, a new telemix lift that brings skiers closer to downtown, and the restoration of the historic Skiers' Chalet buildings — one reborn as a restaurant, the other as an Aspen ski-history museum. Architecture is by Guerin Glass Architects with interiors by Martin Brudnizki Design Studio, above a rooftop pool, a wellness spa, and underground parking.
Architect: Guerin Glass Architects, Martin Brudnizki Design Studio

StatusHistory.note: The Lift One base project at Aspen Mountain is rebranded Chalet Alpina by HayMax Capital and Irongate Group — a ski-in/ski-out resort and residences with a new telemix lift and restored historic Skiers' Chalet buildings

Title: Six Senses Telluride
Description: Six Senses' Mountain West debut, a 77-room wellness resort with 24 branded residences set above Telluride in Mountain Village, framed by the 14,000-foot peaks of the San Juans.
DescriptionLong: Six Senses Telluride is a wellness-driven luxury resort and branded-residence project in Mountain Village, above Telluride, framed by the 14,000-foot peaks of the San Juan Mountains. Developed by the Vault Home Collection under the Tiara Telluride entity and operated under IHG's Six Senses brand, it pairs a 77-key resort with 24 two-to-five-bedroom residences. Cedar-and-stone architecture with interiors by Rose Ink Workshop targets LEED Silver and features a Six Senses spa, a rooftop pool, omakase and specialty dining, and a speakeasy bar — IHG's first Six Senses resort and residences in North America's Mountain West.

StatusHistory.note: IHG unveils Six Senses Telluride in Mountain Village — a 77-key resort plus 24 branded residences, IHG's first Six Senses in North America's Mountain West

Title: The Birdsall, Auberge Resorts Collection
Description: Houston's first Auberge property — a 105-room boutique hotel with 44 private residences and a members' club, anchoring The RO, a 17-acre walkable district near River Oaks.
DescriptionLong: The Birdsall is a 34-story luxury hotel-and-residences tower in Houston's River Oaks, anchoring Transwestern Development's 'The RO' enclave and marking Auberge Resorts Collection's first property in Houston. Designed by Kohn Pedersen Fox with hotel interiors by Roman and Williams and residential interiors by Dillon Kyle Architects, it pairs a 105-key boutique hotel with 44 private residences and a members' club. Named for celebrated River Oaks architect Birdsall Briscoe, the reclaimed-brick tower offers a 70-foot pool, a full-service spa, and residences crowned by private terraces of more than 500 square feet.

StatusHistory.note: Construction underway on The Birdsall as The RO Phase 2 breaks ground in River Oaks
Construction underway on The Birdsall as The RO Phase 2 breaks ground in River Oaks

Title: Rodeo Dunes
Description: Dream Golf's new public-access destination resort on Colorado's high plains — more than 2,000 acres of natural sand with a Coore & Crenshaw course and up to six courses planned.
DescriptionLong: Rodeo Dunes is a destination golf resort by Dream Golf — the Keiser family behind Bandon Dunes and Sand Valley — set on natural sand dunes near Roggen on Colorado's eastern plains, about an hour northeast of Denver. Its debut walking-only 18-hole course is designed by Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw, with a second 18 by Jim Craig and room for up to six full courses plus a short course and putting green. Caddie-supported and remote, the links-style property is positioned as Colorado's premier big-sky golf retreat.

StatusHistory.note: Dream Golf (Keiser family) announces Rodeo Dunes on Colorado's eastern plains near Roggen — debut walking-only course by Coore & Crenshaw, a second 18 by Jim Craig
Construction begins on the first golf course (Coore & Crenshaw) at Rodeo Dunes
Construction begins on the first golf course (Coore & Crenshaw) at Rodeo Dunes

Title: Rosewood Residences Turtle Creek
Description: Rosewood's standalone branded condominium in Dallas — a 17-story Beaux-Arts-inspired tower of roughly 33 large residences along Turtle Creek and the Katy Trail.
DescriptionLong: Rosewood Residences Turtle Creek is a 17-story, 33-residence luxury condominium tower at 3555 Dickason Avenue in Dallas's Turtle Creek corridor, a block from the storied Rosewood Mansion. Developed by One Turtle Creek LP under the Rosewood brand — Rosewood's first standalone branded residences in Texas — it features Beaux-Arts architecture by Lucien Lagrange and interiors by AD100 designer Dan Fink. Residences range from roughly 2,000 to 7,000 square feet above amenities including a rooftop infinity pool, landscaped gardens, a fitness center, library, private dining, concierge and valet service, and a dedicated pet spa.

StatusHistory.note: Rosewood plans a 17-story Rosewood Residences Turtle Creek in Dallas, developed by One Turtle Creek LP under the Rosewood brand — Beaux-Arts design by Lucien Lagrange
Construction commences on Rosewood Residences Turtle Creek
Construction commences on Rosewood Residences Turtle Creek

Title: Canyon Ranch Austin
Description: A roughly $121 million, 223,000-square-foot Canyon Ranch destination wellness resort in the Texas Hill Country near Lake Travis, the brand's first new build in years.
DescriptionLong: Canyon Ranch Austin is a ground-up wellness resort set on more than 600 acres of Texas Hill Country ranchland in Spicewood, overlooking Lake Travis west of Austin. Developed by Canyon Ranch with residences designed by Texas firm Lake Flato, it is anchored by the largest spa in Texas — 40,000 square feet with 37 treatment rooms — alongside a 27,000-square-foot fitness barn, a longevity and medical center, multiple pools, miles of trails, an equestrian program, and lakefront watersports. Dining centers on the resort's signature restaurant Estella, with programming spanning brain health, sleep, metabolic medicine, and a first-of-its-kind women's wellness practice.

StatusHistory.note: Canyon Ranch unveils Canyon Ranch Austin, a 600+-acre Lake Travis wellness resort in Spicewood with capital partner VICI Properties
Canyon Ranch unveils Canyon Ranch Austin, a 600+-acre Lake Travis wellness resort in Spicewood with capital partner VICI Properties
Canyon Ranch unveils Canyon Ranch Austin, a 600+-acre Lake Travis wellness resort in Spicewood with capital partner VICI Properties
Canyon Ranch unveils Canyon Ranch Austin, a 600+-acre Lake Travis wellness resort in Spicewood with capital partner VICI Properties

Title: Virgin Hotels Denver
Description: Virgin Hotels' first Colorado property, a 241-room hotel anchoring the 41-acre Fox Park district on the site of the former Denver Post printing plant.
DescriptionLong: Virgin Hotels Denver is the brand's first Colorado property and a signature anchor of Fox Park, the 41-acre redevelopment of the former Denver Post printing plant in the Globeville district along Fox Street. Designed by Tryba Architects with Saunders Construction and developed by Vita alongside Trammell Crow and the World Trade Center Denver campus, the new-build offers Chambers-style guestrooms, an 8,000-square-foot spa, a rooftop lounge with skyline views, and Virgin's signature Commons Club, Shag Room and Funny Library Coffee Shop.

StatusHistory.note: Virgin Hotels Denver announced at the Fox Park groundbreaking — Virgin's first Colorado hotel anchoring the 41-acre Globeville redevelopment of the former Denver Post printing plant

Title: Dallas Proper Hotel and Residences
Description: A 34-story Proper-branded luxury tower at the gateway to Uptown Dallas, pairing a 200-room hotel with roughly 80 branded residences designed by SOM with interiors by Kelly Wearstler.
DescriptionLong: Dallas Proper Hotel & Residences is a 34-story luxury tower anchoring Lincoln Property Company's four-acre mixed-use development at 2500 Cedar Springs Road in Uptown Dallas, near the Crescent and the Katy Trail. Operated under Proper Hospitality's Proper Hotels brand, it pairs roughly 200 guestrooms with about 80 branded residences, with architecture by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill and interiors by Kelly Wearstler. Amenities include a 15,000-square-foot wellness and padel club, multiple restaurants and bars, a rooftop pool deck, and a members-only Proper Club — positioned as Proper's second Texas hotel.

StatusHistory.note: Proper Hospitality + Lincoln Property unveil 34-story Dallas Proper: 200-key hotel + 80 SOM-designed residences at 2500 Cedar Springs Rd, Uptown

Title: The East Bend
Description: A 47-acre redevelopment of a former East Bank scrapyard site into a mixed-use Nashville neighborhood on the Cumberland River, planned with residential, retail, public green space, and cultural amenities — anchored by what would be Nashville's first dedicated children's museum.
DescriptionLong: The East Bend is a multi-decade, mixed-use redevelopment of a 47-acre former PSC Metals scrapyard on Nashville's East Bank, fronting the Cumberland River just south of Nissan Stadium. Led by a David Byerley–directed investor group, the master plan reimagines the long-industrial riverfront as a walkable, family-oriented neighborhood of residences, neighborhood retail, parks and greenways, and cultural and entertainment space — with zoning that bars stadiums and arenas and caps hotels, signaling a residential-and-culture vision rather than an entertainment district. A dedicated children's museum is under discussion as a potential cultural anchor.
ProjectType: Mixed-Use, Residences, Retail, Park, Cultural, Museum, Eateries, Entertainment

StatusHistory.note: 47-acre former PSC Metals riverfront scrapyard on Nashville's East Bank sold for $245M to a David Byerley–led group (TEB LLC) for redevelopment as The East Bend, a multi-decade mixed-use neighborhood

Title: Ambergris Cay
Description: A 1,100-acre private island all-inclusive resort 600 miles south of Miami in the Turks and Caicos, with 29 bungalows and 11 villas (3-11 bedrooms), each with a private heated plunge pool, an on-island international airport with a 5,700-foot runway, and full-service amenities including a Club House, spa, fitness pavilion, tennis courts, and the Calico restaurant and bar.
DescriptionLong: Ambergris Cay is a 1,100-acre private island all-inclusive resort on the southeast edge of the Turks and Caicos string, 600 miles south of Miami and tracing the world-famous Caicos Banks. Originally established as a private homeowner community and opened as a resort in 2019, the property now offers a luxury boutique hotel feel across vast and largely untouched terrain. Accommodation spans 29 bungalows — including the recently opened collection of 12 Waterside Bungalows with direct calm-water access, private heated plunge pools, and kayaks and paddleboards at the door — and 11 villas ranging from three to eleven bedrooms, all bookable on an all-inclusive basis with private heated plunge pools throughout. Amenities include a Club House, full-service spa, fitness pavilion, tennis, the Hangover Tiki Bar, and Calico restaurant for all-day dining with international chefs and made-to-order menus. Guests reach the island through Ambergris Cay International Airport, with one of the longest runways in the Caribbean at 5,700 feet — accommodating aircraft up to 92 feet in length for direct private jet arrival, with complimentary onward transfer flights from Providenciales or South Caicos for commercial guests. The resort was named a 2026 Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice Best of the Best winner, ranking among the Caribbean's top all-inclusive properties.

Title: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Dominican Republic at Tropicalia
Description: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Dominican Republic at Tropicalia is a low-density luxury resort and branded-residence community set along the sands of Playa Esmeralda in Miches, on the Dominican Republic's northeastern coast facing Samana Bay. Spread across roughly 60 acres of beachfront framed by tropical forest and lagoons, the property pairs a tropical-modernist resort with private oceanfront residences and beach villas. Guests enjoy a spa and wellness program, a collection of restaurants and bars, pools, and direct beach access, all designed around sustainability and a light environmental footprint. Natural materials and open-air living define a setting that aims to set a new standard for green luxury in the Caribbean.
DescriptionLong: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Dominican Republic at Tropicalia is the brand's first property in the Dominican Republic, set on a 60-acre beachfront site along Playa Esmeralda on the southern shores of Samaná Bay in the country's lush northeast. Developed by Cisneros Real Estate as part of the family's long-envisioned Tropicalia community, the resort features tropical-modernist architecture by acclaimed Brazilian architect Isay Weinfeld, with 95 guest rooms and suites and 25 private residences of three and four bedrooms, all stepping toward the sand. Conceived around sustainability and a light environmental footprint, the low-slung, nature-led design pairs Four Seasons service with a protected stretch of Caribbean coastline, positioning the resort as a flagship for sophisticated, regenerative luxury travel in the region.

StatusHistory.note: Four Seasons & Cisneros Real Estate announce 60-acre oceanfront resort + branded residences at Playa Esmeralda, Miches
Construction of the resort begins; Bouygues Construction named as general contractor

Title: Hoshinoya Niseko
Description: Hoshinoya Niseko is a luxury residence hotel perched at the top of the Hirafu slope in Kutchan, in Japan's powder-snow capital of Niseko on the island of Hokkaido. The first residence hotel under its esteemed Japanese brand, it offers ski-in/ski-out access to one of the area's largest ski resorts along with easy reach of the town's dining and nightlife. Guest rooms are designed as residential retreats, each with a separate kitchen and a living-dining room, while a top-floor terrace features an onsen pool framed by sweeping views toward Mount Yotei. The property blends traditional Japanese hospitality and minimalist design with the comforts of an alpine home, immersed in the surrounding nature year-round.
DescriptionLong: HOSHINOYA Niseko is the ninth property in Hoshino Resorts' flagship HOSHINOYA brand and its first to incorporate branded residences, planned for the upper slopes of the Hirafu ski area in Hokkaido's Niseko region. Designed by Azuma Architect & Associates — the practice behind every HOSHINOYA to date — the ski-in/ski-out retreat rises across seven levels in reinforced concrete and steel, with around 62 units that owners can enroll in a rental program operated as hotel accommodation by Hoshino Resorts. The building is crowned by a signature rooftop onsen framing sweeping views of the iconic, near-symmetrical cone of Mount Yotei, channeling the brand's hallmark blend of refined Japanese hospitality, contemplative design and a powerful connection to landscape. Positioned directly on Hirafu's coveted upper runs, it offers direct access to Niseko's celebrated powder in winter and a tranquil mountain base for green-season exploration, all wrapped in the quiet luxury and ryokan-rooted service that define the HOSHINOYA name.

StatusHistory.note: Hoshino Resorts announces HOSHINOYA Niseko — its 9th Hoshinoya and first residence hotel, atop Hirafu, Kutchan
Hoshino Resorts announces HOSHINOYA Niseko — its 9th Hoshinoya and first residence hotel, atop Hirafu, Kutchan
Construction commenced Oct 2024 as the project sold to a Tokyu Land-led consortium; completion revised to Feb 2029
Construction commenced Oct 2024 as the project sold to a Tokyu Land-led consortium; completion revised to Feb 2029

Title: Six Senses Niseko
Description: Six Senses Niseko is a wellness-driven alpine resort in the Hirafu area of Japan's renowned Niseko ski region on the northern island of Hokkaido. Set against the backdrop of Mount Yotei and some of the world's most reliable powder snow, the property pairs Six Senses' signature sustainability and wellness ethos with ski access. Guests can expect an extensive spa and wellness program, dining venues, and a traditional onsen bathhouse, with design that draws on natural materials and the surrounding landscape. The resort is positioned as a year-round mountain retreat combining winter sports in season with hiking, nature, and onsen-style relaxation.
DescriptionLong: Six Senses Niseko brings the wellness-driven luxury brand to Hokkaido's famed Niseko ski region, set in the Hirafu area at the foot of the legendary powder slopes beneath Mount Yotei. Operated under the Six Senses banner, part of IHG Hotels & Resorts, the intimate alpine retreat is planned with around 76 rooms and suites — including a penthouse with a generous terrace — alongside the brand's signature wellness offering anchored by a spa and a traditional Japanese onsen bathhouse. Two restaurants and a bar round out a hospitality experience that fuses Six Senses' sustainability ethos and barefoot-luxury sensibility with the deep snow, soaring birch forests and year-round mountain adventure that have made Niseko one of the world's most sought-after alpine destinations. Designed as a sanctuary for both winter skiers and green-season travelers, the resort offers ski access in the colder months and hiking, cycling and onsen rituals when the snow recedes.

StatusHistory.note: Six Senses announced for Niseko (Hirafu area), Hokkaido under IHG
Six Senses announced for Niseko (Hirafu area), Hokkaido under IHG

Title: Four Seasons Resort and Private Residences Okinawa
Description: Four Seasons Resort and Private Residences Okinawa is an expansive beachfront resort and branded-residence community along the western coast of Okinawa in Onna Village, set within one of the world's celebrated longevity Blue Zones. Stretching across more than 100 acres of coastline, the low-density plan weaves hotel accommodations, private villas, and oceanfront residences into a walkable seaside village. Contemporary Japanese architecture and natural materials harmonize with the subtropical setting, complemented by lush gardens and landscaped grounds. Amenities include restaurants and bars, a spa, fitness and pool facilities, a beach club, and private onsen baths, all anchored by a longevity- and wellness-focused lifestyle.
DescriptionLong: The Four Seasons Resort and Private Residences Okinawa is an expansive oceanfront luxury destination taking shape along the celebrated coastline of Onna Village on Japan's subtropical island of Okinawa. Developed by Malaysia's Berjaya Land in partnership with Four Seasons and designed by Kengo Kuma & Associates with landscape architecture by EDSA, the resort spreads across a site of more than 130,000 square meters facing the East China Sea. The program pairs roughly 127 guest rooms with about 124 branded condominium residences and 28 private villas, many featuring their own onsen baths and plunge pools, set amid lush gardens that step down toward white-sand beaches and clear turquoise water. Drawing on Kuma's signature use of natural materials and layered, landscape-attuned architecture, the resort is conceived as a wellness-oriented retreat blending Okinawan craft, indoor-outdoor living and panoramic ocean views, delivering Four Seasons service in one of Asia's most coveted island settings.

StatusHistory.note: Berjaya's US$1.12B Four Seasons Resort and Private Residences Okinawa announced in Onna Village
Berjaya's US$1.12B Four Seasons Resort and Private Residences Okinawa announced in Onna Village
Berjaya Land breaks ground on the resort and private residences; contractors Fujita Corporation and Seikou Okinawa Construction

Title: The Oberoi, Mayfair
Description: The Oberoi, Mayfair is a luxury boutique hotel in London's Mayfair district, set within a restored historic listed building of roughly 33,000 square feet on Brook Street in the heart of the West End. Forming the hospitality centerpiece of a larger mixed-use quarter that weaves together offices, residences, shops, restaurants, and revitalized public spaces, the hotel brings a celebrated standard of refined, understated service to one of the city's most prestigious addresses. Guests can expect intimately scaled accommodations, world-class personalized service, and meticulous attention to detail throughout. Its corner setting makes it a polished new gateway into Mayfair's storied streetscape.
DescriptionLong: The Oberoi, Mayfair marks the celebrated Indian luxury group Oberoi Hotels & Resorts' first hotel in the United Kingdom, set within Grosvenor's transformative South Molton development in the heart of Mayfair. Designed by Hopkins Architects, the wider scheme reweaves a cluster of blocks between Davies Street, Brook Street and South Molton Lane into a pedestrian-friendly mixed-use quarter of shops, restaurants, workspace and homes, anchored by a new luxury hotel beside the Bond Street Elizabeth line entrance. For Oberoi, the London debut brings the brand's renowned, deeply personal service and richly detailed interiors to one of the world's most prestigious retail and residential districts, with elegant guest rooms and suites, signature dining and wellness spaces woven into a sustainable, heritage-sensitive reinvention of the streetscape. It positions Oberoi at the center of Mayfair's luxury landscape, steps from Bond Street's flagship boutiques and the green expanses of Hyde Park and Grosvenor Square.

StatusHistory.note: Oberoi announces its first UK hotel at 40-46 Brook Street, Mayfair, within Grosvenor's South Molton scheme
Oberoi announces its first UK hotel at 40-46 Brook Street, Mayfair, within Grosvenor's South Molton scheme
On-site construction work began November 2023 within Grosvenor's South Molton scheme

Title: Mandarin Oriental, Setouchi
Description: Mandarin Oriental, Setouchi is a waterfront urban resort on the harbor front of Takamatsu, the capital of Kagawa Prefecture and a gateway to Japan's scenic Seto Inland Sea. Overlooking calm, island-dotted waters, the hotel pairs refined Asian-inspired hospitality with a coastal sense of place and a mild year-round climate. Guests can expect elegant rooms and suites, a selection of restaurants and bars, and a comprehensive spa and wellness facility. Conceived as part of a multi-island collection, it serves as both a serene seaside retreat and a launch point for exploring the region's art islands and coastal culture.
DescriptionLong: Mandarin Oriental, Setouchi is the centerpiece of the group's ambitious expansion into Japan's Seto Inland Sea region — a 92-room urban resort set on the waterfront of Takamatsu, the harbor capital of Kagawa Prefecture and the gateway to the celebrated art islands of the Setouchi region. Conceived as part of a planned trio of boutique Mandarin Oriental properties scattered around the Inland Sea, the Takamatsu resort pairs the brand's signature service with a strong sense of place rooted in the region's maritime culture, contemporary-art scene and slow-travel ethos. Guests are positioned to explore Naoshima, Teshima and the other islands made famous by the Setouchi Triennale, returning to refined rooms and suites, destination dining and a spa, all oriented toward the calm, island-dotted horizon of the Inland Sea. The result is envisioned as a sophisticated coastal base that blends design-led luxury with the understated beauty and craft traditions of western Japan.

StatusHistory.note: Mandarin Oriental announces Setouchi resort trio; Takamatsu is a 92-key waterfront urban resort
Mandarin Oriental announces Setouchi resort trio; Takamatsu is a 92-key waterfront urban resort
Mandarin Oriental announces Setouchi resort trio; Takamatsu is a 92-key waterfront urban resort

Title: London Museum
Description: The London Museum is a free public museum in London's West Smithfield neighborhood, set within the painstakingly restored Victorian General Market halls. Across interconnected gallery experiences, it tells the sweeping story of the city through a vast collection of objects, from a contemporary data-driven entry hall to subterranean galleries reaching down toward Roman street level. A dramatic viewing window lets visitors watch trains pass beneath the galleries, blending working infrastructure with cultural display. The destination pairs immersive permanent collections with rotating programming and event spaces, anchoring a landmark of adaptive reuse in the heart of historic Smithfield.
DescriptionLong: The London Museum's new home is an ambitious adaptive reuse of the historic Smithfield market buildings at West Smithfield, bringing one of the world's great city museums back to the heart of the City of London. Led by Stanton Williams and Asif Khan with conservation architect Julian Harrap, the project rescues the long-vacant General Market and Poultry Market — soaring Victorian iron-and-glass halls — and converts them into luminous galleries, public gathering spaces and event venues. The design choreographs a journey from light-filled market halls down into dramatic sunken galleries set within the building's brick vaults, organizing the visitor experience around themes of the city's past, present and future. Restored domes, recovered original ironwork and a new public entrance reanimate a once-derelict corner of London, while the museum is conceived as a free, around-the-clock civic destination — open, porous and woven into the surrounding streets, markets and the new Elizabeth line connections nearby.
Architect: Stanton Williams, Asif Khan, Julian Harrap Architects

StatusHistory.note: Museum of London rebrands as the London Museum and confirms the restored Victorian General Market at West Smithfield as its new home

Title: Earls Court Development
Description: Earls Court Development is a landmark mixed-use regeneration in West London, transforming roughly 44 acres of former exhibition-center land into one of the capital's largest new urban quarters. The plan delivers around 4,000 new homes alongside some 2.5 million square feet of workspace, cultural venues, shops, and restaurants, all knitted together by roughly 20 acres of green space including a major new park. Built around a zero-carbon energy strategy with substantial biodiversity gains and a thousand new trees, the scheme pairs sustainability with a vibrant, walkable public realm and improved east-west connections. A tall landmark tower anchors a varied skyline across the phased neighborhood.
DescriptionLong: The Earls Court Development is one of London's most significant urban regeneration schemes — a multi-billion-pound transformation of the 40-acre former Earls Court exhibition centre site straddling Kensington and Chelsea and Hammersmith and Fulham. Led by the Earls Court Development Company with a masterplan team including Hawkins\Brown and Studio Egret West, the project reknits a long-dormant railway-severed site back into the city, organized around a generous new central park and a network of public squares, streets and east-west connections. The plans bring roughly 4,000 new homes, around 230,000 square metres of workspace, and three new cultural venues, alongside shops, restaurants and community space, with sustainability and biodiversity woven through the green spine at its heart. Conceived as a series of distinct character areas rather than a single megastructure, the development aims to create a vibrant, mixed-use west London district that reanimates the legacy of the storied Earls Court name.

StatusHistory.note: Updated 7.5M sq ft Earls Court masterplan submitted for planning; ~4,000 homes, ~2.5M sq ft workspace across ~44 acres
Updated 7.5M sq ft Earls Court masterplan submitted for planning; ~4,000 homes, ~2.5M sq ft workspace across ~44 acres
Updated 7.5M sq ft Earls Court masterplan submitted for planning; ~4,000 homes, ~2.5M sq ft workspace across ~44 acres

Title: Canada Water Masterplan
Description: Canada Water Masterplan is a large-scale mixed-use regeneration on roughly 53 acres in the Canada Water district of southeast London, reshaping a former dockside area into a new town center.
